title: Apply for the Stanton Fellowship 2026: Support for Los Angeles Change-Makers and Community Leaders
summary: The Stanton Fellowship, offered by the Durfee Foundation, is a prestigious opportunity for experienced leaders in Los Angeles County to step away from day-to-day responsibilities and explore bold, high-impact ideas. Designed to support inquiry, innovation, and cross-sector collaboration, the fellowship provides both funding and time to pursue solutions to complex challenges facing the region. Application Timeline Application Opens: April 1 Application Deadline: June 24 Site Visits: August 4–27 Award Notifications: Early September In-person Orientation: January 2027 About the Stanton Fellowship The Stanton Fellowship supports up to six leaders each year with $100,000 over two years to investigate a compelling question that has the potential to create meaningful impact in Los Angeles County. The program is intended for individuals who are deeply embedded in their fields and communities, and who are well-positioned to explore innovative approaches to pressing issues. Fellows are encouraged to pursue adaptive, exploratory work rather than rigid, technical solutions. Funding is flexible and may be used for: Travel and convenings Research and consulting support Conferences and collaborative activities The fellowship creates space for leaders to think critically, test ideas, and develop new approaches that may not be feasible within their regular professional roles. Fellowship Experience At the core of the program is a collaborative cohort model. Fellows engage with a diverse, cross-disciplinary group of peers, sharing insights and learning from one another’s work and perspectives. Participation includes: A minimum of three months of dedicated project time over two years (in increments of at least two weeks) Three overnight retreats Six day-long cohort meetings Ongoing knowledge-sharing within professional networks Fellows are expected to contribute actively to the cohort and disseminate insights from their inquiry to broader audiences. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a resident of Los Angeles County Work at an organization located and operating within Los Angeles County Have at least 10 years of demonstrated leadership and impact in the nonprofit sector, government, arts, social movements, or social entrepreneurship Be in a position to influence peers, policymakers, or the public Present a clearly defined proposal focused on a “big question” to explore over two years Demonstrate financial need for project support (fellowship is supplemental income) Commit to all fellowship activities and time requirements Not currently receive institutional or academic support for similar research Not have received a Durfee Sabbatical within the past five years The Durfee Foundation strongly encourages applications from organizations serving historically marginalized communities, including BIPOC, LGBTQ, and low-income populations. Review Criteria Applications are evaluated based on three core areas: 1. The Candidate Reviewers assess whether the applicant: Is a recognized thought leader in their field Has a strong reputation and influence related to the proposed topic Demonstrates openness to experimentation and non-linear exploration Has access to platforms or networks to share insights Can commit dedicated time away from daily responsibilities Will actively contribute to and benefit from the fellowship cohort Lacks access to traditional research or inquiry opportunities 2. The Inquiry The proposed project should: Address a complex and significant issue affecting Los Angeles County Be clearly articulated and thoughtfully planned Demonstrate originality and potential to break new ground Be feasible within the two-year timeframe Include a clear approach to using time and resources effectively 3. Timing Strong applications will show: The applicant is at the right stage in their career for this opportunity There is urgency or relevance to the proposed inquiry The applicant’s professional and personal circumstances are stable enough to complete the fellowship Program Goals The Stanton Fellowship aims to: Enable Los Angeles leaders to pursue in-depth exploration of critical issues Build a diverse, cross-sector community of thinkers and practitioners Generate insights, relationships, and ideas that influence systems and communities across Los Angeles County How to Apply Interested candidates should prepare a compelling proposal outlining their inquiry and submit their application before June 24 . APPLY HERE Discover similar opportunities here. title: Apply Now: Oxford University Postdoctoral Fellowship in African Studies 2026
summary: The University of Oxford is inviting applications for a Postdoctoral Research Fellow in African Studies at the Oxford School of Global and Area Studies (OSGA). This is a prestigious opportunity for early-career scholars to advance their research within a globally recognized academic environment. Overview of the Fellowship Position: Postdoctoral Research Fellow in African Studies Institution: African Studies Centre (ASC), OSGA Location: Oxford , United Kingdom Salary: £39,424 – £47,779 per annum (Grade 7) Contract Type: Full-time (37.5 hours/week), 2-year fixed-term Start Date: 1 October 2026 (or as soon as possible thereafter) Application Deadline: 20 April 2026 (12:00 noon UK time) About the Fellowship The African Studies Centre at Oxford is seeking an outstanding early-career researcher to develop an innovative and independent research programme under the broad theme of “Africa and the World.” The fellowship focuses on understanding Africa’s place within contemporary global, comparative, and transnational contexts. Working under the supervision of Rita Abrahamsen , the successful candidate will contribute to advancing cutting-edge scholarship and strengthening Oxford’s position as a leading hub for African Studies. Key Responsibilities The selected Fellow will: Conduct an independent and ambitious research programme in African Studies Publish high-quality research in peer-reviewed journals and academic presses Present research at conferences, seminars, and academic forums Contribute to shaping the “Africa and the World” research agenda at Oxford Organize seminars, conferences, and collaborative research initiatives Teach an option course aligned with their expertise Supervise master’s dissertations in the MSc in African Studies Actively participate in the academic community at OSGA and ASC Benefits of the Position Working at the University of Oxford offers a comprehensive benefits package, including: 38 days of annual leave (inclusive of public holidays), with options to extend Generous family leave schemes , including maternity, adoption, and paternity leave A competitive pension scheme Sustainable commuting options , including cycle loans and discounted travel Access to a vibrant community of social, cultural, and sports clubs Eligibility Criteria Applicants should: Hold (or be close to completing) a relevant doctoral degree Have completed their doctorate no earlier than three years before 1 January 2026 Demonstrate strong academic writing and publication potential Show experience presenting research at conferences and seminars Have a commitment to teaching and postgraduate supervision Desirable Experience Teaching and supervising theses Leading independent research projects Collaborating with other researchers Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion The Oxford School of Global and Area Studies is committed to fostering an inclusive academic environment. Applications are particularly encouraged from women and individuals from Black and minority ethnic backgrounds, who are underrepresented in academic roles at Oxford. The School also holds a Bronze Athena Swan Award , recognizing its commitment to advancing gender equality. Application Process Candidates must apply through the official Oxford University recruitment portal. The application requires: A Curriculum Vitae (CV) A supporting statement addressing the selection criteria A research proposal outlining the proposed project Applicants are advised not to submit published work at this stage. Read more and Apply Here Important Dates Application Deadline: 20 April 2026 (12:00 noon UK time) Interview Date: 14 May 2026 (online) This fellowship presents a unique opportunity to join a globally respected institution and contribute to influential research on Africa’s role in the world. Scholars with strong, original research ideas and a commitment to academic excellence are highly encouraged to apply. Discover more similar opportunities Here . title: NSF TechAccess: AI-Ready America Coordination Hubs — Up to $1M/Year to Expand AI Skills and Access
summary: The U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF), in partnership with the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA NIFA), the United States Department of Labor , and the Small Business Administration , is inviting applications for the TechAccess: AI-Ready America Coordination Hubs initiative. This major funding opportunity supports the creation of state- and territory-based hubs designed to expand AI access, literacy, and workforce readiness across the United States. About the Initiative The TechAccess program aims to strengthen the U.S. workforce and economy by ensuring that individuals, businesses, and communities are equipped to understand and adopt artificial intelligence technologies. Selected Coordination Hubs will serve as regional centers for AI education, training, and adoption support , helping bridge the gap between emerging technologies and real-world application. What the Hubs Will Do Funded hubs will: Provide AI literacy and foundational education programs Deliver applied AI skills training for workers and businesses Offer technical assistance to support AI adoption Facilitate hands-on learning opportunities, including internships and project-based experiences Strengthen regional innovation ecosystems and workforce development Funding Details Award amount: Up to $1,000,000 per year per hub Duration: 3 years (with potential for a 4th year) Funding supports large-scale, collaborative, and impact-driven initiatives Who Should Apply This opportunity is targeted at organizations capable of leading regional initiatives, including: Universities and research institutions Nonprofits and workforce development organizations Public-private partnerships State or territory-based entities Applicants should demonstrate the capacity to coordinate multi-stakeholder efforts and deliver measurable impact in AI readiness. Why This Opportunity Matters As AI continues to reshape industries, access to skills and tools becomes critical. This initiative ensures that workers, small businesses, and underserved communities are not left behind in the digital transformation. It is a strategic investment in building an inclusive, AI-ready workforce and economy. Application Deadline 📅 June 23, 2026 (5:00 PM EDT) Informational Webinar 📅 Date: April 14, 2026 ⏰ Time: 1:00 PM EDT 🔗 Register here. For more Grant Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: AI Fellows Program 2026 at Gates Foundation India: Apply to Work on High-Impact AI Solutions
summary: The Bill Melinda Gates Foundation India office is now accepting applications for its first-ever AI Fellows cohort —a unique opportunity to apply artificial intelligence to real-world challenges in health, agriculture, and development. This 12-month fellowship places selected candidates on high-impact projects, where they will work closely with program teams and receive structured mentorship while building innovative AI-driven solutions. About the Program Each fellow will be embedded within Strategic Initiatives or Programmatic Strategy Teams, contributing to priority projects that address critical development challenges. Fellows will also be supported by experienced mentors and gain exposure to interdisciplinary collaboration across sectors. Fellowship Focus Areas Five fellows will be selected, each working on a specialized AI application area: AI Fellow #1: Maternal Fetal Health Risk Stratification Work with longitudinal pregnancy datasets to improve maternal and fetal health outcomes. Develop AI models for early detection of conditions such as gestational diabetes and preeclampsia. Build prototype risk-scoring tools for frontline healthcare decision-making. AI Fellow #2: AI Agricultural Advisory Integrate weather, satellite, and agronomic data into advisory tools for farmers. Develop conversational AI interfaces (voice and text) for smallholder engagement. Support climate risk prediction and crop advisory systems. AI Fellow #3: AI for Immunization Coverage Apply AI/ML to identify underserved populations (zero-dose and dropout children). Develop targeting algorithms and dashboards to improve vaccination outreach. Enhance last-mile delivery and monitoring systems. AI Fellow #4: AI for Drug Discovery Explore AI applications in target identification, molecule design, and screening. Work with multimodal datasets including genomics and clinical data. Collaborate with research institutions to strengthen AI-driven discovery pipelines. AI Fellow #5: Rapid Prototyping of Applied AI Solutions Develop AI prototypes across sectors like health, education, and agriculture. Use tools such as chatbots, knowledge graphs, and AI frameworks. Promote responsible AI practices and build internal toolkits and learning resources. Benefits Hands-on experience working on real-world AI projects Mentorship from experts within the foundation Exposure to global development challenges and interdisciplinary teams Opportunity to build scalable, impactful AI solutions Access to a strong professional network Why This Opportunity Matters This fellowship goes beyond theory—offering a chance to directly contribute to solutions that improve health systems, food security, and economic opportunities . It is ideal for individuals who want to use AI for meaningful, large-scale impact. How to Apply 🔗 Apply here For more Fellowship opportunities, visit OFY here. title: Call for Proposals: ACE Microgrants 2026 for African Climate and Environmental Initiatives
summary: The Pulitzer Center is now inviting applications for the Africa Climate and Environment (ACE) Microgrants 2026 , an exciting opportunity designed to support African local movements working at the intersection of climate, environment, and governance. About the ACE Microgrants The ACE Microgrants are part of the Pulitzer Center’s Africa outreach program, aimed at funding innovative engagement projects that creatively incorporate stories supported by the Center. These projects are expected to drive meaningful conversations and action around environmental transparency, accountability, and governance . By leveraging storytelling and community engagement, the initiative seeks to deepen public understanding of critical environmental and climate challenges across Africa. What Kind of Projects Are Supported? Applicants are encouraged to submit creative and impactful outreach projects that engage: Young professionals Local communities Stakeholders across sectors Projects should focus on key issues such as: Government management of natural resources Harmful industrial and corporate practices Conflicts of interest and corruption Impacts on biodiversity and ecosystems Indigenous communities and human rights Climate change and environmental sustainability Successful proposals will demonstrate the ability to: Amplify community voices and lived experiences Encourage dialogue among media, government, civil society, and businesses Inspire action through storytelling and public engagement Why You Should Apply This is a unique opportunity to: Secure funding for your climate or environmental initiative Engage communities in meaningful dialogue and advocacy Collaborate across sectors to influence policy and governance Contribute to transparency and accountability in Africa’s environmental landscape If you re part of a local movement passionate about climate action and governance, the ACE Microgrants could be your chance to turn ideas into impactful change. Eligible Applicants The program is open to a wide range of African local movements , including: Civil society organizations Media organizations Social enterprises Think tanks Community-based associations Types of Activities The ACE Microgrants support diverse outreach formats, such as: Conferences and workshops Exhibitions and storytelling events Awareness campaigns Community dialogues and forums Projects should be inspired by Pulitzer Center-supported reporting on environmental governance and transparency . Language Requirements Applications can be submitted in: English French Application Deadline April 30, 2026 APPLY HERE Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official guidelines for detailed eligibility criteria and the selection process before applying. title: Tax Fairness Fellowship 2026: Paid Opportunity for Professionals Shaping Fairer Tax Systems
summary: The Tax Fairness Fellowship is now open for applications, offering a paid, career-transition opportunity for professionals ready to shape fairer and more equitable tax systems. title: Einstein Fellowship 2026 in Germany (Fully Funded): Prestigious Opportunity for Young Global Thinkers to Pursue Interdisciplinary Research at the Einstein Forum
summary: The Einstein Fellowship 2026 is one of the most prestigious fully funded international fellowships designed for exceptional young intellectuals who are eager to explore bold, interdisciplinary ideas. Offered by the Einstein Forum in collaboration with the Wittenstein Foundation , this fellowship provides a unique platform for innovative thinkers from across the world to pursue groundbreaking research in a new field outside their previous academic specialization. About the Einstein Fellowship The Einstein Fellowship is a highly competitive international program aimed at supporting outstanding young thinkers who are not only excellent in their fields but are also willing to venture beyond disciplinary boundaries. Inspired by the intellectual legacy of Albert Einstein , the fellowship encourages applicants to propose projects that differ significantly from their previous research work. The goal is to foster creativity, intellectual risk-taking, and interdisciplinary innovation. Unlike traditional academic programs, this fellowship is not designed to complete ongoing research such as a PhD or dissertation. Instead, it supports new, original, and intellectually ambitious ideas that may evolve into long-term research projects. Fellowship Benefits (Fully Funded Package) Successful applicants of the Einstein Fellowship 2026 will receive a comprehensive funding package, including: Financial Support €10,000 stipend for living expenses Full travel expense reimbursement (to and from Germany) Accommodation Free stay for 5–6 months in the garden cottage of Einstein’s summerhouse in Caputh, Brandenburg Academic Professional Benefits Opportunity to work in a stimulating intellectual environment near leading universities in Berlin and Potsdam Exposure to international scholars and interdisciplinary research culture Opportunity to present your project in a public lecture at the Einstein Forum and Wittenstein Foundation Who Can Apply (Eligible Categories of People) The Einstein Fellowship is open to: 1. Age Requirement Applicants must be under 35 years old 2. Academic Background Must hold a university degree in: Humanities Social Sciences Natural Sciences 3. Intellectual Profile Outstanding young thinkers with: Strong academic or intellectual achievements Demonstrated creativity and originality Interest in interdisciplinary research 4. Project Requirement Proposed project must: Be completely different from previous work Not be part of a dissertation or ongoing academic study Show originality, feasibility, and intellectual depth 5. PhD Status Having a PhD is not required Applicants may apply whether or not they are pursuing a PhD Eligible Countries and Regions One of the most attractive features of the Einstein Fellowship is its global inclusivity: Open to applicants from ALL countries worldwide Applicants can come from: Africa Europe Asia North America South America Oceania This makes it an excellent opportunity for international scholars, including applicants from Africa and developing regions, to access world-class research exposure in Germany. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following documents in English: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Two-page project proposal Two letters of recommendation Optional: Cover letter (in some cases) Key Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated based on: Originality of the project idea Feasibility within the fellowship duration Intellectual quality and depth Evidence of exceptional intellectual development The fellowship prioritizes creative thinkers who challenge traditional academic boundaries. Fellowship Duration and Timeline Duration: 5–6 months Preferred timing: Summer months Completion: Must end within the same calendar year Selection Timeline: Application Review Begins: June Final Decisions: End of July Important Rules and Conditions Applicants must note the following critical conditions: No fellowships are awarded for dissertation research Projects must be new and unrelated to prior academic work Fellows are required to: Stay in Caputh during the fellowship Deliver a public lecture at the end of the program Why You Should Apply for the Einstein Fellowship The Einstein Fellowship is more than just funding—it is an intellectual transformation experience. Here is why it stands out: 1. Interdisciplinary Freedom You are encouraged to explore ideas outside your academic comfort zone. 2. Prestigious Recognition Being selected places you among a global network of top young intellectuals. 3. Unique Research Environment Living in Einstein’s historic summerhouse creates a deeply inspiring atmosphere. 4. Global Exposure You gain access to leading academic hubs in Germany and Europe. How to Apply To apply for the Einstein Fellowship 2026: Prepare all required documents. Develop a strong, original project proposal. Submit your application through the official Einstein Forum platform. Ensure all materials are submitted before the deadline. Apply here Deadline 15th May 2026 The Einstein Fellowship 2026 is a rare and prestigious opportunity for young scholars who are ready to think beyond traditional academic boundaries. With full funding, global eligibility, and a strong emphasis on interdisciplinary innovation, it provides a platform for intellectual exploration unlike any other. title: Attend Oxford Africa Conference 2026: Key Speakers, Agenda and How to Get Tickets
summary: The Oxford Africa Conference 2026 returns for its 16th edition, bringing together some of the most influential voices shaping Africa’s future. Hosted at the prestigious University of Oxford by the Oxford University Africa Society , this year’s conference promises two days of powerful dialogue, networking, and action-driven insights. Event Details Venue: Mathematical Institute, University of Oxford (Andrew Wiles Building) Dates: Saturday, 16 May 2026 – Sunday, 17 May 2026 Theme: Anchoring Africa: Grounded Leadership in the Age of Disruption Tickets: Available now — secure your spot early as tickets sell out fast Welcome to Oxford Africa Conference 2026 The Oxford Africa Conference continues to serve as a leading platform for thought leadership on Africa’s most pressing issues. This year’s theme reflects a critical moment in Africa’s journey—marked by rapid shifts in politics, economics, technology, and climate. Over two impactful days in Oxford, the conference will convene leaders across policy, business, finance, academia, and culture to: Share diverse perspectives Challenge prevailing assumptions Foster meaningful collaborations The goal is simple yet powerful: ensure conversations translate into long-term impact across Africa and the global African community. Conference Impact Beyond dialogue, the conference is committed to turning ideas into action . By spotlighting bold innovations and supporting transformative initiatives, the Oxford Africa Conference continues to catalyze change across the continent. Keynote Speaker One of the headline speakers for 2026 is: Julius Maada Bio – President of the Republic of Sierra Leone Expect insights from top-level leadership on governance, development, and Africa’s evolving global role. Featured Panels The conference features a diverse lineup of panels addressing Africa’s most critical sectors: Grounded Governance – Public trust, institutional reform, and state capacity Engines of Growth – Economic transformation and investment opportunities Founders in Focus – Entrepreneurship and innovation ecosystems Narrating Africa – Storytelling, media, and cultural influence Powering Africa s Energy Futures – Sustainable energy solutions Building Africa s Digital Capital – Technology and digital transformation Spotlight Panel: Grounded Governance This session explores how African states can rebuild trust, strengthen accountability, and deliver services effectively at scale. Fireside Chats Networking Engage in intimate, unscripted conversations with top leaders during fireside chats designed to go beyond surface-level discussions. Social Experiences Gala Dinner: A unique opportunity to dine and connect with speakers and delegates in a historic Oxford college setting AfroBop After Party: Celebrate African culture, music, and community in a vibrant closing event Featured Speakers Meet a dynamic lineup of innovators and changemakers, including: Theo Baloyi – Founder Group CEO, Bathu Peace Hyde – Creator Executive Producer of Young, Famous African Ron Chari – Policy and governance leader Rose M. Mutiso – Founder, African Tech Futures Lab More speakers and moderators will be announced soon. Accommodation Options Attendees can choose from a range of convenient and comfortable accommodations near the venue, including: Rhodes Trust Vanbrugh House Hotel Courtyard by Marriott Oxford Voco Oxford Spires Additional options include The Head of the River and Bear and Ragged Staff. Travel Information The conference has partnered with Kenya Airways as the official travel partner. Discount: 15% off flights Promo Code: OAC26KE Booking Window: Now until 17 May 2026 Travel Dates: 9 May – 24 May 2026 Delegates are encouraged to book early to take advantage of this exclusive offer. Secure Your Spot Be part of a transformative experience shaping Africa’s future. With limited seats available and high demand expected, early registration is strongly recommended. Get your tickets now and join the conversation driving Africa forward. Discover more similar opportunities Here. title: TWAS Fellowships & Research Opportunities 2026: Fully Funded PhD, Postdoctoral Visits to Germany, and Global Research Collaboration Programs
summary: The World Academy of Sciences (TWAS), in partnership with leading global institutions such as the German Research Foundation (DFG) and the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) India, is offering a range of fully funded fellowship and research opportunities for 2026. These programmes are designed to support scientists from developing countries through advanced research training, international collaboration, and capacity building. This comprehensive guide covers three major TWAS opportunities: TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (Sub-Saharan Africa) TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (MENA Region) TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme (PhD in India) Each programme provides unique benefits, from short-term research visits in Germany to fully funded doctoral studies in India. 1. TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (Sub-Saharan Africa) The TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme for Sub-Saharan Africa offers postdoctoral researchers the opportunity to undertake a fully funded three-month research visit to Germany. Programme Overview Duration: 3 months Host Country: Germany Target Group: Postdoctoral researchers in Sub-Saharan Africa Deadline: April 15, 2026 The programme is designed to initiate and strengthen research collaborations between African scientists and German institutions, with the goal of building long-term academic partnerships. Key Benefits Participants will receive: Full travel expenses to Germany Subsistence allowance during the stay Access to advanced research facilities Opportunity to establish international research collaborations Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Hold a PhD obtained in 2021 or later Special extension for women: up to 6 additional years depending on number of children Be currently employed at a research institution in Sub-Saharan Africa Have at least 5 years of experience in the science system Not already be in Germany at the time of application Not have an established collaboration with the German host Required Documents Applicants must submit: Passport copy Invitation letter from a German host institution Two reference letters PhD certificate Proof of language proficiency (English or German) Institutional support letter Important Notes Visits must take place within 12 months of award No family support is provided Only one TWAS application is allowed per year Learn more and apply here 2. TWAS-DFG Cooperation Visits Programme (MENA Region) This programme mirrors the Sub-Saharan Africa version but is tailored for researchers in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. Programme Overview Duration: 3 months Host Country: Germany Target Group: Postdoctoral researchers in MENA Deadline: April 15, 2026 The goal is to foster collaboration between MENA researchers and German scientists. Key Benefits Travel funding Living expenses covered Access to German research institutions Opportunity for long-term collaboration Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Hold a PhD (2021 or later, with extensions for women) Be employed in a MENA-based institution Have at least 5 years of research or academic experience Not have prior collaboration with the host institution Application Requirements Similar to the SSA programme, including: Invitation letter References Academic credentials Language proof Institutional support Additional Notes Women scientists are strongly encouraged to apply All academic disciplines are eligible Collaboration must be newly initiated Learn more and apply here 3. TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme (PhD in India) The TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme offers fully funded PhD opportunities for students from developing countries to study in India. Programme Overview Level: PhD Duration: Up to 4 years Host Country: India Deadline: May 6, 2026 This programme supports research in cutting-edge areas of science and technology. Fields of Study Eligible disciplines include: Agricultural Sciences Biology and Biotechnology Medical and Health Sciences Chemistry Engineering Physics Mathematics Astronomy and Earth Sciences Fellowship Types Full-Time Fellowship For applicants not enrolled in a PhD Duration: Up to 4 years Sandwich Fellowship For PhD students already enrolled in their home country Duration: 6 months to 3 years Benefits Monthly stipend Subsidized accommodation Health insurance Access to CSIR research facilities Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be under 35 years old Be nationals of developing countries (excluding India) Hold a Master’s degree in a relevant field Be employed in their home country Provide proof of English proficiency Secure acceptance from a CSIR institution Required Documents Acceptance letter from CSIR institution Academic certificates Research proposal Proof of employment English proficiency Important Notes Applicants must return to their home country after completion No concurrent assignments allowed Only one TWAS programme application per year Learn more and apply here Why You Should Apply These TWAS opportunities offer: Fully funded international research experience Access to world-class institutions Career advancement in science and academia Global collaboration opportunities Exposure to advanced research environments Application Deadlines Summary TWAS-DFG (SSA): April 15, 2026 TWAS-DFG (MENA): April 15, 2026 TWAS-CSIR PhD: May 6, 2026 Discover more global Fellowship and Scholarship opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY). title: Fully Funded UN Climate Change Youth Event 2026: Apply Now for the ACE Hub Youth Event (Global Opportunity for Young Climate Leaders)
summary: If you are a young climate advocate, environmental innovator, or aspiring global change-maker, the 2026 Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub Youth Event offers one of the most impactful international platforms to elevate your voice, build your skills, and collaborate with global peers. Organized under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC), this prestigious event is designed to empower youth to lead meaningful climate action at all levels. What Is the ACE Hub Youth Event? The ACE Hub Youth Event is a flagship international gathering under the broader Action for Climate Empowerment (ACE) Hub, a global initiative focused on equipping individuals—especially youth—with the knowledge, skills, and tools to actively participate in climate action. The ACE Hub itself is a collaboration between UN Climate Change and the Government of North Rhine-Westphalia in Germany, supporting the implementation of climate education, public awareness, training, and participation under the Paris Agreement. This annual youth event is specifically designed to: Build the capacity of young leaders to drive climate solutions Promote collaboration and innovation in climate initiatives Facilitate global networking and knowledge exchange Strengthen youth engagement in policy and grassroots action Why You Should Apply Participating in the ACE Hub Youth Event is not just about attending a conference—it is about becoming part of a global movement shaping climate policy and action. Key Benefits Gain hands-on training in climate advocacy and project design Engage with international experts and policymakers Collaborate with youth leaders from across the world Strengthen your leadership and communication skills Expand your global network in climate and sustainability Opportunity to attend sessions linked to UN climate negotiations Participants also benefit from immersive learning experiences including workshops, peer-led sessions, and expert-led discussions. Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria) This opportunity targets young climate leaders and changemakers who are actively engaged in climate-related initiatives. Eligible Category of People Youth leaders Climate activists and advocates Environmental professionals Students and early-career researchers Community organizers working on climate solutions General Requirements Applicants must: Be aged 18 to 35 years Have proficiency in English (spoken and written) Be able to attend the full event in person in Bonn, Germany Demonstrate interest or experience in climate action Be engaged in areas such as: Climate education Public awareness Community-based climate initiatives Policy advocacy Geographic Eligibility Open to all countries worldwide Additional slots available for local youth from North Rhine-Westphalia (Germany) Program Structure and Activities The ACE Hub Youth Event is structured as a 3-day intensive experience combining learning, collaboration, and innovation. What to Expect Day 1: Skills-building workshops and peer-led sessions Day 2: Collaborative group work on climate solutions Day 3: Networking, presentations, and knowledge exchange Participants will: Present their own climate initiatives Work on solving real-world environmental challenges Participate in expert-led sessions on communication and advocacy The program emphasizes interactive learning and practical application, ensuring participants leave with actionable skills. Themes and Focus Areas The 2026 edition continues to focus on youth leadership and climate entrepreneurship, with strong emphasis on: Community-based climate action Innovation and sustainable solutions Climate communication and advocacy Local-to-global climate impact strategies Funding and Financial Support While the event includes both funded and self-funded participation options: A limited number of international participants may receive funding support Additional participants may attend through self-funding or independent accreditation Funding (when available) may cover: Travel expenses Accommodation Participation costs Why This Opportunity Matters Climate change is one of the most urgent global challenges of our time, and youth are at the forefront of driving solutions. The ACE Hub Youth Event directly contributes to empowering young people under Article 6 of the UNFCCC and Article 12 of the Paris Agreement, which focus on education, awareness, and participation. By participating, you are not only gaining skills—you are becoming part of a global ecosystem working toward sustainable and equitable climate solutions. Whether you are already leading initiatives or just starting your journey, this opportunity provides the platform, resources, and network needed to amplify your impact. title: Anzisha Coach Job Opportunity 2026 (Remote) at African Leadership Academy: Empower Africa’s Next Generation of Entrepreneurs
summary: If you are passionate about entrepreneurship, youth development, and creating meaningful impact across Africa, the Anzisha Coach position at African Leadership Academy offers a unique and transformative career path. This role sits at the intersection of education and entrepreneurship, two of the most critical drivers of economic growth and innovation across the African continent. Through its flagship initiative, Anzisha Program , the Academy is shaping a generation of young entrepreneurs who are building sustainable businesses and creating jobs in their communities. The position is ideal for experienced professionals who want to mentor, coach, and directly influence the growth of early-stage entrepreneurs across Africa. About the Anzisha Program The Anzisha Program is a partnership-driven initiative supported by organizations such as Mastercard Foundation . It focuses on empowering very young entrepreneurs (typically under 22 years old) to build and scale impactful businesses. The program consists of three key stages: 1. Idea Program (3 Months) A foundational stage where young entrepreneurs: Develop a Minimum Viable Product (MVP) Validate business ideas with real-world sales or impact Prepare for scaling opportunities 2. Build Program (5 Months) Focused on: Strengthening business models Driving measurable growth (revenue, jobs, impact) Building sustainable and scalable ventures 3. Venture Building Fellowship A multi-year fellowship that: Provides continuous business and leadership support Recognizes outstanding young entrepreneurs Helps scale ventures across markets Role Overview: What Does an Anzisha Coach Do? As an Anzisha Coach, you will play a pivotal role in shaping Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem by working directly with young founders. This is not just a coaching role—it is a high-impact leadership position involving mentorship, program design, ecosystem building, and strategic execution. You will: Provide one-on-one coaching Support entrepreneurs in business development Facilitate training sessions and workshops Connect founders to networks, partners, and investors Track progress using data-driven insights Key Responsibilities Explained in Detail 1. Coaching and Mentorship You will serve as a trusted advisor to young entrepreneurs by: Guiding business strategy, financial planning, and marketing execution Helping founders navigate real-world challenges Building strong, empathy-driven relationships Supporting participants across multiple programs, including AL for Education initiatives You will also travel across African countries to: Visit entrepreneurs Understand local business environments Provide in-person support where necessary 2. Program Development and Project Management This role requires strong operational leadership. You will: Design and implement entrepreneurship programs Lead workshops on leadership and business growth Develop innovative support strategies for young founders Manage projects end-to-end, from concept to execution Additionally, you will: Deliver tailored interventions with external partners Contribute to monthly fellowship reports Continuously improve program effectiveness 3. Networking and Ecosystem Building A major part of your work will involve building connections across Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem: Link entrepreneurs to industry experts, mentors, and investors Identify strategic partners across different countries Support expansion of the Anzisha network 4. Talent Identification and Selection You will help discover the next generation of entrepreneurs by: Identifying promising candidates Supporting the selection process Reviewing and grading applications and assignments 5. Tracking, Evaluation, and Reporting Data-driven decision-making is central to this role. You will: Monitor entrepreneur performance Track progress against KPIs Provide detailed performance reports Use insights to improve program delivery 6. Collaboration and Thought Leadership As a visible representative of the program, you will: Share insights through articles, podcasts, and events Participate in outreach activities and information sessions Support content creation and storytelling Conduct research on challenges facing African entrepreneurs Minimum Requirements and Qualifications Educational Background Bachelor’s degree in: Business Entrepreneurship Or a related field A Master’s degree is an added advantage Professional Experience Candidates must have: Proven experience as: Entrepreneur Business consultant Coach Strong understanding of entrepreneurship principles Experience working with young people Demonstrated leadership and emotional intelligence Core Skills Required Project management and execution Data analysis and KPI tracking Problem-solving and critical thinking Communication and interpersonal skills Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities Preferred Qualifications Financial expertise and investor network Experience running a business in Africa Multilingual ability (especially French) Strong stakeholder management skills Competencies and Personal Attributes To succeed in this role, candidates must demonstrate: Technical Competencies Coaching and mentorship Program development Networking and partnerships Monitoring and evaluation Personal Attributes High integrity and professionalism Emotional maturity and resilience Strong interpersonal and conflict-resolution skills Ability to work under pressure Initiative and proactive mindset Who Can Apply? Eligible Candidates This opportunity is open to: Professionals with mid to senior-level experience Individuals passionate about entrepreneurship and youth development Candidates with experience working in or with African markets Geographic Eligibility Open to applicants across Africa Remote role, but: Preference is given to candidates based in South Africa Must be willing to travel across African countries Apply here Why This Opportunity Matters This is more than a job—it is a chance to: Shape Africa’s entrepreneurial future Mentor the next generation of business leaders Work within a high-impact, pan-African organization Contribute to job creation and economic development By joining the African Leadership Academy , you become part of a mission-driven ecosystem committed to transforming Africa through leadership and innovation. Deadline 17th April 2026 The Anzisha Coach role is ideal for individuals who combine entrepreneurial experience, coaching ability, and a passion for impact. title: Launch Your Global Career: Apply for NATO Internships 2026 in the Netherlands
summary: Looking to gain international experience with a globally recognized organization? The NATO Internships 2026 at Joint Force Command Brunssum (JFCBS) offer students a unique opportunity to work across diverse fields such as political affairs, cybersecurity, logistics, and legal services. Based in the Netherlands, this paid internship program provides hands-on experience, professional exposure, and the chance to contribute to real-world international operations. Applications close on 22 April 2026 . About the Opportunity The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) is offering multiple internship opportunities at its Joint Force Command Brunssum (JFCBS) headquarters in the Netherlands. This program is designed to give students practical experience in an international, multidisciplinary environment. Interns will have the opportunity to work across a wide range of departments, depending on their interests and academic background. Available internship areas include: Psychological Operations (PsyOps) Political Affairs (POLAD) Legal Affairs (LEGAD) Civil-Military Cooperation (CIMIC) Budget and Finance (J8 Budget) Education and Training (J7 E IT) Cyberspace and IT (J6 CYD) Logistics and Information Systems (J4 LISS) Provost Marshal Office (PMO) Integrated Advisory Team (IAT) These internships allow participants to contribute to ongoing projects, support operational functions, and gain insight into how NATO operates across multiple domains. The internships are based in Brunssum, Netherlands , and are structured as part of NATO’s broader effort to engage and train the next generation of professionals in international security, policy, and operations. Why This Opportunity Matters Internships at NATO provide more than just work experience—they offer insight into how international cooperation functions in practice. As global challenges become increasingly complex, there is a growing need for professionals who understand multidisciplinary approaches to security, governance, and collaboration. By working across areas such as cybersecurity, logistics, political affairs, and civil-military cooperation, interns gain a holistic understanding of how large international organizations operate. This exposure is especially valuable for students aiming to pursue careers in global institutions, government, or international development. Additionally, the opportunity to work in a multicultural environment helps participants develop essential skills such as communication, adaptability, and cross-cultural collaboration. These are critical competencies in today’s interconnected world. Overall, the NATO internship program equips students with both technical knowledge and practical experience , preparing them for future roles in international careers. What You’ll Gain This internship provides a range of valuable professional and financial benefits: Monthly stipend of €1,477.30 , providing financial support during the internship Reimbursement of travel costs (within set limits) Hands-on experience in an international and highly professional environment Exposure to global operations , policy-making, and strategic processes Opportunity to work across multiple disciplines , from cybersecurity to political affairs Networking opportunities with international professionals and experts Structured work schedule , including 2 days of leave per month This opportunity is ideal for students seeking to build a career in international relations, security, policy, or technical fields . Eligibility To be eligible for the NATO internship program, applicants must: Be currently enrolled in full-time education Ensure that the internship is part of their academic program Remain enrolled for the entire duration of the internship Applicants must also meet the application requirements, including submitting all necessary documents through the official platform. It is important to note that applications prepared using generative AI tools will not be accepted , so candidates must ensure that all submitted materials are their own original work. Application Process and Deadline To apply for the NATO internship program, candidates must follow these steps carefully: Apply through the NATO Talent Acquisition Platform (NTAP): Complete the application form via the official platform. Select an Internship Opportunity: Choose the specific internship at HQ JFC Brunssum that aligns with your interests (e.g., PsyOps, Legal, Cyberspace, Logistics, etc.). Submit Required Documents: CV/Resume One-page motivation letter explaining your interest, expectations, and objectives Ensure Completeness: Incomplete applications will not be considered. Deadline: 22 April 2026 (11:59 PM) To learn more and apply, visit the official NATO internship porta l. title: Unlock a PR Career Boost: Apply for the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship 2026
summary: If you’re a graduate student looking to break into the public relations industry, the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship 2026 offers a powerful opportunity to gain hands-on experience, earn competitive pay, and publish your research. This prestigious summer fellowship combines real-world PR work with research and industry exposure, making it ideal for students ready to take their careers to the next level. Applications close on April 17, 2026 . About the Opportunity The Ketchum PRISM Fellowship for Excellence in Public Relations Research, Insights, Strategy, and Measurement is a highly competitive program designed for graduate students in public relations, communications, business, and related fields. Organized by Ketchum Analytics in collaboration with the Institute for Public Relations (IPR) , this fellowship focuses on bridging the gap between academic research and real-world PR practice. It emphasizes “research that matters,” equipping participants with the ability to generate insights that directly impact communication strategies and business decisions. The fellowship runs for eight weeks during the summer (mid-June to mid-August 2026) and takes place in person at one of Ketchum’s offices in New York City, Chicago, or Dallas . During this period, fellows will work closely with Ketchum’s Analytics and Strategy teams, contributing to real client projects and gaining exposure to the inner workings of a global PR agency. In addition to practical experience, fellows are required to produce a research paper aimed at industry practitioners. This paper, which should incorporate modern tools and innovative approaches, may be published on the Institute for Public Relations website—offering significant professional visibility. What You’ll Gain This fellowship offers a combination of financial, professional, and academic benefits: Paid experience at $22 per hour for approximately 40 hours per week Hands-on PR industry exposure , working on real projects with experienced professionals Research publication opportunity on the Institute for Public Relations platform $2,500 grant awarded upon acceptance of the research paper Industry recognition , including participation in the IPR Annual Awards Gala in New York City Skill development in analytics, strategy, and communication research Networking opportunities with leading PR professionals and researchers This opportunity is particularly valuable for students aiming to build a career in public relations, communications strategy, or research and analytics . Why This Opportunity Matters In today’s data-driven communication landscape, public relations is no longer just about messaging—it is about insights, measurement, and strategic decision-making . Organizations increasingly rely on research to understand audiences, evaluate campaigns, and drive impact. The Ketchum PRISM Fellowship addresses this shift by training the next generation of PR professionals to think critically, analyze data, and produce meaningful insights. By combining hands-on agency experience with research development, the program ensures that participants are well-prepared for the evolving demands of the industry. Moreover, the opportunity to publish research and present findings to industry audiences gives fellows a competitive edge. It allows them to build credibility, expand their professional network, and position themselves as thought leaders early in their careers. For students seeking to stand out in a competitive job market, this fellowship offers both practical experience and long-term career value . Eligibility To be eligible for the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship, applicants must: Be a graduate student (Master’s or PhD) in public relations, communications, business, or a related research field Have completed at least one year of study Demonstrate fluency in written and spoken English Be able to work at least three days per week in person at a Ketchum office (New York City, Chicago, or Dallas) Be available to work approximately 40 hours per week during the fellowship period Post-doctoral candidates are also encouraged to apply. Applicants should have a strong interest in public relations research, measurement, and strategy , as well as the ability to translate complex ideas into practical insights. Application Process and Deadline To apply for the Ketchum PRISM Fellowship, candidates must prepare and submit the following materials: Statement of Interest: Explain why you are a strong candidate for the fellowship Research Prospectus (max 1,000 words): Outline the research topic you plan to explore Resume: Highlight relevant academic, research, or professional experience Applications are reviewed by a panel of public relations researchers and practitioners. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews with Ketchum. Deadline: April 17, 2026 Fellowship Period: Mid-June to mid-August 2026 Final Paper Deadline: November 10, 2026 To learn more and apply, visit the official Ketchum PRISM website . title: Apply Now: McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship 2026
summary: Are you a Ukrainian professional ready to shape your country’s future? The McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship is now open, offering a unique opportunity to develop leadership skills, engage with global experts, and contribute to Ukraine’s post-war reconstruction. This fully funded, three-month hybrid fellowship combines online learning with an in-person workshop in Bosnia and Herzegovina, providing both practical skills and international exposure. About the Opportunity The McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship is a pilot initiative launched by the McCain Institute at Arizona State University. It is designed to support a new generation of Ukrainian leaders committed to rebuilding a democratic, inclusive, and resilient Ukraine. The fellowship honors the legacy of Senator John McCain, who was a strong advocate for Ukraine’s sovereignty and democratic development. Through this program, the McCain Institute aims to equip Ukrainian professionals with the tools, knowledge, and networks needed to contribute meaningfully to post-war recovery efforts. This three-month hybrid program will run from July to September 2026 . Participants will engage in a combination of virtual leadership modules, thematic discussions, and collaborative work with peers from diverse sectors. A key highlight of the fellowship is a fully funded, week-long in-person workshop in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina (August 15–22, 2026) . This immersive experience allows fellows to connect in person, deepen their learning, and collaborate on solutions for Ukraine’s reconstruction. Throughout the program, fellows will work together to develop recommendations for a democracy-centered and inclusive reconstruction process , while also creating actionable plans to apply their learning within their own communities and sectors. Why This Opportunity Matters Ukraine’s reconstruction will require strong, visionary leadership across multiple sectors. Beyond infrastructure rebuilding, the country faces complex challenges related to governance, economic recovery, social cohesion, and environmental sustainability. This fellowship addresses these challenges by investing in people—equipping leaders with the skills and networks necessary to drive meaningful change. By fostering collaboration across sectors, the program encourages holistic solutions that reflect the interconnected nature of reconstruction efforts. Importantly, the fellowship emphasizes democracy, inclusion, and community-driven approaches , ensuring that rebuilding efforts are not only effective but also equitable. Participants will gain the tools to influence policy, mobilize resources, and implement solutions that benefit their communities. In a global context, this initiative highlights the importance of international cooperation and knowledge exchange , bringing together local expertise and global perspectives to address one of the most pressing challenges of our time. What You’ll Gain This fellowship offers a powerful combination of professional development and global exposure: Fully funded participation , including travel and accommodation for the international workshop Advanced leadership training focused on real-world challenges in post-war reconstruction Technical skills development in areas such as communication and proposal writing Access to international experts and practitioners in reconstruction and policy Networking opportunities within the Washington, D.C. policy space Cross-sector collaboration with leaders from diverse backgrounds Practical impact , through action plans and policy recommendations This is an exceptional opportunity for Ukrainian professionals looking to expand their influence, strengthen their expertise, and contribute to national recovery efforts . Eligibility To apply for the McCain Global Leaders Ukraine Fellowship, candidates must meet the following criteria: Be based in Ukraine Have at least six years of relevant professional experience Demonstrate working proficiency in English Have permission from the Ukrainian government to travel internationally for the August workshop The program is open to professionals from a wide range of sectors, including: Policy Veterans Civil society Business and technology Environment and ecology Media Culture The fellowship strongly encourages applications from individuals outside the Kyiv region , promoting geographic diversity and inclusion. Application Process and Deadline To apply for the fellowship, candidates must prepare and submit the following materials: A CV (maximum 2 pages) A cover letter (maximum 1 page) explaining your motivation and relevant experience An essay (maximum 1 page) addressing: Key post-war reconstruction challenges in your sector How these challenges connect with other sectors How the fellowship will help you create impact Applications must be submitted through the official application platform. Candidates should ensure all materials are complete, as incomplete applications will not be considered. Deadline: 30 April 2026 (17:00 EST) Applicants must also be willing to undergo a background check as part of the selection process. To learn more and apply, visit the official McCain Institute website . title: Constellation Fund Grant 2026: A Comprehensive Funding Opportunity to Scale Impact in Education, Employment, and Health
summary: Organizations committed to tackling poverty and driving meaningful social change have a significant opportunity to secure funding through the Constellation Fund Grant Cycle for April 2026. This initiative is designed to support high-impact programs that improve the lives of individuals and families living below the federal poverty threshold, with a strong emphasis on scalability, sustainability, and measurable outcomes. As economic inequality and access disparities continue to affect communities, especially in urban and underserved areas, targeted funding mechanisms like the Constellation Fund play a critical role in empowering organizations that are already delivering solutions on the ground. A Strategic Approach to Fighting Poverty The Constellation Fund distinguishes itself by focusing on solutions that are closest to the challenges they aim to solve. Rather than supporting theoretical or early-stage ideas alone, the fund prioritizes organizations and programs that have demonstrated effectiveness and are ready to expand their reach. This strategic approach ensures that funding is directed toward initiatives that not only address immediate needs but also create long-term, systemic change. By investing in proven interventions, the fund helps amplify impact and accelerate progress toward poverty reduction. Focus Areas for the 2026 Grant Cycle For the April 2026 application round, the Constellation Fund is concentrating on three core sectors that are essential to improving quality of life and economic mobility: Education Education remains one of the most powerful tools for breaking the cycle of poverty. The fund supports programs that enhance access to quality education, improve learning outcomes, and equip individuals with skills necessary for future employment. This includes initiatives focused on youth development, adult education, and workforce training. Employment Economic stability is directly linked to employment opportunities. The fund seeks to support programs that help individuals secure stable jobs, increase their income, and build sustainable livelihoods. This may include job training programs, career readiness initiatives, and partnerships with employers. Health Health and well-being are fundamental to personal and community resilience. The fund supports organizations that provide access to healthcare services, promote mental health, and address social determinants of health. Programs that integrate health with economic and educational outcomes are particularly valuable. These focus areas are interconnected, reflecting the understanding that poverty is a multidimensional issue requiring integrated solutions. Who Can Apply and What Makes a Strong Candidate To be eligible for this funding opportunity, organizations must operate within specific counties in Minnesota, including Anoka, Carver, Dakota, Hennepin, Ramsey, Scott, and Washington, all within the broader metropolitan area of Minneapolis . In addition, applicants must: Primarily serve individuals living below 200% of the federal poverty guidelines Deliver programs aligned with one or more of the fund’s core impact areas Demonstrate a track record of measurable impact Show strong potential for growth or scalability Organizations that are most competitive are those that can clearly articulate their impact, provide data or evidence of success, and present a compelling vision for how additional funding will enable them to expand or deepen their work. Application Timeline and Process The application window for the Constellation Fund Grant 2026 is limited, making early preparation essential: Applications Open: 1 April 2026 Deadline: 30 April 2026 All applications submitted during this period will be reviewed for consideration in the Fall 2026 grant round. The application process is designed to be accessible while still rigorous. Applicants are encouraged to review available resources, including a video overview and frequently asked questions, to better understand expectations and requirements. Organizations may also reach out directly to program officers for clarification or guidance, ensuring that applicants can submit the strongest possible proposals. What the Fund Looks For The Constellation Fund is particularly interested in: Programs with a proven model that can be replicated or scaled Organizations that demonstrate strong leadership and operational capacity Initiatives that address root causes of poverty rather than only symptoms Clear plans for measuring outcomes and tracking progress This focus ensures that funding is not only impactful in the short term but also contributes to sustainable development and long-term community resilience. Beyond Funding: Building Long-Term Impact Receiving a grant from the Constellation Fund is not just about financial support—it is also about becoming part of a broader ecosystem of changemakers. Funded organizations gain recognition and validation that can help attract additional partners, donors, and opportunities. Moreover, the emphasis on scalability and impact encourages organizations to think strategically about growth, innovation, and long-term sustainability. Final Thoughts The Constellation Fund Grant 2026 represents a powerful opportunity for organizations working at the forefront of poverty reduction to scale their impact and reach more communities in need. By focusing on education, employment, and health, and by supporting proven, high-impact initiatives, the fund plays a crucial role in advancing equitable and sustainable development. For organizations ready to take their work to the next level, this grant offers not only financial support but also a platform to amplify their mission and create lasting change. title: Apply Now: Remote Climate Researcher/Writer Role with the Institute for Human Rights and Business
summary: Are you an experienced researcher or writer passionate about climate adaptation and social impact? The Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) is hiring 1–2 Researchers/Writers to lead global storytelling and research on climate resilience. This fully remote consultancy offers a competitive package of approximately £70,000 over 18 months , along with the opportunity to influence climate policy and amplify community-led solutions worldwide. Applications close on 15 April 2026 . About the Opportunity The Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) is an international think tank founded in 2009, working at the intersection of business, human rights, and sustainability. Through collaboration with governments, companies, and civil society, IHRB aims to promote accountability and ensure that human rights are embedded in global business practices. This opportunity is part of IHRB’s JUST Stories project , a flagship initiative focused on documenting and sharing real-world examples of community-led climate adaptation and resilience . The project highlights how local communities—especially those on the frontlines of climate change—are designing and implementing solutions that can inform global policy and investment decisions. The selected candidates will play a leading role in the second phase of the JUST Stories project , which focuses on producing high-quality, evidence-based narratives. These stories are designed to influence decision-makers across sectors such as government, finance, and civil society, while authentically representing the voices and experiences of communities. This role combines research, storytelling, and policy engagement , making it ideal for professionals who can bridge academic analysis with compelling narrative writing. Why This Opportunity Matters Climate adaptation is one of the most urgent global priorities, particularly for communities that are already experiencing the impacts of climate change. However, many existing solutions fail to fully incorporate the perspectives and knowledge of those most affected. The JUST Stories project addresses this gap by documenting and elevating community-led solutions , ensuring that these approaches inform global strategies and decision-making processes. By focusing on real-world examples, the project helps bridge the gap between policy and practice. This role is especially important because it combines evidence-based research with powerful storytelling , enabling complex ideas to reach and influence a broader audience. The selected candidates will contribute to shaping more inclusive, effective, and human-centered climate policies . What You’ll Gain This role offers a unique combination of professional and global impact benefits: Competitive Compensation: Earn approximately £70,000 over the contract period. Global Influence: Contribute to research that informs climate adaptation policies and investment decisions. Meaningful Work: Amplify the voices of frontline communities and showcase real-world solutions. International Exposure: Engage with global stakeholders and participate in high-level events. Flexible Work: Work remotely with a flexible schedule while contributing to a major global initiative. This opportunity is particularly valuable for professionals seeking to combine research, storytelling, and advocacy in the climate and development space . Eligibility Applicants should meet the following requirements: At least 7 years of experience in research, writing, or related fields Strong expertise in climate adaptation and resilience , particularly community-led approaches Proven experience in qualitative research, interviews, and long-form writing Familiarity with frameworks such as UN climate adaptation or resilience frameworks Excellent analytical and storytelling skills Additional desirable qualifications include: Experience working with Indigenous communities or vulnerable populations Background in journalism, development studies, anthropology, or human rights Knowledge of climate finance, policy, or philanthropy sectors Additional language skills relevant to project regions Candidates must also be able to work remotely, manage their own schedule, and collaborate effectively across time zones. Application Process and Deadline To apply for the Researcher/Writer role: Complete the Application Form: Fill out the official online application form. title: Climate Resilience Internship 2026 in Washington, DC: Gain Hands-On Experience in Climate Policy and Community Resilience
summary: The Climate Resilience Internship at the Center for Climate and Energy Solutions offers a unique opportunity for students and early-career professionals to contribute to impactful climate initiatives while gaining real-world experience in policy research, stakeholder engagement, and sustainability strategy. Based in Washington, DC , this Summer 2026 internship provides a dynamic environment where interns work alongside leading experts to address some of the most pressing challenges related to climate change and community resilience. About C2ES The Center for Climate and Energy Solutions (C2ES) is a highly respected, independent, and nonpartisan nonprofit organization dedicated to accelerating the transition to a low-carbon, resilient, and equitable economy. The organization collaborates with policymakers, businesses, and international stakeholders to drive meaningful climate action. With a strong commitment to inclusivity, collaboration, and innovation, C2ES fosters a work culture that values diverse perspectives and emphasizes real-world impact. Internship Overview As a Climate Resilience Intern, you will work directly with the Climate Resilience Manager and program team to support research and engagement initiatives focused on strengthening community resilience to climate change. The internship is full-time (up to 37.5 hours per week) during Summer 2026 and offers flexibility, allowing participants to work either in-person at the Washington, DC office or remotely from eligible U.S. states. Key Responsibilities Interns will engage in a variety of meaningful tasks, including: Conducting independent research on climate resilience topics such as extreme heat policies, environmental regulations, and disaster preparedness Supporting research on local and federal climate policies and resilience strategies Assisting with stakeholder engagement and outreach for the Climate Resilient Communities Accelerator Contributing to event planning, logistics, and coordination Developing written outputs such as blog posts, policy briefs, and research summaries Participating in interdisciplinary discussions and supporting broader program activities Learning Opportunities This internship provides exposure to cutting-edge climate resilience initiatives, particularly through involvement in the South-Central Puget Sound Accelerator. Interns will explore: Nature-based solutions and urban planning strategies Public-private partnerships in climate action Economic development approaches linked to resilience Data-driven and policy-oriented climate solutions The program emphasizes both independent thinking and collaborative learning, allowing interns to tailor their research focus based on their academic background and interests. Eligibility Criteria Applicants should demonstrate: A strong interest in climate change, sustainability, or environmental policy Excellent writing and analytical skills The ability to work independently and collaboratively A willingness to engage with diverse stakeholders Academic background in fields such as environmental studies, engineering, political science, public policy, economics, or related disciplines Internship Format and Benefits Full-time internship (Summer 2026) Hourly compensation of $20 per hour Transportation stipend for in-person interns Opportunity to work remotely from eligible U.S. states Hands-on experience with a leading climate policy organization Application Process Applicants must submit the following: Resume Cover letter (maximum one page) Academic transcripts Writing sample (maximum five pages, preferably related to climate or environmental topics) Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged. Diversity and Inclusion Commitment C2ES is deeply committed to fostering diversity, equity, inclusion, and justice within its organization and partnerships. The internship encourages applications from individuals of all backgrounds, ensuring a wide range of perspectives in advancing climate solutions. Final Thoughts The Climate Resilience Internship at C2ES is an excellent opportunity for aspiring climate professionals to gain practical experience, contribute to meaningful projects, and build a strong foundation in climate policy and resilience strategies. Whether working remotely or in Washington, DC, interns will be part of a mission-driven team shaping the future of climate action. title: NECE Lab 2026 in Warsaw: Master Civic Dialogue and Address Polarisation Across Europe
summary: In an era marked by increasing societal divisions and ideological polarisation, the need for effective civic dialogue has never been more urgent. The NECE Lab 2026, titled “Working with Polarised Groups in Civic Education,” offers a unique opportunity for civic educators, activists, and changemakers to gain hands-on experience in designing and scaling impactful dialogue initiatives. Scheduled to take place from 12–14 July 2026 in Warsaw , this immersive lab is designed to move beyond theory and equip participants with practical tools to foster meaningful conversations across differences. Why Civic Dialogue Matters Today Across Europe, rising divisions are reshaping public discourse and creating challenges for those working in civic education. Dialogue is often viewed as a solution to bridge divides, but successful dialogue requires more than facilitation skills. It demands strategic planning, inclusive recruitment, innovative formats, and the ability to translate discussions into real-world impact. The NECE Lab addresses these challenges by focusing on building “dialogue infrastructure”—the systems and processes needed to organize, scale, and sustain civic engagement initiatives. What You Will Learn and Experience Participants in the NECE Lab will engage in a dynamic blend of expert-led sessions, interactive workshops, and immersive dialogue experiences. Drawing on the extensive experience of the New Community Foundation , which has facilitated over 120 dialogue sessions and 100 workshops in Poland, the program offers real-world insights and tested methodologies. Key learning outcomes include: Designing and implementing civic dialogue initiatives Reaching ideologically diverse and often hard-to-engage audiences Framing sensitive or polarising topics constructively Managing both online and in-person dialogue formats Communicating outcomes effectively to maximize social impact A distinctive feature of the Lab is its dual-format approach, allowing participants to explore both digital and on-site dialogue methods. This ensures adaptability and relevance across different contexts and communities. Hands-On Project Development Beyond learning, participants will have the opportunity to develop their own dialogue project ideas . These projects will be refined with feedback from experts and peers, enabling participants to transform concepts into actionable initiatives. Importantly, selected participants are expected to organize a follow-up dialogue activity within 3–6 months after the Lab , sharing knowledge with a broader network of civic educators and engaged citizens. Financial Support and Accessibility To ensure accessibility, the program offers financial support for participants traveling from across Europe. Selected participants may receive travel reimbursement of up to 500 EUR for journeys to and from Warsaw. Even if applicants are able to cover their own travel costs, they are still encouraged to apply. Organisers and Partners The NECE Lab is organised by the New Community Foundation in cooperation with THE CIVICS Innovation Hub . The initiative is financially supported by the German Federal Agency for Civic Education and co-funded by the European Union , reinforcing its credibility and impact within the European civic education landscape. Application Process and Deadline Applications for the NECE Lab 2026 are open until: Deadline: 26 April 2026, 11:59 PM (CEST) Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early and demonstrate their motivation to implement dialogue initiatives in their communities. For applicants based in Poland, direct contact with the organizers is required via the New Community Foundation. Final Thoughts The NECE Lab 2026 is more than just a training program—it is a platform for building the future of civic dialogue in Europe. By equipping participants with practical tools, real-world experience, and a strong network, the Lab empowers individuals to actively combat polarisation and foster inclusive, constructive public discourse. title: Rare Impact Fund 2026 Grants: A Comprehensive Global Funding Opportunity Advancing Youth Mental Health (Up to $500,000 Available)
summary: The Rare Impact Fund is a globally recognized philanthropic initiative founded in 2020 by Selena Gomez . The fund focuses on addressing the global youth mental health crisis by supporting organizations that provide education, therapy access, and community-based mental health services. Since its inception, it has already raised over $20 million and supported 30+ nonprofit organizations across five continents, impacting millions of young people. Rare Impact Fund 2026 Grant Opportunity (RFP) One of the most significant current opportunities under this initiative is the Rare Impact Fund Request for Proposals (RFP) 2026, designed to strengthen the youth mental health workforce. Benefits of the Rare Impact Fund Grant Organizations selected for funding receive more than just financial support: 1. Significant Funding Support Grants of up to $500,000 enable large-scale program expansion. 2. Global Visibility Being associated with the Rare Impact Fund enhances credibility and recognition. 3.
