title: Fully Funded PhD in African Health Economics & Diagnostics at LSE 2026: Complete Guide to the AHEaD Studentship Opportunity
summary: The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) is offering two highly competitive, fully funded PhD studentships under the Wellcome-funded African Health, Economics and Diagnostics (AHEaD) Project. This prestigious doctoral opportunity is designed for candidates who are passionate about advancing research at the intersection of health systems, economics, and diagnostic technologies, particularly within African contexts. This studentship goes beyond a traditional PhD. It integrates rigorous academic training with real-world policy research, offering candidates the chance to contribute directly to a major international research programme shaping the future of diagnostics and healthcare systems across Africa. About the AHEaD Project The African Health, Economics and Diagnostics (AHEaD) Project is a major research initiative funded by Wellcome, focused on understanding and improving the role of diagnostics in health systems across Africa. Diagnostics, particularly point-of-care tests, are critical in improving early detection, treatment decisions, and overall health outcomes. However, their accessibility, affordability, and effectiveness are influenced by complex economic, political, and systemic factors. The AHEaD project seeks to address these challenges by examining: How diagnostic tools are developed, produced, and distributed The economic and health impacts of diagnostic technologies Policy frameworks that influence diagnostic markets Barriers to access and adoption across different health systems PhD candidates will play a central role in this research, contributing to evidence-based insights that can influence policy, markets, and healthcare delivery systems. Programme Overview Successful applicants will be enrolled in the MPhil/PhD in Health Policy at LSE while working closely with the AHEaD research team. Key Programme Details Institution: London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE) Department: LSE Health Department of Health Policy Programme: MPhil/PhD in Health Policy Start Date: September 2026 Duration: 4 years (full-time) Funding: Fully funded (home and overseas tuition covered) Deadline: April 27, 2026 (5:00 PM) This dual structure ensures that candidates receive both world-class academic training and hands-on research experience. Research Scope and Thematic Areas The AHEaD PhD studentships support a wide range of research topics within the economics of diagnostics. Candidates are encouraged to develop innovative, interdisciplinary research proposals aligned with the project’s goals. Key Research Areas Political Economy of Diagnostics Manufacturing systems and supply chains Market dynamics and governance structures Institutional influences on diagnostic availability Health and Economic Impact Cost-effectiveness of diagnostic technologies Macroeconomic benefits of local production Impact on healthcare delivery and outcomes Health Technology Assessment (HTA) Evaluation of diagnostic tools Reimbursement and pricing models Policy implications for adoption and scale-up Financing and Regulatory Frameworks Investment strategies in diagnostics Regulatory policies and incentives Sustainable market development Access and Uptake of Diagnostics Affordability challenges Behavioral and systemic barriers Data integration and digital health systems While the research has a strong African focus, applicants are encouraged to incorporate comparative or global perspectives where relevant. Structure of the PhD Programme The four-year doctoral programme is carefully structured to balance coursework, independent research, and active participation in the AHEaD project. Year 1: Foundation and Research Development Completion of required coursework in health policy and economics Development and refinement of PhD research topic Early engagement with AHEaD research activities Years 2–4: Advanced Research and Fieldwork Full-time doctoral research Fieldwork and primary data collection (including international travel) Collaboration with global research partners Contribution to AHEaD project outputs Ongoing Academic Engagement Throughout the programme, candidates will: Meet regularly with supervisors and project managers Participate in seminars, workshops, and academic discussions Contribute to collaborative research initiatives Engage in interdisciplinary learning and networking Funding and Financial Support One of the most attractive aspects of this opportunity is its comprehensive financial package. What the Funding Covers Full tuition fees for both home and international students Research Assistant role with additional income (up to approximately 20 hours per week) Funding for travel related to fieldwork and data collection Support for attending conferences and engaging with global research networks This ensures that candidates can focus fully on their research without financial constraints. Roles and Responsibilities of PhD Candidates PhD students under the AHEaD programme will take on multiple roles as researchers, collaborators, and contributors to a broader research ecosystem. Key Responsibilities Conduct independent doctoral research aligned with AHEaD objectives Support literature reviews and evidence synthesis Participate in data collection and empirical analysis Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams Contribute to policy-relevant research outputs Candidates are expected to demonstrate initiative, intellectual curiosity, and the ability to work both independently and collaboratively. Eligibility Criteria and Person Specification The programme seeks highly motivated candidates with strong academic backgrounds and a clear interest in health economics and policy. Essential Requirements Master’s degree in economics, political economy, or a related field Strong analytical and quantitative skills Experience with economic or policy-relevant datasets Demonstrated interest in diagnostics, health systems, or public policy Excellent academic writing and communication skills Ability to work independently and within teams Additional Requirements Willingness to reside in the UK for the duration of the programme Availability for international travel (1–2 months at a time) Strong problem-solving and research capabilities Desirable Qualifications Experience working in African contexts (e.g., Senegal, Ethiopia) French language proficiency Background in econometrics, HTA, or applied economics Knowledge of diagnostic, pharmaceutical, or healthcare markets Entry Requirements Applicants must meet LSE’s PhD entry requirements, including: A Master’s degree with a minimum average equivalent to 65% A dissertation mark of at least 65% Importantly, prior expertise in diagnostics is not required, making this opportunity accessible to candidates from diverse academic and professional backgrounds. Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide To apply for the AHEaD PhD studentship, candidates must complete a Formal Application to LSE. Steps to Apply Apply to the MPhil/PhD in Health Policy at LSE Clearly indicate “Economics of Diagnostics: AHEaD” in your application Submit a research proposal (maximum 1,000 words) Writing a Strong Research Proposal The research proposal is a critical component of the application and should demonstrate: Identification of a clear and relevant research gap Justification of the topic’s importance and timeliness Identification of key stakeholders Clear methodology and data sources Potential policy or system-level impact Applicants should aim for conceptual clarity, methodological rigor, and alignment with micro, meso, or macro-level analysis. Application Deadline The final deadline to submit your application is: April 27, 2026 (5:00 PM) Late submissions are not accepted, and early preparation is strongly advised. Why This Opportunity Stands Out This PhD studentship is not just an academic programme—it is a career-defining opportunity. Key Advantages Study at one of the world’s leading institutions Work on high-impact, policy-relevant research Gain international exposure and collaboration opportunities Build expertise in a rapidly growing field Contribute to improving healthcare systems across Africa Contact Information For informal enquiries, applicants can contact: Lesong Conteh – l.conteh@lse.ac.uk Click HERE to learn more about the PhD Studentships in African Health, Economics and Diagnostics at The London School of Economics and Political Science. Discover more global PhD Scholarship and Research opportunities on OFY . title: Regional Focal Point (APAC) at SDSN Youth 2026: Fully Remote Global Leadership Opportunity in Sustainable Development
summary: The SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point (RFP) for Asia-Pacific (APAC) is a highly impactful global leadership opportunity designed for passionate young leaders committed to advancing sustainable development. Hosted under the umbrella of the UN Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN), this role places you at the heart of international collaboration, policy engagement, and youth-driven innovation aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and the Paris Climate Agreement. This fully remote position offers an unparalleled platform to work with global stakeholders, coordinate youth-led initiatives, and contribute meaningfully to solving some of the world’s most pressing environmental, social, and economic challenges. About SDSN: A Global Network for Sustainable Solutions Established in 2012 under the auspices of the United Nations Secretary-General, SDSN mobilizes scientific and technological expertise worldwide to promote practical solutions for sustainable development. The organization plays a pivotal role in: Advancing the implementation of the SDGs Supporting climate action under the Paris Agreement Promoting integrated approaches to global challenges Facilitating knowledge exchange across countries and sectors About SDSN Youth: Empowering the Next Generation of Changemakers Launched in 2015, SDSN Youth is a flagship initiative focused on empowering young people globally to lead sustainable development efforts. Its core mission revolves around: Building youth capacity through education and training Promoting awareness of sustainable development issues Supporting youth-led innovation and advocacy Encouraging collaboration across global networks Through its extensive network of youth-led organizations, SDSN Youth has become a powerful force in mobilizing young leaders worldwide. Understanding the Youth Networks Program The Youth Networks Program is a cornerstone of SDSN Youth’s global strategy. It connects young leaders through national and regional networks, enabling them to: Develop skills and knowledge related to sustainability Collaborate with governments, NGOs, and private sector stakeholders Launch and scale impactful sustainable development initiatives Each network is coordinated locally by National Coordinators (NCs) and regionally by Regional Focal Points (RFPs), ensuring alignment with global sustainability goals. Role Overview: Regional Focal Point (APAC) As a Regional Focal Point, you will serve as the key liaison between SDSN Youth’s global leadership and regional youth networks across the Asia-Pacific region. This role requires strong leadership, communication, and coordination skills to support and guide National Coordinators effectively. Key Responsibilities Leadership and Coordination Act as the primary point of contact between SDSN Youth and regional networks Support National Coordinators in project management and strategic planning Foster collaboration and knowledge-sharing across countries Monitoring and Reporting Track and evaluate activities within regional networks Ensure alignment with SDGs and SDSN Youth objectives Report regional progress to global leadership teams Communication and Engagement Organize monthly calls with National Coordinators Participate in global and regional meetings Maintain consistent communication across time zones Strategic Development Identify growth opportunities within the region Support recruitment of new National Coordinators Promote SDSN Youth initiatives to relevant stakeholders Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply? This opportunity is specifically designed for young leaders who meet the following criteria: Age Requirement Applicants must be between 18 and 30 years old Geographic Eligibility Must be based in the Asia-Pacific (APAC) region Must remain in the region for at least 12 months after application Educational and Professional Background Experience in youth leadership, student organizations, or volunteer work Background in project coordination, event management, or stakeholder engagement is highly desirable Skills and Competencies Excellent command of the English language (written and spoken) Strong organizational and communication skills Familiarity with tools like Google Workspace Ability to work independently in a remote, multicultural environment Additional Advantages Prior involvement with SDSN or SDSN Youth networks Connections with youth organizations or sustainability initiatives Commitment and Working Conditions Duration Initial term: 12 months Includes a 3-month trial period إمكانية التمديد based on performance Time Commitment Minimum of 10 hours per week Work Setup Fully remote (global virtual environment) Requires flexibility to work across multiple time zones Compensation This is a voluntary (unpaid) position Benefits: Why You Should Apply Despite being a voluntary role, the SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point position offers substantial professional and personal development opportunities: Global Exposure Represent SDSN Youth at international events Engage with global stakeholders across sectors Skill Development Gain hands-on experience in leadership, coordination, and strategy Access training programs and capacity-building resources Career Advancement Build a strong personal brand in sustainability Enhance your resume with a globally recognized organization Networking Opportunities Collaborate with professionals from governments, NGOs, and the private sector Join a vibrant global community of changemakers Ideal Candidates: Who Should Consider This Role? This opportunity is best suited for: Young professionals passionate about sustainable development and climate action Emerging leaders seeking international exposure and impact Individuals interested in policy, advocacy, and global collaboration Students or graduates looking to build a career in development, sustainability, or international relations Application Process Candidates are encouraged to apply through official platforms such as: Indeed Resume submission LinkedIn application portal Given the competitive nature of this role and the lack of a fixed deadline, early application is highly recommended. Apply here Deadline Not explicitly specified (applications expected to be reviewed on a rolling basis; early application strongly recommended). The SDSN Youth Regional Focal Point (APAC) role is more than just a volunteer position—it is a gateway to global leadership in sustainable development. If you are driven by purpose, eager to lead, and committed to shaping a better future, this opportunity offers the perfect platform to make a meaningful impact. Take the next step in your leadership journey and become part of a global movement working toward the 2030 Agenda. title: African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Africans
summary: The African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026 is a premier pan-African capacity-building initiative designed to equip young climate leaders with the knowledge, skills, and confidence to actively participate in global climate governance. As one of the continent’s leading youth-focused climate diplomacy programs, AYNF is dedicated to strengthening African representation in international climate negotiations, particularly within the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC). Now in its fifth cohort, the fellowship continues to build a strong and inclusive pipeline of African youth leaders capable of shaping climate policies at national, regional, and global levels. The program is organized by the Green Africa Youth Organization (GAYO), in collaboration with the Environmental Protection Authority (EPA) Ghana and the Climate and Development Knowledge Network (CDKN). About the African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) The AYNF Fellowship was established to address the urgent need for increased African youth participation in climate decision-making processes. Climate negotiations are complex and require both technical expertise and strategic negotiation skills. This fellowship bridges that gap by preparing young Africans to effectively engage in these processes. Participants gain exposure to: Climate science fundamentals Global climate governance systems Negotiation frameworks and diplomacy Policy analysis and development Leadership and advocacy skills The program also creates pathways for fellows to engage in key international climate events, including Conference of the Parties (COP) engagements. Program Structure and Learning Model The AYNF Fellowship 2026 is delivered in three comprehensive phases, combining online learning, in-person engagement, and long-term mentorship. Phase 1: Foundational Online Learning All applicants must begin their journey with a mandatory learning phase through the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal. Key components include: Completion of the Climate Science 101 course Submission of a certificate of completion during application Possible additional assignments, quizzes, or courses for shortlisted candidates This phase ensures that all participants have a solid understanding of climate science and foundational concepts before advancing. Phase 2: Intensive Hybrid Training (Ghana) Selected fellows will participate in a one-week hybrid training program hosted in Ghana. This phase includes: Advanced climate negotiation training Policy writing workshops Simulation exercises replicating real-world negotiations Deep exploration of UNFCCC structures and processes Analysis of regional and global climate policy intersections This immersive experience equips fellows with practical, real-world negotiation and policy skills. Phase 3: Mentorship and COP31 Engagement The final phase focuses on applied learning, mentorship, and direct engagement in climate policy processes. Activities include: Thematic negotiation labs Development of policy briefs One-on-one mentorship and guidance Coordinated engagement before, during, and after COP31 Strategic exposure to global climate negotiation platforms This phase ensures that fellows transition from learners to active contributors in climate governance. Fellowship Objectives and Impact The AYNF Fellowship is designed to: Strengthen African youth representation in climate negotiations Build technical expertise in climate science and policy Enhance negotiation and diplomacy skills Foster leadership and advocacy among young Africans Promote inclusive participation in global climate decision-making The program contributes to shaping a new generation of African climate leaders capable of influencing policies that affect the continent and the world. Mandatory Pre-Application Requirement Before submitting an application, all candidates must complete the Climate Science 101 course via the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal. Steps to follow: Visit the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal Click on “Start Learning” Register or log in Complete the Climate Science 101 course Download and save your certificate Upload the certificate during the application process Failure to provide this certificate will result in disqualification from the application process. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the AYNF Fellowship 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a citizen of an African country Be between the ages of 15 and 35 Demonstrate strong passion for climate justice and diplomacy Have experience or interest in climate policy (national or international) Complete at least one course on the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal Be fully available for all three phases of the program Possess proficiency in English (the language of instruction) Application Process Applicants are required to: Complete the Climate Science 101 course Obtain and upload the certificate Fill out the online application form Provide accurate personal and professional information Shortlisted candidates may be required to complete additional learning tasks before final selection. Why You Should Apply The AYNF Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to gain first-hand experience in climate diplomacy while building the skills needed to influence policy and drive change. Participants benefit from high-level training, mentorship, and exposure to global climate processes, positioning them as leaders in the fight against climate change. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026 is: April 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM GMT Applicants are strongly encouraged to complete their applications early to avoid last-minute challenges. Ready to apply? Fill out the form Important Notes The program is delivered entirely in English Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Applicants must be committed to all phases of the fellowship Strong emphasis is placed on authenticity, passion, and commitment Contact Information For inquiries, applicants can reach out via email: aynf@greenafricayouth.org Click HERE for more information about the African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026. title: GIZ Kenya Jobs and Internships 2026: High-Paying Opportunities in Nairobi with the German Development Agency
summary: If you are searching for impactful career opportunities in international development, governance, or sustainability, the latest openings at GIZ Kenya present some of the most competitive and rewarding options in East Africa. Based in Nairobi , these roles span internships and technical advisory positions across critical sectors such as governance, entrepreneurship, migration, and digital trade. About GIZ Kenya Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) is a globally recognized organization that delivers international technical cooperation services. Operating in Kenya since 1964, GIZ plays a vital role in: Economic development Green energy and climate initiatives Water resource management Governance and institutional strengthening Food security Peace and security programs The organization primarily works on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), while also collaborating with private sector partners to bridge development policy and global trade. Overview of Available Opportunities All listed roles are based in Nairobi, Kenya and are currently open for application through Fuzu. 1. Intern – Strengthening Good Governance Programme Category: Internship Location: Nairobi, Kenya This internship focuses on governance systems, policy implementation, and institutional strengthening. It is ideal for candidates interested in public administration, political science, or international relations. Deadline: 13 days left 2. Junior Technical Advisor – AWE Category: Job Location: Nairobi, Kenya A mid-entry level professional role designed for candidates with technical expertise. The position supports program implementation, stakeholder coordination, and advisory services. Deadline: 12 days left 3. M E Intern – Programme for Promotion of Self-Employment and Entrepreneurship in Kenya Category: Internship Location: Nairobi, Kenya This role focuses on Monitoring Evaluation (M E), data analysis, and program impact assessment in entrepreneurship development initiatives. Deadline: 8 days left 4. Intern – Better Migration Management Program Category: Internship Location: Nairobi, Kenya A specialized internship for candidates interested in migration policy, human rights, and regional cooperation frameworks. Deadline: 6 days left Who Can Apply: Eligible Candidates These opportunities are highly competitive and generally target the following categories of applicants: 1. Educational Background Applicants are typically expected to have or be pursuing degrees in: International Relations Political Science Economics Development Studies Business Administration Communications Data Science / Statistics (for M E roles) 2. Professional Level Internships: Suitable for students, recent graduates, or early-career professionals Junior Technical Advisor: Requires prior experience (usually 1–3 years minimum) 3. Geographic Eligibility Primarily open to Kenyan nationals May also be open to East African or African applicants, depending on the role Some positions may consider international candidates, especially those already residing in Kenya Key Skills and Qualifications Required Across these roles, GIZ typically looks for candidates with: Strong analytical and research skills Excellent written and verbal communication Project management capabilities Cross-cultural competence and teamwork Interest in international development and policy Digital literacy (especially for M E and communications roles) For technical roles: Sector-specific expertise Experience with donor-funded programs Stakeholder engagement experience Why You Should Apply Working with GIZ offers unique advantages: 1. International Exposure You will work in a globally connected environment with international experts and institutions. 2. Career Growth GIZ roles provide a strong foundation for careers in: United Nations agencies NGOs Government institutions Development consulting 3. title: Rooted Futures Lab Environmental Justice & Technology Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded Remote Opportunity)
summary: The Rooted Futures Lab Environmental Justice Technology (EJIT) Fellowship 2026 is a transformative, project-based summer fellowship designed for emerging leaders working at the intersection of technology, infrastructure, and environmental justice. This highly impactful program offers a unique opportunity to contribute to real-world projects that address critical global challenges while building interdisciplinary expertise. The fellowship is structured to empower participants to independently lead projects, collaborate across disciplines, and produce tangible outputs that advance environmental justice in technological systems. About Rooted Futures Lab Rooted Futures Lab is an innovative organization committed to addressing the systemic neglect of environmental justice in technology development and application. The organization envisions a future where technological advancements actively support both human and planetary well-being, especially for underserved and marginalized communities. Through its core pillars of education, research, and action, Rooted Futures Lab works to reshape how technology is designed and deployed globally. The organization draws intellectual inspiration from environmental justice pioneer Dr. Robert Bullard and the concept of “histofuturism,” encouraging critical reflection on past injustices while co-creating equitable futures. Fellowship Overview The EJIT Fellowship is designed as a remote, flexible, and interdisciplinary experience that allows fellows to explore complex challenges at the intersection of environmental justice and technology. Key Details: Duration: June 15 – September 4, 2026 (~12 weeks) Time Commitment: Approximately 20 hours per week (flexible) Location: Fully remote Compensation: USD $3,000 stipend (flexible payment structure) Application Deadline: April 30, 2026 Application Review: Rolling basis Notification Timeline: Within 3 weeks after the application deadline Fellowship Tracks for 2026 Cohort The 2026 cohort offers four specialized fellowship tracks, each designed to address a distinct dimension of environmental justice in technology. 1. Data Centers Environmental Justice – Creative Technologist Fellow This track focuses on uncovering the hidden environmental and social costs of digital infrastructure, particularly data centers. Fellows will use computational and technical methods to investigate these impacts. Core Responsibilities: Design and execute an independent computational research project Apply techniques such as machine learning, remote sensing, or data scraping Develop datasets, models, or analytical reports Contribute to the Data Center Impact Dashboard Translate technical findings into accessible tools and visualizations Key Outputs: Research reports, datasets, or computational tools Contributions to open-source platforms and dashboards This role is ideal for individuals with technical expertise who are interested in applying data-driven approaches to environmental justice challenges. Learn more HERE. 2. Data Centers Environmental Justice – Investigative Storyteller Fellow This track emphasizes storytelling and investigative research into the socio-environmental impacts of data centers. Core Responsibilities: Conduct an independent investigation into environmental justice issues Produce storytelling outputs (written, visual, audio, or multimedia) Translate complex data into compelling narratives Contribute insights to the Data Center Impact Dashboard Focus Areas: Land displacement Water consumption Air and noise pollution Rising energy costs This role is suited for journalists, creatives, and communicators passionate about translating complex systems into accessible stories. Learn more HERE. 3. Study Hall – Democratic Governance Collective Systems Fellow The Study Hall track focuses on building community-driven systems and participatory governance models for environmental justice initiatives. Core Responsibilities: Research democratic governance systems (e.g., sociocracy) Design tools and frameworks for collective decision-making Support student-led communities in applying these systems Document processes for publications and toolkits Program Context: Previously engaged 130+ students across North America Expanding into a structured fellowship track This role is ideal for individuals interested in community organizing, education, and participatory systems design. Learn more HERE. 4. GLITCH Zine – Editor Strategist Fellow The GLITCH Zine Fellowship combines editorial leadership with strategic development of a creative publication platform. Core Responsibilities: Define editorial vision and theme for the 2026 zine Curate and edit submissions Develop outreach and contributor engagement strategies Oversee production and distribution (print and digital) Build long-term strategy for the zine platform Strategic Focus: Archiving past issues Building partnerships Expanding audience reach This role is ideal for individuals with editorial, creative, and strategic experience in publishing or storytelling. Learn more HERE. Fellowship Structure and Experience Across all tracks, fellows will: Work independently while collaborating with peers and staff Participate in weekly check-ins (especially in early stages) Receive mentorship, guidance, and feedback Engage in interdisciplinary collaboration Produce outputs that contribute to real-world impact Fellows are expected to manage their own timelines, take initiative, and navigate open-ended challenges. Support and Resources Participants will receive: Access to Rooted Futures Lab’s interdisciplinary team Technical, editorial, and strategic mentorship Collaboration opportunities with developers, researchers, and storytellers Integration into a global cohort of EJIT fellows Tools and platforms for project development and dissemination Eligibility Criteria While specific qualifications vary by track, general requirements include: Strong English communication skills Ability to work independently and manage projects Interest in environmental justice, technology, or related fields Interdisciplinary mindset and openness to collaboration Technical roles may require experience in areas such as: Machine learning, GIS, or data science Journalism, storytelling, or media production Community organizing or governance systems Editorial and publishing work Importantly, applicants from non-technical and interdisciplinary backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion Rooted Futures Lab is committed to equity and inclusion. The fellowship does not discriminate based on: Gender or gender identity Sexual orientation Race or ethnicity Religion Age or economic background Applicants from underrepresented and marginalized communities are especially encouraged to apply. Application Process To apply, candidates must submit: Personal information (name, email, location, timezone) LinkedIn profile CV (PDF or document format, max 10MB) Responses to application questions Applications are submitted via an online form. For inquiries: Email: info@rootedfutureslab.io Subject: “EJIT Fellowship Application” Learn more and apply for the Rooted Futures Lab Environmental Justice Technology (EJIT) Fellowship HERE. Important Notes for Applicants Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis Authenticity is highly valued; applicants are encouraged to use their own voice Minor grammatical errors will not affect selection Over-reliance on AI-generated responses is discouraged Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers a rare opportunity to: Work on cutting-edge environmental justice issues Gain hands-on experience in interdisciplinary projects Build a global professional network Receive financial support while contributing to meaningful work Develop outputs that influence policy, research, and community practices Discover more global Fellowship and Internship opportunities on OFY. title: Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship 2027 in Germany: €30,000 Funded Opportunity for Global Sustainability Leaders
summary: The Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship 2027 , offered by the Research Institute for Sustainability (RIFS) at the GFZ Helmholtz Centre in Potsdam, Germany, is now open for applications. This prestigious international fellowship is designed for mid-career professionals who are actively contributing to sustainable development and have the potential to drive transformative change across sectors. Named after Prof. Dr. Klaus Töpfer, a global leader in sustainability and the founding director of the Institute for Advanced Sustainability Studies (now RIFS), this fellowship supports individuals committed to bridging science, policy, society, and innovation. This opportunity provides financial support, institutional backing, and a collaborative research environment to develop impactful sustainability projects with long-term global relevance. About the Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship The Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship is part of the RIFS Fellow Programme, which focuses on interdisciplinary, transdisciplinary, and co-creative research to advance sustainability transitions. The fellowship aims to: Support innovative thinkers and practitioners working on sustainability Encourage collaboration across sectors such as science, policy, business, arts, and civil society Promote projects that generate long-term societal impact Strengthen global dialogue on sustainability transformation The fellowship will run for up to one year , beginning in the first quarter of 2027 , and requires fellows to work on-site in Potsdam, Germany. What the Fellowship Offers The programme provides comprehensive financial and institutional support to ensure fellows can focus on impactful work. Key Benefits Monthly stipend to support living expenses Additional financial compensation for relocation and living costs in Germany A discretionary project budget of up to €30,000 , which can be used for: Travel expenses Research and publication costs Organizing workshops and events Artistic or project production costs Access to a world-class research environment at RIFS Opportunities to collaborate with international experts and institutions Programme Structure and Flexibility The fellowship is designed to accommodate different professional and personal circumstances. Duration: Up to 12 months Flexible structure: Can be divided into multiple intervals if necessary Location: On-site in Potsdam, Germany Engagement: Fellows are expected to actively contribute to the institute’s research and activities Who Should Apply The fellowship is targeted at highly talented mid-career professionals with a strong background in sustainability-related work. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Have at least 10 years of professional experience in sustainability-related fields Demonstrate the potential to act as a change agent Have experience working across different countries and cultures Present an innovative project with long-term impact Applicants may come from diverse sectors, including: Science and academia Public policy and governance Civil society and NGOs Business and private sector Arts and creative industries Project Requirements Applicants must submit a well-developed project proposal that includes: Project title and objectives Research or activity focus Work plan and timeline Expected outcomes and long-term impact Required resources and budget considerations The project should demonstrate the ability to bridge different sectors and contribute to sustainability transformation. Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following documents in English: Letter of Motivation (max. 6,500 characters) Highlight experience at the intersection of science and society Explain your vision and how you discovered the fellowship Project Proposal (max. 15,000 characters + references) Detailed plan of your proposed work Curriculum Vitae (CV) (max. 10,000 characters) Include career highlights and five key achievements Two Letters of Recommendation Must include referees’ contact details Applicants must also disclose any use of AI tools in preparing their application. Application Timeline Application Deadline: April 5, 2026 (11:59 PM CEST) Selection Announcement: Autumn 2026 Fellowship Start: Early 2027 Applications must be submitted via the Official Online Application Portal. Diversity and Inclusion Commitment RIFS strongly promotes diversity and equal opportunities. Applications are encouraged from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of: Nationality Gender identity Ethnicity Religion Age Physical ability Women and underrepresented groups are especially encouraged to apply. Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Work in a globally recognized sustainability research institute Access funding and resources to implement impactful projects Collaborate with experts across disciplines and sectors Contribute to shaping global sustainability solutions Download this position as a PDF. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. How long is the fellowship? The fellowship lasts up to one year and can be structured flexibly. 2. Is the fellowship fully funded? Yes, it includes a stipend, living cost support, and a project budget of up to €30,000. 3. Where is the fellowship based? In Potsdam, Germany, at the Research Institute for Sustainability (RIFS). 4. Can the fellowship be done remotely? No, fellows are expected to be on-site during their fellowship period. 5. Who is eligible to apply? Mid-career professionals with at least 10 years of experience in sustainability-related fields. 6. What kind of projects are expected? Projects that create long-term impact and bridge different sectors of society. 7. Is there an age limit? No specific age limit is mentioned. 8. When will results be announced? Selected fellows will be announced in autumn 2026. 9. Can applicants from any country apply? Yes, the fellowship is open globally. 10. What makes this fellowship unique? Its focus on transdisciplinary collaboration and long-term sustainability impact . Learn more about the Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship 2027 HERE. title: Moral Ambition Fellowships 2026–2027: Fully Funded Global Fellowship to Transform Your Career and Tackle the World’s Biggest Problems
summary: The The School for Moral Ambition has launched one of the most transformative global opportunities for professionals seeking meaningful, high-impact careers—the Moral Ambition Fellowships. title: Journalism Fellowship 2026: Cover Workplace Mental Health with NPF in Washington, D.C.
summary: Journalists looking to deepen their reporting on workplace mental health have an opportunity to gain specialized training through the Covering Workplace Mental Health Fellowship organized by the National Press Foundation . title: Ocean Literacy Training Courses 2026 (Free Online by UNESCO) – Learn Anytime, Anywhere

title: Officially Green Leaders Fellowship 2026 in Nigeria: Fully Funded National Climate Leadership Programme for Emerging Sustainability Leaders
summary: The Officially Green Leaders Fellowship 2026 , convened by The Green Institute, is now open for applications. This prestigious, fully funded national programme is designed to identify, train, and empower one outstanding sustainability leader from each of Nigeria’s 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory (FCT). More than just a fellowship, this initiative is a national movement to institutionalize sustainability leadership , strengthen climate governance, and build a coordinated network of environmental changemakers across Nigeria. Through an intensive residential training and long-term action implementation, the programme equips fellows with the knowledge, tools, and networks needed to drive measurable environmental impact at the state level. About the Officially Green Leaders Fellowship The Officially Green Leaders Fellowship is a highly competitive programme that selects 37 exceptional individuals —one per state and the FCT—based on merit, leadership potential, and commitment to sustainability. The fellowship combines: A one-week immersive residential training Strategic collaboration and mentorship Development of actionable sustainability plans Long-term implementation of state-level climate initiatives At the end of the residency, each fellow becomes an Officially Green State Lead , responsible for executing a 12-month sustainability action blueprint in their respective state. Programme Structure and Focus Areas The fellowship is designed to provide a comprehensive and multidisciplinary learning experience across key sustainability domains. 1. Energy Transition and Green Innovation Participants will explore: Renewable and decentralized energy systems Circular economy models Sustainable enterprise development Climate finance and funding pathways 2. Climate Leadership and Governance This component focuses on: Climate science and policy frameworks Environmental governance at subnational levels Institutional engagement and policymaking processes Localization of Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) 3. State Green Action Blueprint Lab Fellows will develop: A 12-month sustainability roadmap Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and measurable targets Strategic partnership frameworks Monitoring and evaluation systems 4. Advocacy and Strategic Communication Training includes: Policy advocacy strategies Stakeholder engagement approaches Media positioning and public speaking Building influence across sectors 5. State Sustainability Cabinet Formation Each fellow will establish a State Sustainability Cabinet , comprising key leadership roles such as: Renewable Energy Lead Circular Economy Lead Climate Education Coordinator Biodiversity and Conservation Officer Community Engagement Director Research and Policy Analyst What the Fellowship Offers The programme is fully funded to ensure inclusive participation from all regions of Nigeria. Fully Funded Benefits Include: Full residential accommodation for the one-week training All meals and refreshments during the programme Transportation funding to and from the residency location This ensures that selection is based solely on merit, not financial capacity. Fellowship Outcomes and Impact Within 12 months of participation, the fellowship aims to achieve: Establishment of 37 State Sustainability Cabinets Implementation of state-level environmental initiatives Nationwide sustainability campaigns Strengthened youth-led climate advocacy Creation of a unified national sustainability ecosystem Participants will also contribute to a major national sustainability activation , positioning Nigeria as a leader in global climate action. Who Should Apply The programme is open to individuals who demonstrate: Proven commitment to sustainability or environmental action Leadership experience or strong leadership potential Ability to mobilize stakeholders at the state level Commitment to measurable, structured impact Applicants may come from: Civil society organizations Academia Public sector Private sector Grassroots initiatives Why You Should Apply The Officially Green Leaders Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Become a recognized sustainability leader in your state Gain advanced knowledge in climate policy and governance Build a network of national and global changemakers Drive real environmental impact through structured initiatives Application Timeline Application Deadline: August 26, 2026 Applications are competitive, and early submission is strongly encouraged. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the Official Fellowship Portal. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. Is the Officially Green Leaders Fellowship fully funded? Yes, the fellowship covers accommodation, meals, and transportation. 2. Who is eligible to apply? Individuals across Nigeria with a strong commitment to sustainability and leadership. 3. How many fellows will be selected? A total of 37 fellows —one from each state and the FCT. 4. What happens after the residency? Fellows implement a 12-month sustainability action plan in their state. 5. Where will the residency take place? The location will be communicated to selected fellows. 6. What does the residency focus on? Climate leadership, governance, innovation, and sustainability strategy. 7. Can more than one person apply from a state? Yes, but only one fellow will be selected per state. 8. Is there an age limit? No specific age limit is stated. 9. When is the application deadline? August 26, 2026. 10. How will applicants be notified? Selected candidates will be contacted after the review process. 11. What makes this fellowship unique? It combines leadership training with state-level implementation and national coordination , creating long-term systemic impact. Learn more about the Officially Green Leaders Fellowship 2026 HERE. title: Changemaker Fellowship with Microsoft Elevate: Empowering Nonprofits Through AI
summary: In a rapidly evolving digital world, artificial intelligence is no longer a luxury—it’s a necessity for organizations striving to scale impact and remain sustainable. The Changemaker Fellowship , launched in collaboration with Microsoft Elevate, is a transformative opportunity designed specifically for nonprofit leaders and social impact professionals ready to harness the power of AI for good. Participants will gain: Practical AI knowledge tailored for social impact One-on-one coaching from AI experts Tools to automate workflows and improve operational efficiency Strategies to responsibly integrate AI into their organization Whether your goal is to increase outreach, improve service delivery, or build sustainable funding streams, this fellowship offers the roadmap and support to get there. 💡 Key Benefits of the Fellowship 1. By equipping organizations with AI-driven tools and strategies, it empowers leaders to scale their missions, optimize operations, and create lasting change in communities around the globe. title: African Liberty Writing Fellowship 2026/2027: Paid Online Fellowship for Emerging African Writers and Thought Leaders
summary: The African Liberty Writing Fellowship 2026/2027 , powered by African Students For Liberty, is now open for applications. This highly competitive and fully online fellowship is designed to identify, train, and mentor young African writers who are passionate about public policy, governance, economics, and freedom-oriented ideas. With a structured training phase followed by a long-term writing fellowship, participants gain the opportunity to publish their work in top international media outlets, develop strong analytical writing skills, and build a global intellectual footprint. If you are an aspiring writer, student, or graduate eager to shape conversations around policy and society in Africa, this fellowship provides a powerful platform to amplify your voice. About the African Liberty Writing Fellowship The African Liberty Writing Fellowship is a flagship programme under African Students For Liberty, aimed at nurturing the next generation of African public intellectuals and policy writers. Through intensive training, mentorship, and real-world publishing opportunities, fellows are equipped with the skills needed to produce high-quality opinion articles, policy analyses, and thought leadership pieces. Fellows have previously been published in globally recognized platforms such as: Foreign Policy Al Jazeera Mail Guardian Programme Structure and Timeline The fellowship is divided into two major phases: 1. Training Phase (5 Weeks) Duration: May 25 – June 22, 2026 Intensive virtual training sessions Focus on writing, research, argumentation, and policy analysis 2. Writing Fellowship Phase Duration: July 2026 – July 2027 Long-term mentorship and writing development Continuous publication opportunities The entire programme is conducted fully online , making it accessible to participants across Africa and beyond. What the Fellowship Offers Selected fellows receive a combination of training, mentorship, and career development support: Key Benefits Paid fellowship (compensation based on performance assessments) Intensive training in opinion writing and policy analysis One-on-one mentorship from experienced editors Opportunity to publish in top international media outlets Global exposure and professional networking opportunities Development of a strong writing portfolio Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the fellowship, applicants must: Be enrolled in or have graduated from an African institution of higher learning , or Be an African studying outside the continent The fellowship is open to individuals who demonstrate: Strong interest in writing, public policy, and governance Ability to think critically and communicate ideas effectively Commitment to completing the programme Application Process Applicants must complete the Online Application form through the official platform. Important Notes No application fee required No admission fee required Only one application per candidate is allowed Selection Process The programme is highly competitive: Approximately 1,900 applications were received in the previous cycle Around 60 candidates were selected for training Only 33 fellows were finally inducted Selection is based on: Writing ability and clarity of thought Analytical and critical thinking skills Motivation and commitment Application Deadline Deadline: April 30, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early due to the competitive nature of the fellowship. APPLY HERE Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. Is this a paid fellowship? Yes, it is a paid fellowship. Compensation depends on performance during monthly assessments. 2. Is the programme online or in-person? The entire fellowship, including training and writing phases, is conducted fully online. 3. Is there an application or admission fee? No, both application and admission are completely free. 4. How competitive is the fellowship? It is highly competitive, with thousands of applicants and limited slots. 5. Can I apply if I am studying outside Africa? Yes, as long as you are an African pursuing higher education abroad. 6. When does the programme run? Training: May–June 2026 Fellowship: July 2026 – July 2027 7. What kind of writing will I do? Fellows produce opinion articles, policy analyses, and thought leadership content. 8. Do I need prior publication experience? Not necessarily, but strong writing skills are essential. 9. Who organizes the fellowship? The programme is organized by African Students For Liberty. 10. Who can I contact for inquiries? You can contact the Editor-in-Chief via email provided on the official platform. Why You Should Apply The African Liberty Writing Fellowship is more than just a training programme—it is a career-launching opportunity for aspiring African writers and thinkers. By participating, you will: Gain world-class writing and analytical skills Build a strong portfolio of published work Join a network of influential writers and policy thinkers Contribute to shaping conversations on Africa’s future Learn more about the African Liberty Writing Fellowship 2026/2027 HERE. title: Apply Now: National Service Trainee (Community Development and CSR) – Accra, Ghana
summary: he National Service with Operations (Community Development Programme) offers an exciting opportunity for graduates in Ghana to gain practical, hands-on experience in community development and digital skills training. Based in Accra within the Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) department, this role is designed to equip National Service personnel with essential professional skills while contributing to impactful initiatives that promote digital inclusion among young people. The trainee will play a key role in supporting programmes such as Coding for Kids (C4K), coding competitions, mentorship initiatives, and broader community outreach efforts. This position blends programme management, stakeholder engagement, monitoring and evaluation, and communications—making it ideal for individuals seeking a well-rounded early-career experience. Role Details Location: Accra, Ghana Department: Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Working Hours: 40 hours per week Job Description As a National Service Trainee, you will support the planning, coordination, implementation, and monitoring of community-based digital skills programmes. You will collaborate with schools, volunteers, partners, and internal teams to ensure successful delivery and measurable impact. This role provides a unique opportunity to build experience in programme coordination, communications, stakeholder engagement, and event management, all while contributing to meaningful social impact initiatives. Key Responsibilities Programme Coordination Implementation You will assist in organizing and executing community development initiatives aimed at improving digital literacy and skills among young learners. This includes coordinating with partner schools, managing programme schedules, and supporting logistics for events such as coding competitions, exhibitions, bootcamps, and outreach programmes. Monitoring, Evaluation Reporting The role involves collecting and compiling programme data, including attendance records, assessments, and participant feedback. You will support the preparation of reports and impact summaries, track key performance indicators (KPIs), and document success stories that highlight programme achievements. Stakeholder Partner Engagement You will help maintain strong relationships with schools, teachers, parents, volunteers, and partners. Responsibilities include assisting with onboarding processes, preparing presentations and partnership materials, and supporting outreach to potential donors and sponsors. Communications Content Support The trainee will contribute to communication efforts by drafting social media content, documenting programme activities through photos and reports, and supporting newsletters and CSR updates. This helps amplify the impact and visibility of the programmes. Administrative Support You will assist with documentation management, meeting coordination, and logistical arrangements for field visits and programme activities. This ensures smooth day-to-day operations and effective team collaboration. Qualifications To be eligible for this opportunity, applicants must: Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Education, Social Sciences, Business Administration, or a related field Be eligible to undertake National Service in Ghana Preferred Skills (Nice to Have) While not mandatory, the following skills will strengthen your application: Strong organizational and coordination abilities Excellent written and verbal communication skills Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Basic knowledge of digital tools and programming (e.g., Scratch, HTML, Python) Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines Strong interpersonal and stakeholder engagement skills Passion for youth development and digital inclusion Perks and Benefits Participants in this programme will enjoy a range of benefits designed to support both professional and personal growth: Competitive monthly allowance Provision of a workstation (laptop, external monitor, etc.) End-of-year package Participation in AmaliTechies bonding activities, including bi-monthly social events and sports activities Additional Information AmaliTech is committed to inclusivity and encourages applications from Persons with Disabilities (PWDs). Applicants who require assistance during the application process are encouraged to contact the HR team via email at recruitment@amalitech.com , providing details on the support needed. Recruitment Process The selection process is structured to assess both technical and practical capabilities: General application (click “I’m Interested” to apply) Assessment (Simulation Project) Online interview(s) Job offer How to Apply Interested and qualified applicants should follow the application process by clicking the “Apply Now” button and selecting “I’m Interested.” Required Documents Ensure you prepare the following documents before applying: Latest CV (PDF format) Academic and professional certificates CLICK HERE TO APPLY What to Expect Joining AmaliTech offers a supportive and diverse work environment where innovation and collaboration are encouraged. As a National Service Trainee, you will gain valuable exposure to real-world projects, work alongside a talented and welcoming team, and develop skills that can launch your career in the global IT and development space. This opportunity is ideal for graduates looking to combine technical knowledge with social impact, while building a strong foundation for future career growth. title: Apply for OceanX Ocean Futures Fellowship 2026 (Paid Remote Program for Ocean Professionals)
summary: The Ocean Futures Fellowship, offered by OceanX , is a forward-looking professional development program designed to equip emerging ocean professionals with the skills, experience, and global exposure needed to address some of the most urgent challenges facing the world’s oceans. This six-month, full-time fellowship provides a unique combination of technical training, mentorship, and hands-on project work, all delivered in a fully remote format. At its core, the fellowship aims to build sustainable career pathways in ocean-related fields while fostering a new generation of leaders committed to ocean conservation, innovation, and public engagement. Participants benefit from OceanX’s extensive international network, gaining access to interdisciplinary collaborations that span science, media, and technology. The program is closely aligned with global sustainability priorities, contributing to the objectives of the United Nations ’s Ocean Decade and Sustainable Development Goal 14 (Life Below Water). Through this alignment, the fellowship emphasizes the importance of ocean literacy, storytelling, and scientific advancement in protecting marine ecosystems. Fellows also receive a monthly stipend and may have the rare opportunity to travel aboard the OceanXplorer , OceanX’s state-of-the-art research and media vessel, depending on program needs and individual performance. Fellowship Focus Areas The Ocean Futures Fellowship offers six specialized tracks, each tailored to different professional interests and skill sets. Applicants are expected to demonstrate experience or strong interest in at least one area, alongside a working knowledge of artificial intelligence (AI) and responsible use of emerging technologies. 1. AI Fellow AI Fellows work at the intersection of technology and ocean science. They design and implement multimodal AI systems in collaboration with OceanX teams to accelerate scientific insights and enhance storytelling. This role is ideal for individuals interested in applying machine learning, data science, and AI tools to real-world environmental challenges. 2. Digital Correspondent Fellow Digital Correspondent Fellows focus on communicating ocean science and conservation through compelling storytelling. Using journalism and digital platforms, they translate complex scientific findings into engaging narratives that can reach global audiences. 3. Digital Asset Archives Fellow This role supports the organization and management of OceanX’s extensive media archive. Fellows help maintain, catalog, and improve access to a growing collection of scientific and production assets, ensuring that valuable content is preserved and easily retrievable. 4. Immersive Media Fellow Immersive Media Fellows explore cutting-edge technologies such as virtual reality (VR), augmented reality (AR), game design, and user experience (UX/UI). Their goal is to create interactive experiences that bring ocean exploration and education to life for diverse audiences. 5. Marketing and Communications Fellow This track provides experience in planning and executing strategic communication campaigns. Fellows contribute to content creation, digital outreach, and cross-channel marketing initiatives while collaborating with various teams across the organization. 6. Partnerships Fellow Partnerships Fellows play a key role in building and maintaining relationships with stakeholders. This includes identifying potential collaborators, supporting ongoing partnerships, and contributing to strategic growth initiatives. Program Timeline, Structure, and Location Key Dates March 24, 2026 – Applications open May 3, 2026 (11:59 PM ET) – Application deadline Mid-May 2026 – Candidate interviews Late May 2026 – Fellowship offers announced June 22, 2026 – Fellowship begins with onboarding December 2026 – Fellowship concludes Remote Format and Work Expectations The fellowship is conducted entirely remotely, allowing participants to work from their home countries. Despite its remote structure, the program is highly interactive and requires active participation in virtual training sessions, mentorship meetings, and collaborative projects. Fellows are expected to align their working hours with Singapore Time (SGT) to facilitate coordination with OceanX teams. However, candidates in the Marketing and Communications track may have the flexibility to operate within either Singapore or New York (Eastern Time) business hours, depending on their location. Financial Support and Benefits Fellows receive a monthly stipend intended to support their full-time engagement in the program. It is important to note that this stipend does not cover additional expenses such as housing, relocation, transportation, or dependent care. In certain cases, a technology stipend may be provided to support specific needs related to the fellowship. Additionally, the program offers intangible benefits including mentorship, global networking opportunities, and exposure to real-world ocean exploration initiatives. Why the Ocean Futures Fellowship Matters The Ocean Futures Fellowship stands out as a multidisciplinary program that bridges science, technology, storytelling, and policy. By combining professional training with real-world application, it prepares participants to become influential voices in ocean conservation and innovation. Through its alignment with global initiatives and its emphasis on emerging technologies such as AI and immersive media, the fellowship reflects the evolving nature of ocean careers. It not only builds technical expertise but also cultivates leadership, creativity, and a strong sense of global responsibility. For individuals passionate about the future of the oceans and eager to contribute to meaningful change, this fellowship offers a transformative opportunity to develop skills, expand networks, and engage with one of the most dynamic fields in the world today. Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the Ocean Futures Fellowship, applicants must meet several requirements: Be at least 21 years old Be authorized to work in their country of residence Be available to commit full-time from June to December 2026 Hold a bachelor’s degree from an accredited institution before the fellowship begins Demonstrate experience or strong interest in one of the six fellowship tracks Possess full professional proficiency in English (C2 level or equivalent) Have practical knowledge of AI literacy and a commitment to using emerging technologies responsibly Show strong motivation for purpose-driven work, mentorship, and global collaboration Additional academic requirements apply to specific tracks: Partnerships Fellows should have backgrounds in fields such as finance, policy, science, nonprofit management, business, or communications Immersive Media and Digital Correspondent Fellows should have academic training in ocean science, conservation, media, or related disciplines Application Process Applicants must complete an online application that includes multiple components designed to assess both technical qualifications and personal motivation. These include: General background information and work experience related to ocean education or relevant fields A current resume or CV Written responses to short essay questions A short video explaining the applicant’s qualifications and reasons for applying Before starting the application, candidates should prepare the following: A link to their resume/CV (required) A link to their video response (required) Optional links to sample projects and proof of English proficiency CLICK HERE TO APPLY Discover more similar opportunities here. title: Apply Now: Nova Media Fellowship 2026–2027 ($60K for Journalists and Newsrooms)
summary: The Nova Media Fellowship 2026–2027 is a prestigious, fully funded program designed to support exceptional journalists and nonprofit newsrooms reporting on the complex, interconnected factors that shape health and well-being. This fellowship empowers storytellers to produce impactful, evidence-based, and solution-focused journalism that advances public understanding and inspires meaningful change. Each year, Nova selects a small cohort of Media Fellows whose work explores pressing global issues related to human flourishing. Past fellows have published in leading outlets such as The New York Times, The Guardian, Science, Bloomberg, NPR, and Grist , among others—demonstrating the fellowship’s strong reputation and reach. About the Nova Media Fellowship The Nova Media Fellowship provides selected journalists with the time, funding, and editorial freedom needed to pursue ambitious reporting projects. Fellows maintain full editorial independence , allowing them to define their storytelling approach, angles, and dissemination strategies. The program emphasizes: Evidence-based journalism Solution-oriented storytelling Broad audience engagement across sectors (science, policy, communities, and the public) For the 2026–2027 cohort , Nova will select three outstanding fellows . Fellowship Benefits Selected Media Fellows will receive: $60,000 grant distributed over 12 months Additional travel and conference stipends Flexible support to explore in-depth and evolving storylines Opportunities to reach global audiences and decision-makers Fellowship Duration: September 1, 2026 – August 31, 2027 Why Apply? The Nova Media Fellowship is more than just funding—it’s an opportunity to: Produce high-impact journalism that influences global conversations Explore complex health challenges through innovative lenses Contribute to a future where all people can thrive and flourish If you are a journalist passionate about uncovering solutions and telling stories that matter, this fellowship offers the platform, resources, and freedom to bring your ideas to life. Eligibility Criteria To apply for the Nova Media Fellowship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Who Can Apply Professional journalists with a proven track record of publication (local, regional, or national) Nonprofit newsrooms Student-led newsrooms affiliated with educational institutions Key Requirements Applicants must reside in the United States throughout the fellowship period Projects may include international or global reporting Not Eligible Book proposals Poetry, songwriting, or artwork Academic research projects Nova strongly encourages applications from individuals and communities historically underrepresented in media, including: Women People of color LGBTQ+ individuals Indigenous and Native communities Fellowship Focus Areas (2026–2027) Nova is particularly interested in proposals addressing innovative and emerging themes, including: 1. Mental Health and Justice Legal systems and the “legalome” Compassionate and equitable justice frameworks 3. Planetary Health and Collective Well-Being Climate, ecology, and interconnected global systems Practices that foster connection with the natural world 4. AI, Technology, and Society Impacts of artificial intelligence on healthcare and mental health Broader societal implications of emerging technologies 5. Health System Innovation New models of primary healthcare Addressing systemic inequities 6. Food Systems and Regenerative Agriculture Sustainable agriculture practices Transformative food systems Note: These themes are not exhaustive. Nova welcomes creative, interdisciplinary proposals that explore new ways of understanding health and human flourishing. What Makes a Strong Application? Successful proposals should: Go beyond identifying problems to highlight innovative solutions Demonstrate a clear reporting plan and timeline Include a strategy for reaching broad audiences Provide a publication and dissemination roadmap , including: Target outlets Social media strategies Newsletters (e.g., Substack) Speaking engagements Applicants are also encouraged to include: A travel budget Existing editorial partnerships or contacts A realistic set of deliverables for a one-year project Application Requirements For Freelance Journalists: Written project proposal Résumé Relevant work samples For Newsrooms: Application completed by a senior representative (e.g., managing editor) Organizational details including: Structure Editorial focus Available resources Finalists: Must submit two professional letters of recommendation Participate in virtual panel interviews CLICK HERE TO REGISTER Application Timeline Applications Open: Friday, March 20, 2026 Info Session (Zoom): Thursday, April 2, 2026 (4:00 PM ET) Application Deadline: Monday, April 20, 2026 (11:59 PM ET) Semi-finalist Interviews: Mid-May 2026 Finalist Interviews: Mid-June 2026 Fellowship Awards Announced: Early July 2026 Program Start Date: September 1, 2026 Note: Dates (except the deadline) may be subject to change. Additional Requirements and Expectations Fellows must credit Nova’s support in all published work related to the fellowship Nova affiliation should be included in bios and bylines , even for unrelated work during the fellowship term Fellows are encouraged to embrace curiosity-driven exploration , following new leads and evolving narratives Discover more similar opportunities here.
