title: Apply Now: Head of Justice and Violence Prevention Programme at the Institute for Security Studies
summary: The Head of Justice and Violence Prevention Programme role at the Institute for Security Studies is a senior leadership opportunity based in Pretoria . This position offers experienced professionals the chance to lead impactful research and policy initiatives aimed at reducing violence, strengthening justice systems, and improving governance across Africa. This is not an entry-level role or internship—it is a high-level, impact-driven career opportunity suited for individuals with extensive experience in criminal justice, public policy, or violence prevention who want to shape real-world outcomes at national and regional levels. About the Opportunity The Institute for Security Studies (ISS) is a leading African organization dedicated to advancing human security through research, policy advice, and capacity building. The Justice and Violence Prevention Programme is one of its core initiatives, focusing on improving criminal justice systems, promoting accountability, and developing evidence-based strategies to reduce violence. In this leadership role, you will oversee the strategic direction, research agenda, and implementation of projects within the programme. This includes working closely with governments, civil society organizations, and international partners to develop policies and interventions that address crime, corruption, and public safety challenges. You will also lead a multidisciplinary team of researchers and experts, ensuring that the programme produces high-quality research, policy recommendations, and practical solutions that contribute to safer and more just societies. Why This Opportunity Matters Violence, crime, and weak justice systems remain major challenges across many regions, affecting development, stability, and quality of life. Addressing these issues requires evidence-based policies, strong institutions, and effective collaboration between stakeholders. The Institute for Security Studies plays a critical role in this space by providing independent research, policy advice, and technical support to governments and organizations. As Head of the Justice and Violence Prevention Programme, you will be at the forefront of efforts to: Improve criminal justice systems and accountability Promote evidence-based violence prevention strategies Strengthen governance and public safety Support policy development and reform This role is particularly impactful because it bridges research and real-world implementation, ensuring that knowledge translates into meaningful change. What You’ll Gain This role offers significant professional and impact-driven benefits: Leadership at a Respected Institution – Lead a key programme within one of Africa’s top policy research organizations Regional and Global Influence – Shape policies and strategies that impact justice systems and violence prevention across the continent Strategic Career Growth – Gain senior-level experience in research leadership, programme management, and policy engagement Collaborative Networks – Work with governments, international organizations, and civil society actors Meaningful Impact – Contribute directly to reducing violence, improving governance, and strengthening justice systems Exposure to High-Level Policy Work – Engage in policy dialogues, advisory processes, and capacity-building initiatives This is an ideal opportunity for professionals looking to combine leadership with meaningful social impact at scale. Eligibility While specific requirements may vary, ideal candidates for this role typically have: Extensive experience in criminal justice, public policy, or violence prevention A strong background in research, programme management, or policy analysis Proven leadership experience managing teams and complex projects Expertise in governance, security, or justice systems , particularly in African contexts Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills Advanced academic qualifications (often at Master’s or PhD level) Experience working with governments, NGOs, or international organizations is highly desirable. Application Process and Deadline The application process typically involves: Submitting a detailed CV and cover letter Demonstrating relevant leadership and professional experience Providing supporting documents as required Shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews and further assessments. To learn more about the process and see the full guidelines to apply please review the official ISS website . title: Mila AI Policy Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Global Opportunity for AI Policy Leaders, Researchers, and Professionals (Earn While Shaping the Future of Artificial Intelligence Governance)
summary: The Mila AI Policy Fellowship is one of the most forward-thinking international fellowship programs designed to bridge the critical gap between artificial intelligence (AI) research and policymaking. Hosted by Mila – Quebec Artificial Intelligence Institute, this program is tailored for professionals and researchers who want to influence how AI is governed globally. As artificial intelligence rapidly transforms industries, economies, and societies, policymakers often struggle to keep pace with technical advancements. This fellowship directly addresses that challenge by empowering participants to produce evidence-based, real-world policy solutions that shape the future of AI governance. What Is the Mila AI Policy Fellowship? The Mila AI Policy Fellowship is a six-month, part-time, fully funded international fellowship that brings together interdisciplinary experts to collaborate on AI policy challenges. Participants (known as fellows) work closely with Mila researchers and advisors to: Develop impactful policy briefs Organize policy roundtables and stakeholder events Contribute to global AI governance discussions Strengthen their ability to translate research into policy action The fellowship emphasizes a socio-technical approach, combining insights from AI with disciplines such as law, sociology, economics, ethics, and public policy. Fellowship Duration, Structure and Commitment Duration: 6 months (typically September to February) Time Commitment: 10–15 hours per week (part-time) Format: Hybrid (virtual + optional in-person activities in Montreal, Canada) Compensation: Approximately CAD 40/hour (up to 15 hours per week) This structure makes it ideal for professionals who want to participate without leaving their current jobs or academic roles. Key Benefits of the Mila AI Policy Fellowship 1. Financial Support Fellows are paid hourly for their contributions Travel costs for in-person components are covered (up to 14 days) 2. Global Exposure Engage with international policymakers, researchers, and stakeholders Participate in high-level discussions on AI governance 3. Mentorship and Collaboration Work directly with Mila AI Advisors and experts Gain access to one of the world’s leading AI research ecosystems 4. Career Advancement Build a strong portfolio in AI policy Publish policy briefs and research outputs Fellowship Thematic Areas (Areas of Research Focus) Applicants must align their project with one or more of the fellowship’s thematic areas: • AI Safety and Governance Focuses on regulating AI systems responsibly and mitigating risks. • AI Adoption and Productivity Explores how AI can be adopted equitably across sectors. • AI, Media, and Democracy Addresses issues like misinformation, deepfakes, and AI in journalism. • Substantive Equality in AI Examines fairness, inclusion, and bias in AI systems. • AI and Climate Looks at AI’s environmental impact and its role in sustainability. • Indigenous AI Focuses on data sovereignty, rights, and inclusion of Indigenous communities. title: Wangari Maathai Fellowship 2026 in Tanzania: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Changemakers
summary: The global climate movement continues to rely on bold, innovative, and community-driven leaders—and the Wangari Maathai Fellowship (WMF) Cohort II – 2026 is designed to nurture exactly that. Organized by The Climate Hub Tanzania with the support of Climate-KIC , the Embassy of Ireland in Tanzania , and the Segal Family Foundation , this fellowship empowers young Tanzanian leaders to take meaningful climate action. Named after the iconic Wangari Maathai , the first African woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize and founder of the Green Belt Movement, this fellowship carries forward her legacy of environmental stewardship, grassroots activism, and sustainable development. Why the Wangari Maathai Fellowship Matters Africa is among the regions most affected by climate change, yet it is also home to some of the most innovative grassroots solutions. However, many young leaders face barriers such as: Limited access to funding Lack of mentorship and structured guidance Insufficient exposure to policy engagement The Wangari Maathai Fellowship directly addresses these gaps by combining financial support, mentorship, and capacity building , enabling young leaders to implement impactful, community-based climate solutions. About the 2026 Fellowship Cohort Building on the success of its first cohort in 2025, the 2026 program will: Select 15 fellows from across Tanzania Run for 8 months (April–November 2026) Operate in a hybrid format (in-person and virtual) Provide seed funding and mentorship for real-world climate projects This fellowship is not just a training program—it is a launchpad for climate leadership and innovation . What Fellows Will Receive Financial Support Each selected fellow will receive: A €500 sub-grant to implement their climate project This funding is specifically designed to help fellows turn their ideas into tangible impact within their communities. Capacity Building and Training Participants will benefit from: Structured learning sessions on climate policy and advocacy Training in project design and implementation Personal and professional development workshops Exposure to real-world climate challenges and solutions Mentorship and Networking Fellows will gain access to: Personalized mentorship from climate experts Networking opportunities with policymakers and practitioners Participation in climate-related events and discussions This creates a strong ecosystem of support, collaboration, and growth. Hands-On Project Implementation A key component of the fellowship is the Climate Action Impact Project , where fellows will: Design and implement solutions in areas such as: Sustainable agriculture Clean energy Reforestation Water conservation Community advocacy This ensures that learning is translated into real, measurable impact . Post-Fellowship Opportunities The fellowship goes beyond the 8-month program: Top 3 projects will receive extended support Opportunities for scaling and partnerships Potential participation in regional and international climate conferences Increased visibility for fellows and their work Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates must: Be 18 to 30 years old Be fluent in English and Kiswahili (spoken and written) Be based in Tanzania at the time of application Have completed some level of formal education Demonstrate active engagement in climate or environmental initiatives Present a feasible and impactful climate project idea Applicants are expected to show passion, leadership, and a commitment to community-driven solutions. Commitment to Inclusion and Diversity The The Climate Hub Tanzania strongly promotes Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI). Applications are highly encouraged from: Women Persons with disabilities Individuals from diverse and underrepresented backgrounds This ensures that climate leadership reflects the diversity of communities affected by environmental challenges. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Wangari Maathai Fellowship 2026 is: 5 April 2026 Late applications may not be considered, so early submission is strongly recommended. Final Thoughts The Wangari Maathai Fellowship is more than a program—it is a movement to empower the next generation of climate leaders in Tanzania . If you have a vision for environmental change and a project that can make a difference, this fellowship provides the tools, support, and platform to bring that vision to life. title: Top UNDP Career Opportunities 2026: Senior-Level Global Jobs in New York, Berlin, and Bonn

title: Fully Funded UCL & V&A PhD Studentship 2026: Study Migrant Labour and British Craft in the 18th Century
summary: Are you passionate about history, museums, and uncovering hidden narratives? A prestigious fully funded PhD studentship is now open for applications through University College London (UCL) and the Victoria and Albert Museum (V A) under the AHRC Collaborative Doctoral Partnership (CDP) scheme. This unique opportunity allows doctoral researchers to explore the untold contributions of migrant labour to British craft and design during the long eighteenth century, while gaining hands-on experience with one of the world’s leading museums. 📅 Key Details Start Date: 1 October 2026 Application Deadline: 15 April 2026 (23:59 UK time) Interview Date: 15 May 2026 (online) Location: London (UCL + V A, hybrid engagement) Duration: 4 years full-time (or part-time equivalent) 🎓 About the PhD Project The project, titled “Invisible Hands: Migrant Labour and British Craft in the Long Eighteenth Century,” investigates how migrant artisans shaped Britain’s material culture between 1688 and 1837. Despite the recognition of some groups like Huguenot silk weavers, many migrant contributors remain hidden in archives. This research aims to uncover those overlooked stories using a combination of: Historical research Museum and archival analysis Digital humanities techniques You will work with extensive collections including furniture, textiles, ceramics, and metalwork, while exploring archival materials such as apprenticeship records, guild registers, and court documents. 🔍 Research Focus Areas Possible research questions include: How can migrant craftspeople be identified in historical archives? What patterns of labour, mobility, and collaboration existed? How visible are migrant contributions in museum collections? How can digital tools help uncover hidden histories? The project is flexible, allowing you to tailor your research focus based on your academic interests. 🏛️ What Makes This Opportunity Unique? This is a collaborative PhD between a top global university and a world-renowned museum. As a student, you will: Work closely with expert supervisors from both institutions Gain hands-on experience with museum collections and archives Develop skills in cataloguing, curatorial practice, and public engagement Join a national network of CDP-funded researchers 💰 Funding and Benefits This fully funded studentship includes: Full tuition fee coverage (including international fee waiver) Annual stipend of £21,805 + £2,000 London weighting Additional £600 yearly maintenance payment Up to £1,250 for travel and research expenses Access to professional development and networking opportunities 📚 Eligibility Criteria Applicants should: Hold (or expect) a Master’s degree in a relevant field such as History, Art History, Museum Studies, Digital Humanities, Anthropology, or related disciplines Demonstrate interest in the museum and heritage sector Show potential for research and interdisciplinary collaboration Both home and international applicants are eligible, with international students required to reside in the UK during the program. 📝 Application Process To apply: Submit your PhD application through the UCL online admissions system Include the project title and supervisor names Prepare the following documents: CV (max 2 pages) Research statement (max 1 page) Cover letter (max 2 pages) Optional writing sample There is no application fee. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . title: APNIC Foundation 2026 Research Fellowship: $10,000 Grant for Digital Innovation in Asia Pacific
summary: The APNIC Foundation has officially opened applications for its 2026 Research Fellowship , a prestigious program designed to support high-impact, evidence-based research addressing critical digital challenges across the Asia Pacific region. Following the success of its inaugural cohort, the 2026 edition expands its commitment to empowering researchers, technical experts, and policy professionals to shape the future of digital infrastructure, artificial intelligence, and inclusive technology systems. This fellowship is not just about research—it is about influencing real-world policy, strengthening digital resilience, and ensuring that technological advancement benefits all communities across the region. 🌏 Why This Fellowship Matters in 2026 The Asia Pacific region is undergoing rapid digital transformation. However, this progress is uneven. While some economies are advancing in artificial intelligence and digital systems, many communities still lack reliable Internet access and inclusive infrastructure. This imbalance creates serious risks, including: Widening socio-economic inequality Fragile and vulnerable Internet infrastructure Ethical and sustainability concerns around AI Fragmented digital systems lacking interoperability The APNIC Foundation Research Fellowship directly addresses these challenges by supporting locally grounded, policy-relevant research that bridges the gap between technical expertise and real-world implementation. 💡 Fellowship Overview The 2026 APNIC Foundation Research Fellowship offers: 💰 USD 10,000 stipend for each selected fellow 👩‍🔬 5 fellowship positions available 🗓️ Duration: 8 months (April – November 2026) 🌍 Format: Fully remote (global collaboration, local impact) Fellows will conduct independent research projects that contribute to strengthening digital ecosystems and informing policy decisions across the Asia Pacific. 🔍 Research Focus Areas (Thematic Tracks) Applicants must align their proposals with one of the following three tracks: 1. Internet Infrastructure and Resilience This track focuses on the backbone of digital connectivity, addressing vulnerabilities in network systems and ensuring long-term stability. Key topics include: Internet routing security Regional traffic measurement Subsea cable resilience Network reliability under geopolitical pressure 2. Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) This track explores systems that enable essential services such as digital identity, payments, and data exchange. Key topics include: Interoperability of digital systems Cross-border infrastructure Financial inclusion and last-mile connectivity Bridging the digital divide 3. Sustainable AI This track tackles the environmental and operational challenges of artificial intelligence systems. Key topics include: Energy efficiency of AI systems Environmental impact of data centres Sustainable compute infrastructure AI solutions for environmental challenges 🧠 What Fellows Will Do Selected fellows will: Conduct original, high-quality research Develop policy-relevant insights and recommendations Produce outputs such as: Research papers Measurement tools Proofs of concept Strategic frameworks The APNIC Foundation may publish or integrate these outputs into broader regional initiatives, amplifying their impact. 👩‍💼 Who Should Apply? The fellowship is open to experienced professionals across three key profiles: Researchers Experts in data science, economics, or social sciences Skilled in quantitative modeling and impact analysis Able to translate complex data into policy insights Technical Practitioners Engineers, system architects, infrastructure specialists Experience in real-world digital system deployment Ability to develop technical frameworks and solutions Policy Professionals Consultants, legal experts, government advisors Skilled in policy drafting and strategic planning Ability to connect technical research with national development goals ✅ Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Reside in one of the 56 Asia Pacific economies served by the APNIC Foundation Have 7–10 years of professional experience Hold at least a Master’s degree in a relevant field Have at least one published research paper (first author) Be willing to publish outputs under a Creative Commons (CC BY 4.0) license 📅 Key Dates Applications Open: 10 March 2026 Application Deadline: 31 March 2026 (11:59 PM UTC+10) Selection Announcement: 15 April 2026 Fellowship Period: 15 April – 30 November 2026 🌐 How to Apply Interested applicants should visit the official APNIC Foundation Fellowships page to: Access detailed guidelines Review eligibility requirements Submit their application Make sure your proposal is aligned with one of the thematic tracks and clearly demonstrates both technical depth and policy relevance. title: Junior Economist and Modeler at the OECD (Paris, France): Launch Your Career in Global Economic Policy
summary: The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is offering an exceptional opportunity for early-career economists to join its prestigious Economics Department as a Junior Economist and Modeler in Paris, France. This full-time position places you at the heart of global economic policymaking, working alongside leading experts to shape policies that impact economies worldwide. If you are passionate about economic research, data-driven policy analysis, and international collaboration, this role offers a unique platform to grow your career in one of the most influential international organizations. About the OECD and Its Mission The OECD is an international organization composed of 38 member countries, dedicated to building better policies for better lives. It works closely with governments, policymakers, and stakeholders to promote economic growth, social inclusion, and environmental sustainability. Through data analysis, research, and international collaboration, the OECD addresses some of the world’s most pressing challenges, including job creation, education reform, economic resilience, and global tax systems. About the Economics Department (ECO) The Economics Department plays a central role in shaping OECD’s policy advice. It focuses on: Macroeconomic analysis Structural policy development Country-specific and cross-country research Evidence-based policymaking Its work contributes directly to major OECD publications such as Economic Outlook, Economic Surveys, and policy reports that influence decision-makers worldwide. Role Overview: Junior Economist and Modeler As a Junior Economist and Modeler, you will join the Structural Policy and Research Division (ECO/SPRD). You will contribute to cutting-edge research on macroeconomic and structural models, helping generate insights that guide economic policies across countries. You will work under the supervision of a Senior Economist and collaborate with a wide range of stakeholders, including: Governments and central banks International organizations Research institutions and think tanks Academic experts Key Responsibilities 1. Modelling and Policy Analysis Contribute to the development of macroeconomic and structural models Conduct quantitative analyses tailored to specific countries Support policy recommendations based on empirical research Assist in coordinating modelling work within the division Provide technical guidance to research assistants and trainees 2. Research and Drafting Draft analytical papers and reports for OECD committees and publications Summarize findings from economic models and research Contribute to flagship OECD outputs such as: Economic Surveys Working Papers Economic Outlook Policy Papers 3. Data Collection and Management Gather and compile economic data Ensure data quality, consistency, and comparability Build and manage databases for research purposes 4. Liaison and Outreach Build professional networks with policymakers and researchers Engage with international stakeholders Support workshops and collaborative initiatives 5. Additional Contributions Assist in organizing events and workshops Proofread and review analytical documents Perform other related tasks as required Ideal Candidate Profile Academic Requirements Advanced university degree in Economics A PhD is considered an advantage Professional Experience 1–2 years of experience in: Economic statistics Econometrics (time-series and panel data) Applied economic modeling Experience in research, internships, or analytical roles is highly valued Technical Skills Strong knowledge of econometric techniques Ability to manage large datasets Programming skills in: Python (essential) MATLAB (essential) R and Stata (advantageous) Core Competencies Strong communication and teamwork skills Analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities Capacity to innovate and adapt in a dynamic environment Language Requirements Fluency in English or French Working knowledge of the other language (or willingness to learn) Additional languages are a plus Salary and Benefits Starting monthly salary: 6,008.38 EUR (tax-exempt in France) Additional allowances depending on eligibility Opportunity to work in a highly international environment Access to global research networks and high-level policy discussions Possibility of missions and international exposure Contract Details Multiple positions available Contract type determined based on organizational needs Possibility of appointment at a lower grade depending on experience Selection Process Shortlisted candidates may go through: Job-based assessments Panel interviews The OECD promotes diversity and equal opportunity and encourages applications from all qualified candidates from member countries. 📅 Application Deadline Applications must be submitted no later than 7 April 2026 (midnight CEST) . Early applications are strongly encouraged as the position may be filled before the deadline. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through the official OECD careers portal. Make sure your application includes: Updated CV Motivation letter Relevant academic and professional references For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Junior Economist and Modeler at the OECD (Paris, France): Launch Your Career in Global Economic Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Peace Corps Uganda Volunteer Opportunities 2026: Community Health, Economic Empowerment & Education Roles (Fully Funded Service Program)
summary: Are you passionate about making a real, lasting impact in communities while gaining global experience? The Peace Corps Uganda Volunteer Program 2026 offers three powerful service opportunities for individuals committed to public health, education, and economic development. These fully supported international service roles— Community Health Promoter , Economic Empowerment Advisor , and School Enrichment Facilitator —provide hands-on experience, cultural immersion, and long-term career benefits while working directly with communities in Uganda. All three roles share a common mission: empowering communities through sustainable development, education, and capacity-building initiatives. Overview of Peace Corps Uganda Volunteer Program The Peace Corps is a globally recognized U.S. government initiative focused on promoting world peace, friendship, and sustainable development. Volunteers serve in communities around the world, working alongside local partners to address pressing challenges. Key Program Details (All Roles) Location: Uganda, Africa Application Deadline: April 15, 2026 Departure Date: November 4, 2026 Duration: 2 years + 3 months training Decision Notification: June 1, 2026 Shared Benefits Across All Positions All selected volunteers will receive a comprehensive benefits package, including: Financial Logistical Support Travel to and from Uganda Monthly living allowance Housing provided Medical and dental coverage Professional Development Extensive language, technical, and intercultural training Hands-on international work experience Competitive advantage for federal jobs Post-Service Benefits Approximately $10,800 service award upon completion Access to graduate school benefits and career pathways 1. Community Health Promoter (Uganda) This role focuses on improving health outcomes through community education and prevention strategies. What You Will Do Promote maternal and child health Lead awareness campaigns on disease prevention (malaria, HIV, nutrition) Train community members on hygiene and sanitation Support health data tracking and behavior change initiatives Organize community health programs and events Uganda faces a significant burden of preventable diseases, with approximately 75% of illnesses linked to preventable causes. Volunteers play a critical role in shifting focus from treatment to prevention. Who Should Apply Degree in any field OR 5 years of professional experience Interest in public health or community development Experience in health education is an advantage Learn more and apply HERE. 2. Economic Empowerment Advisor (Uganda) This role is designed to support entrepreneurship and financial independence among community members. What You Will Do Train communities in financial literacy and entrepreneurship Support microfinance and savings initiatives Develop income-generating activities (e.g., agriculture, small businesses) Facilitate business and marketing training sessions Promote sustainable economic practices Uganda has a very young population with high unemployment rates, making economic empowerment initiatives critical for long-term development. Who Should Apply Degree in business or related field OR 5 years’ experience Skills in entrepreneurship, finance, or training Interest in economic development and youth empowerment Learn more and apply HERE. 3. School Enrichment Facilitator (Uganda) This role focuses on improving education outcomes and holistic student development. What You Will Do Support English and literacy development Introduce extracurricular programs (arts, sports, clubs) Promote hygiene, nutrition, and mental wellness in schools Engage parents and communities in education Organize workshops for teachers and students With a large youth population, Uganda prioritizes education as a key driver of development, making this role essential for community transformation. title: Apply for NASA Dragonfly Program 2026: Fully Funded Graduate Opportunity to Explore Titan
summary: The dream of exploring another world is no longer confined to science fiction. Through the Dragonfly Student Early Career Investigator Program, graduate students now have a rare opportunity to contribute directly to one of the most ambitious planetary missions ever conceived. Led by NASA and developed by the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory , the Dragonfly mission is set to explore Titan —a mysterious and Earth-like world unlike any other in our solar system. This comprehensive guide explains everything you need to know about the program, including the mission, opportunities available, eligibility requirements, application process, and detailed project tracks. Understanding the Dragonfly Mission Dragonfly is not just another space mission—it is a revolutionary leap in planetary exploration. The mission will send a rotorcraft lander (an octocopter drone) to Titan, Saturn’s largest moon, marking the first time such a vehicle will be used on another planetary body. Titan is scientifically fascinating for several reasons: It is the only moon with a dense atmosphere It features a methane-based weather cycle , including clouds, rain, rivers, lakes, and seas Its surface contains complex organic molecules , similar to those that existed on early Earth before life began Beneath its icy crust lies a subsurface ocean of liquid water Dragonfly is scheduled to launch in 2028 and arrive at Titan in 2034 . During its 3.3-year mission, it will travel over 80 kilometers, hopping across the surface every Titan day (approximately 16 Earth days), making it the most mobile robotic lander ever deployed. About the Student Early Career Investigator Program The Dragonfly program is designed to bring fresh perspectives into planetary science by engaging graduate students—especially those without prior experience in space missions. What Participants Will Do Selected students will: Conduct research related to Titan Contribute to mission science and operational planning Assist in developing instruments and hardware Participate in testing and simulations Each student collaborates with Dragonfly mission experts and gains hands-on experience in cutting-edge space exploration. Program Structure and Benefits The program selects 2–4 graduate students annually for a two-year term . Key Features Students dedicate 30% of their time (primarily during summer and academic breaks) Work is conducted at APL and/or the mentor’s institution Hybrid format (in-person + remote work) Benefits Include Funding for travel to Dragonfly team meetings Support to publish and present research at scientific conferences Collaboration with leading scientists and engineers Exposure to real-world planetary mission operations Faculty mentors receive travel support to attend kickoff meetings Eligibility Criteria The program aims to broaden participation, especially among students without prior exposure to planetary science or spacecraft missions. Basic Requirements Applicants must: Be U.S. citizens Be enrolled in a Master’s or PhD program in: Physical sciences Biological sciences Computer science Mathematics Engineering Have a minimum GPA of 3.0 Demonstrate ability to conduct independent research Possess strong communication and organizational skills Identify a faculty mentor willing to support them throughout the program Students without backgrounds in planetary science, geosciences, or atmospheric science are especially encouraged to apply . Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Cover letter Curriculum Vitae (CV) Faculty advisor’s name and contact details A 2–3 sentence statement from the faculty advisor confirming support Important Dates Application Deadline: May 29, 2026 Selection Announcement: September 30, 2026 If you experience submission issues, documents can be emailed directly to the program contact. Specialized Project Opportunities In addition to the main program, Dragonfly offers Student Guest Investigator roles focused on specific technical and scientific challenges. These projects are also two-year hybrid commitments. 1. Modeling Impacts on Titan Using Numerical Simulations This project focuses on understanding how impact craters form on Titan using high-fidelity simulations. What You’ll Do Model heliocentric and planetocentric impact events Analyze crater formation, melt volume, and surface changes Develop scripts using Python or MATLAB Work with large datasets and simulation outputs Ideal Candidates Strong analytical thinkers Experience in coding and modeling Interest in planetary surface processes 2. Prototype Development for Spacecraft Integration This hands-on engineering project involves designing a machine to fabricate non-flight cables used in spacecraft testing. What You’ll Do Study existing harness fabrication processes Design and build a prototype system Conduct testing and validation Collaborate with engineers and technicians Ideal Candidates Background in mechanical, electrical, or software engineering Experience with prototyping or automation systems Strong problem-solving skills 3. Simulating Seismic Scattering on Titan This project explores how seismic waves travel through Titan’s icy crust using computational modeling. What You’ll Do Run simulations using advanced modeling tools Analyze seismic wave propagation Compare Titan data with Earth, Mars, and Moon models Use high-performance computing systems Ideal Candidates Background in physics, mathematics, or engineering Experience with coding and simulations Knowledge of calculus and wave mechanics Faculty Mentor Requirement All applicants must secure a faculty mentor who agrees to: Provide workspace and institutional support Participate in the Fall 2026 kickoff meeting Acknowledge that the project is supplementary , not a replacement for thesis research Why This Opportunity Matters The Dragonfly Student Early Career Investigator Program is more than an academic experience—it is a gateway into the future of space exploration. Participants contribute directly to a mission that could reshape our understanding of how life begins in the universe. Working on Titan offers a unique perspective on Earth’s early chemistry, making this mission one of the most important astrobiology investigations of our time. If you are a graduate student eager to apply your STEM skills to real-world challenges and contribute to a historic mission beyond Earth, this program offers an unmatched opportunity. Whether your interests lie in simulation, engineering, or scientific analysis, Dragonfly provides a platform to turn curiosity into discovery. This is your chance to be part of a mission that will explore one of the most Earth-like yet alien environments in our solar system—and potentially answer one of humanity’s biggest questions: how life begins. title: Back to Inspire: Mastercard Foundation Ghana Alumni Mentorship Initiative
summary: The Back to Inspire: From Here to There initiative is a national mentorship program designed to connect alumni with students in Senior High Schools (SHS) and Senior High Technical Schools (SHTS) across Ghana. Led by the Mastercard Foundation Alumni Network – Ghana Chapter, in collaboration with the Ghana Education Service (GES) and T-TEL, this initiative aims to bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world opportunities. Unlike traditional opportunities such as scholarships or internships, this program focuses on mentorship and knowledge-sharing , giving alumni the chance to directly influence the next generation by sharing their lived experiences, career journeys, and lessons learned. About the Opportunity The Back to Inspire initiative is built on the belief that alumni are powerful agents of change. Having successfully navigated the education system and transitioned into careers or entrepreneurship, alumni are uniquely positioned to guide current students. Through this program, selected alumni will return to their former schools—or support schools within their regions—to engage with students through mentorship activities. These engagements may include career talks, one-on-one mentoring, group sessions, and guidance on educational pathways. The initiative particularly focuses on supporting students in underserved or hard-to-reach communities, where access to mentorship and career guidance is often limited. By bringing alumni back into schools, the program creates a bridge between aspiration and opportunity, helping students better understand their future possibilities. Why This Opportunity Matters Many students in Ghana, particularly in underserved communities, lack access to relatable mentors, accurate career information, and practical guidance on life after school. This often leads to uncertainty and missed opportunities. Back to Inspire addresses this challenge by connecting students with alumni who have walked the same path. By sharing lived experiences, mentors can: Demystify career and education pathways Build students’ confidence and aspirations Strengthen school-level mentoring systems Support smoother transitions into higher education, employment, or entrepreneurship This initiative is not just about mentorship—it is about creating a ripple effect of knowledge, inspiration, and opportunity across generations . What You’ll Gain While this is a volunteer-based initiative, the benefits go far beyond financial rewards. Participants will gain: Leadership and Mentorship Experience – Strengthen your ability to guide, coach, and inspire young people Personal Fulfillment – Make a tangible difference in students’ lives and contribute to national development Professional Growth – Enhance your communication, leadership, and facilitation skills Expanded Network – Connect with fellow alumni, educators, and national education stakeholders Recognition and Impact – Be part of a nationally recognized initiative shaping the future of education in Ghana Additionally, selected alumni will receive training, safeguarding guidance, and facilitation support , ensuring they are well-prepared to engage effectively with students. Eligibility This opportunity is open to: Mastercard Foundation alumni in Ghana Individuals willing to return to their former SHS/SHTS or support schools in their region Alumni interested in mentoring and engaging with students Those passionate about youth development, education, and career guidance Individuals ready to contribute to a collaborative, national initiative No prior mentoring experience is required—just a willingness to give back and inspire. Application Process and Deadline Interested alumni are required to express their interest by completing a short online form . This initial step helps organizers: Understand alumni backgrounds and school affiliations Identify available resources and mentorship gaps Plan coordinated engagement with schools After submission: Alumni data will inform school selection and rollout planning Selected participants will receive onboarding and guidance Mentorship engagements will begin after final selections Before applying, read the official brief . After, you can apply via the official interest form. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? title: AI Science Institute Postdoctoral Fellowship at Boston Consulting Group: A High-Impact Opportunity in Applied AI Research
summary: Introduction: Advancing Science Through AI Innovation In today’s rapidly evolving technological landscape, artificial intelligence is transforming how industries solve complex challenges—from healthcare breakthroughs to climate solutions. The AI Science Institute (AISI) Postdoctoral Fellowship by Boston Consulting Group represents a unique opportunity for early-career scientists to operate at the cutting edge of applied AI research while working on real-world, high-impact problems. Delivered through BCG X , this fellowship blends scientific exploration, industry application, and strategic impact, creating a rare bridge between academia and real-world deployment. About Boston Consulting Group and BCG X Founded in 1963, Boston Consulting Group is one of the world’s leading management consulting firms, known for driving transformation across industries. With a strong focus on integrating digital and human capabilities , BCG supports organizations in achieving sustainable growth and innovation. BCG X , the firm’s technology and innovation arm, brings together over 3,000 experts in AI, engineering, and design to solve some of the world’s most complex challenges. Through its global network, BCG X enables talent to build groundbreaking solutions that impact millions of lives. About the AI Science Institute Fellowship The AI Science Institute (AISI) Fellowship is an elite postdoctoral program designed to empower the next generation of applied science leaders. Unlike traditional academic or big-tech postdoctoral roles, this program places fellows at the intersection of: Advanced scientific research Real-world implementation Strategic decision-making Fellows will work on problems where data, models, and problem definitions are not fully established , requiring creativity, innovation, and interdisciplinary collaboration. Key Responsibilities and Learning Opportunities Participants in the AISI Fellowship will: 1. Conduct High-Impact Research Apply machine learning, simulation, optimization, and Generative AI to real-world scientific problems Lead or contribute to multidisciplinary research initiatives 2. Collaborate Across Disciplines Work closely with scientists, engineers, and business leaders Engage with global partners across industries 3. Publish and Innovate Author research papers in top-tier journals Co-develop intellectual property and innovative solutions 4. Build Scalable Solutions Design workflows that translate AI models into real-world tools Ensure reproducibility and scalability of research 5. Act as a Scientific Ambassador Represent AISI across projects Contribute to shaping research direction and impact Research Areas and Focus Themes Fellows will contribute to cutting-edge domains such as: AI-accelerated materials discovery Scientific foundation models (biology, chemistry, climate science) Simulation-augmented learning (physics-informed AI, reinforcement learning) Compute technologies (chip design, algorithm optimization) Quantum computing and hybrid workflows These areas reflect the program’s ambition to solve globally significant scientific and technological challenges . Program Structure and Benefits Duration 24 months, with the possibility of transitioning into a full-time role at BCG X Mentorship Dedicated mentorship from senior advisors and scientific leaders Resources Access to BCG’s global infrastructure Advanced computing resources Exclusive datasets and research ecosystems Compensation First-year base salary: $158,400 Additional support for: Publishing Conference participation Intellectual property development Comprehensive Benefits Package The fellowship offers a highly competitive benefits package, including: Full medical, dental, and vision coverage (no premiums for employees and families) Low-cost healthcare access Gym and fitness reimbursements Retirement contributions and profit-sharing Paid parental leave and family support programs Generous paid time off and holidays This ensures fellows can focus fully on research and innovation while maintaining personal well-being. Work Locations and Environment The program is based in major U.S. cities, including: Boston New York San Francisco Chicago Seattle Washington, DC Fellows are expected to: Work in-office as required Travel occasionally to client sites Engage in a fast-paced, high-impact work environment Why This Fellowship Stands Out This program is distinct because it: Bridges the gap between academia and industry Focuses on real-world deployment of AI solutions Encourages fellows to define and shape research problems Offers direct collaboration with global industry leaders It is ideal for those who want to move beyond theoretical research and create tangible, scalable impact . Application Process To apply, candidates must: Submit a Resume/CV Include a 1–2 page statement of interest outlining: Research background Areas of interest Motivation for joining AISI Provide links to publications, code, or relevant projects For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post AI Science Institute Postdoctoral Fellowship at Boston Consulting Group: A High-Impact Opportunity in Applied AI Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Powerful Carnegie Young Ambassador Program 2026 in Technology and Society
summary: The Exceptional Carnegie Young Ambassador Program 2026 offers a unique and highly competitive opportunity for young professionals to gain hands-on experience in research-driven policymaking within a globally recognized think tank. Hosted by Carnegie India, a part of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, this prestigious one-year program is designed to nurture future leaders in public policy, technology, and international affairs. With applications open until April 1, 2026, this program invites recent graduates from diverse academic backgrounds to immerse themselves in cutting-edge policy research and global discussions on technology and society. The selected candidate will be based in New Delhi, India, working full-time from May 2026 to May 2027. Exceptional Opportunity to Work in a Global Think Tank The Carnegie India Young Ambassadors Program (CIYAP) provides an early career platform for individuals who are passionate about understanding global governance, public policy, and technological transformations shaping the modern world. As part of a globally connected institution, participants gain exposure to a wide network of experts and thought leaders across cities such as Washington D.C., Brussels, Berlin, Beirut, California, and Singapore. The Exceptional Carnegie Young Ambassador Program 2026 is not just a fellowship—it is a transformative experience that blends research, policy engagement, and professional development within a multidisciplinary environment. Exceptional Learning Experience in Technology Policy Participants in the program will work closely with the Technology Society Program at Carnegie India, focusing on some of the most critical and emerging issues in global policy. Key research areas include: • Artificial Intelligence (AI) • Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) • Cybersecurity • Space policy • Semiconductors • Critical minerals • Subsea cables • Nuclear energy • Defense innovation • Data governance This exposure enables participants to develop a deep understanding of how technology intersects with governance, economics, and global security. Exceptional Skills Development and Professional Growth The program is specifically designed to equip participants with essential professional skills required for leadership in policy and research environments. Throughout the one-year journey, the selected ambassador will: • Develop analytical and critical thinking skills • Enhance report writing and research capabilities • Strengthen leadership and communication abilities • Gain practical experience in policy development and implementation Participants will also receive mentorship from leading scholars and practitioners, allowing them to refine their research interests and career goals. Exceptional Hands-On Experience in Policy and Events A key component of the Exceptional Carnegie Young Ambassador Program 2026 is active participation in the organization’s research and programmatic activities. The selected candidate will: • Assist scholars with ongoing research projects • Conduct background research and contribute to publications • Support program, development, and communication teams • Participate in organizing major events such as the Global Technology Summit (GTS) • Contribute to dialogues such as the Security Studies Dialogue and Innovation Dialogue This hands-on involvement ensures that participants gain real-world experience in both policy research and event coordination within a global institution. Exceptional Global Networking Opportunities One of the standout benefits of the program is the opportunity to build a strong professional network. Participants will engage with a diverse cohort of young leaders in New Delhi and connect with professionals across the global Carnegie network. This exposure not only enhances learning but also opens doors to future career opportunities in international organizations, research institutions, and policy-making bodies. Program Structure and Benefits The Exceptional Carnegie Young Ambassador Program 2026 is a full-time, in-person program based in New Delhi. It offers: • A fixed monthly stipend • A one-time relocation allowance • Access to global research networks • Mentorship from experienced policy experts • Participation in high-impact policy discussions and events The program emphasizes inclusivity and diversity, ensuring that selection is based on merit and potential without discrimination. Who Should Apply The program is ideal for recent graduates who are eager to explore careers in public policy, technology governance, and international affairs. Applicants should: • Have a strong interest in technology policy, AI, law, economics, or related fields • Demonstrate curiosity about global issues and policymaking • Possess strong research and communication skills • Be willing to work full-time in New Delhi Preference will be given to candidates from beyond metropolitan cities in India, reflecting Carnegie India’s commitment to expanding opportunities across diverse regions. Application Process Applicants are required to submit the following: • Curriculum Vitae (CV) • Cover letter • One writing sample Applications must be submitted before the deadline, and only the most qualified candidates will be selected. Deadline for Applications The deadline to apply for the Exceptional Carnegie Young Ambassador Program 2026 is April 1, 2026. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Exceptional Carnegie Young Ambassador Program 2026 and apply, click here : Conclusion The Exceptional Carnegie Young Ambassador Program 2026 is a powerful gateway for young professionals seeking to build a career in public policy and technology. By combining research, mentorship, and global exposure, the program equips participants with the skills and networks needed to make meaningful contributions to society. title: Future Food Sustainability MSc at Cranfield University: Build a Career Solving Global Food Challenges
summary: Introduction: Shaping the Future of Global Food Systems In a world facing rising population growth, climate change, and resource scarcity, ensuring sustainable food systems has become one of the most urgent global priorities. The Future Food Sustainability MSc at Cranfield University is a pioneering postgraduate programme designed to equip students with the knowledge, tools, and strategic mindset needed to tackle these complex challenges. This innovative MSc stands out as the first programme in the UK to combine science, technology, strategic foresight, and management in addressing food sustainability. It aligns closely with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) , particularly those related to food security, environmental sustainability, and responsible production. Why Study Future Food Sustainability? Global food systems are under immense pressure due to: Climate change and environmental degradation Political and economic instability Rapidly changing dietary patterns Increasing demand for safe, nutritious food This programme goes beyond theory. It provides a multidisciplinary approach to understanding and solving real-world problems in food production, supply chains, and sustainability. Students will: Develop strategic foresight and scenario planning skills Learn to design resilient and sustainable food systems Gain exposure to cutting-edge agri-tech innovations Work on industry-led projects with real-world impact Programme Overview Start Date: October (Full-time Part-time) Duration: Full-time: 1 year Part-time: 2–3 years Location: Cranfield Campus, United Kingdom Study Type: Full-time / Part-time Qualification: MSc, PgDip, PgCert Course Structure The programme is structured to balance theory, practical application, and research: Taught Modules: 80 credits (800 hours) Group Project: 40 credits (400 hours) Individual Research Project: 60 credits (600 hours) Course Content and Modules The MSc includes eight compulsory modules , each addressing a key aspect of sustainable food systems: 1. Principles of Sustainability Explore global frameworks such as: Sustainable Development Goals Circular Economy Ecosystem Services and Natural Capital 2. Technologies for Seeds and Crop Protection Learn about: Plant breeding and genetics Agrochemicals and biocontrol Sustainable crop production technologies 3. Soil Systems Understand: Soil health and ecosystem services Soil chemistry and biology Regenerative agriculture practices 4. Food Chain Resilience Focus on: Supply chain management Risk mitigation strategies Sustainable and circular food supply systems 5. Agricultural Informatics Gain skills in: Big data analysis GIS and remote sensing Data-driven agriculture 6. Sustainability and Environmental Assessment Master tools such as: Life Cycle Analysis (LCA) Carbon footprinting Environmental and social impact assessment 7. Water and Sustainable Agrifood Systems Study: Water resource management Irrigation systems Climate-related risks in agriculture 8. Strategic Foresight Develop: Scenario planning skills Horizon scanning techniques Long-term strategic decision-making capabilities Hands-On Learning Experience Group Project Students work on a consultancy-style project , tackling real challenges faced by industry partners. This builds teamwork, leadership, and problem-solving skills. Individual Research Project A major research thesis, often conducted with external organisations, allows students to: Deepen expertise Conduct independent research Propose innovative solutions to global food issues Industry-Relevant Learning The programme is strongly aligned with industry needs through: Advisory panels from global organisations Guest lectures by industry leaders Field trips and practical exposure Real-world case studies This ensures graduates are job-ready and highly competitive in the global market. Career Opportunities After Graduation Graduates of this MSc are well-positioned for impactful careers in: Government and policy-making institutions International organisations such as the Food and Agriculture Organization Private sector companies like Kellogg’s , Carlsberg Group , and Deloitte Research institutes and NGOs Career Support Cranfield Careers and Employability Service provides: CV and interview preparation Career coaching Access to job platforms and employer networks Lifelong career support even after graduation Research and Innovation Opportunities Students benefit from access to: Cutting-edge agrifood research World-leading experts Advanced facilities in soil, biosciences, and sustainability Graduates can also pursue PhD opportunities at Cranfield or other top universities worldwide. Accreditation This MSc is accredited by the Institution of Agricultural Engineers , ensuring academic excellence and professional recognition. Who Should Apply? This programme is ideal for: Graduates passionate about sustainability and food systems Individuals seeking careers in agrifood, policy, or research Professionals looking to transition into sustainability-focused roles It welcomes candidates from diverse academic backgrounds , making it accessible to a wide range of applicants. Fees and Funding Detailed information on tuition fees and funding options is available through the official programme page. title: Community Foundation of Acadiana Funding Nonprofits to Strengthen Local Communities
summary: The Competitive Grants program, administered through the Community Foundation of Acadiana (CFA), is a philanthropic funding opportunity designed to support nonprofit organizations working to improve quality of life and strengthen communities in South Louisiana. The initiative combines two grant streams — the Affiliate Grants and The Fund for Acadiana Grants — each crafted to support nonprofit-led projects that directly benefit residents across several parishes in the region. These grants aren’t scholarships or internships — they are funding awards that expand the capacity of organizations working on education, health, community development, and much more. About the Opportunity CFA’s Competitive Grants program provides structured grant awards to nonprofits that are actively serving communities within Acadiana. Two distinct grant tracks are available: Affiliate Grants : Awards up to $5,000 supporting localized community projects in specific parishes (Evangeline, Iberia, St. Landry, St. Mary, Vermilion). The Fund for Acadiana Grants : Larger grants up to $15,000 for broader nonprofit initiatives that aim for sustainability and systemic change across multiple parishes (Acadia, Evangeline, Iberia, Lafayette, St. Landry, St. Martin, St. Mary, Vermilion). These grants serve as a direct investment in the ideas and actions of community organizations, enabling them to expand services, launch impactful programs, and respond to pressing local challenges. Why This Opportunity Matters Competitive Grants from the Community Foundation of Acadiana aim to go beyond one‑off funding — they are about strengthening the fabric of local communities . By channeling philanthropic dollars into nonprofits that can convert resources into measurable impact, these grants support: Greater community resilience , by enabling organizations to tackle issues like education gaps or public health needs with targeted resources. Local innovation , because nonprofits often understand on-the‑ground challenges better than outside funders and can propose creative, tailored solutions. Collaborative growth , as grantees often form networks of practice, share outcomes, and scale solutions that benefit the entire Acadiana region. For organizations working in partnership with residents, schools, clinics, or cultural institutions, this funding offers crucial support to scale their work and deepen their impact where it matters most. What You’ll Gain Nonprofit organizations selected for a Competitive Grant receive: Financial Support – Grants between $5,000 and $15,000 to implement projects that address local needs or pursue long‑term impact goals. Community Recognition – Visibility within the Acadiana philanthropic ecosystem, helping organizations build credibility and attract additional support. Capacity Development – Even beyond the funding, participating in this competitive process helps organizations refine proposals, clarify outcomes, and strengthen strategic planning capacity. Ultimately, these funds help organizations expand their reach — from educational programs and youth services to health and human services, arts, and community development — bringing tangible improvements to residents’ lives. Eligibility To be eligible, applicants must meet the following criteria : Be a registered nonprofit with 501(c)(3) status under U.S. tax law. Have the proposed project located within the eligible parishes served by CFA’s grant programs. Demonstrate that the project aligns with the community goals and focus areas of the specific parish affiliate or the broader Acadiana grant initiative. Projects that do not qualify include individual support, endowments, operating expenses, completed work, candidate political activities, or simple sponsorship requests. Application Process and Deadline The Competitive Grants cycle typically opens with application availability in March 2026 , with grants ranging from up to $5,000 for Affiliate Grants and up to $15,000 for The Fund for Acadiana. The application process has multiple stages: First‑Round Submission (Eligibility Interest Form) – Applicants must submit a short form by May 14, 2026 . Second‑Round Full Proposal – Comprehensive proposals are accepted between June 22 and July 23, 2026 . Notification and Funding – Grant decisions and funds are typically announced by September 2026 . Interested organizations are encouraged to attend informational sessions and to review the official grant portal guidance materials before applying. Find this and more on the CFA official website. title: Apply for the OIST Fully Funded PhD Program 2026 in Japan
summary: Are you looking to pursue a fully funded PhD in a world-class research environment? The Okinawa Institute of Science and Technology (OIST) is now accepting applications for its 2026 PhD Program , offering an exceptional opportunity to study in Japan while working at the forefront of interdisciplinary science. The PhD Program is designed for students from around the world who are passionate about research and innovation. With full financial support, access to cutting-edge laboratories, and a highly international environment, OIST provides a unique platform for developing the next generation of scientific leaders. About the Program The PhD Program is a five-year, fully funded doctoral program based in Okinawa, Japan. Unlike traditional PhD programs, OIST emphasizes interdisciplinary research , allowing students to explore multiple scientific fields before selecting a specific research unit. Students begin with coursework and lab rotations, gaining exposure to different disciplines such as physics, biology, neuroscience, mathematics, and environmental science. This flexible structure allows candidates to find the best fit for their research interests before committing to a thesis project. The program is conducted entirely in English, making it accessible to international applicants from diverse academic backgrounds. Why This Opportunity Matters The OIST PhD Program stands out for its commitment to innovation, collaboration, and global impact . By removing traditional academic barriers between disciplines, the program encourages students to tackle complex scientific challenges from multiple perspectives. In today’s rapidly evolving world, interdisciplinary research is essential for solving global issues such as climate change, health challenges, and technological advancement. OIST prepares students to contribute meaningfully to these fields. For aspiring researchers, this program represents more than just a degree — it is an opportunity to join a global scientific community and work on research that can shape the future. What You’ll Gain One of the most significant benefits of this program is the full financial support provided to all admitted students. PhD candidates receive a generous annual stipend to cover living expenses, along with full tuition coverage . In addition, OIST provides relocation support, housing assistance, and travel funding , ensuring that students can focus entirely on their research without financial stress. Another major advantage is access to state-of-the-art research facilities . OIST is known for its advanced laboratories and cutting-edge technology, enabling students to work on innovative and high-impact projects. Students also benefit from direct mentorship by leading international faculty . The institute brings together experts from around the world, offering a truly global academic experience. The program further provides strong career development opportunities , including exposure to academic, industry, and research networks. Graduates of OIST are well-positioned for careers in academia, industry, and policy. Additionally, living in Okinawa offers a unique cultural experience, combining high-quality research with a vibrant international community . Eligibility The OIST PhD Program is open to applicants worldwide who meet the following criteria: A completed or expected bachelor’s or master’s degree in a relevant field Strong academic performance and interest in scientific research Ability to conduct research and communicate in English Importantly, OIST does not require a specific undergraduate major, as the program values interdisciplinary potential and research motivation . Application Process and Deadline To apply for the PhD Program, candidates must complete an online application through the official admissions portal. The application typically requires academic transcripts , a statement of purpose , and letters of recommendation . The statement of purpose is especially important, as it allows applicants to explain their research interests, academic background, and why they are interested in OIST’s interdisciplinary approach. While prior research experience can strengthen an application, the program also values curiosity, motivation, and potential. After submission, applications are reviewed, and shortlisted candidates are invited to the final selection stage, where they are assessed more holistically. 🗓 Application Deadline: March 31, 2026 (JST) Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all materials are complete. Learn more and apply through the official OIST website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? title: NAP Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Archivists of African Descent to Advance Their Careers and Projects
summary: The preservation of history, culture, and collective memory is a powerful tool for social justice, identity, and community empowerment. Recognizing the importance of inclusive archival practices, the Nomadic Archivists Project (NAP) has opened applications for the NAP Scholarship 2026 , a unique opportunity designed to support emerging and independent archivists of African descent. This annual scholarship aims to remove financial barriers and create access to professional development opportunities, enabling archivists to expand their skills, networks, and impact. Applications for the 2026 cycle are currently open and must be submitted by March 31, 2026 . About the NAP Scholarship The NAP Scholarship is a financial award of up to $1,500 , granted annually to support: Students in archival studies Early-career archivists Independent archivists The scholarship is specifically intended to help recipients either: Attend the Society of American Archivists (SAA) Annual Conference , or Fund an archival project that contributes to community memory and knowledge preservation By investing in archivists, the program contributes to a more inclusive and representative archival field. Why This Scholarship Matters Access to professional opportunities in archival studies is often limited by financial constraints. Conferences, training programs, and project development require resources that many emerging archivists may not have. The NAP Scholarship directly addresses these challenges by: Increasing access to professional networks Supporting skill development and education Enabling community-centered archival projects Promoting representation within archival institutions The initiative emphasizes that access to information and opportunities is essential for equity in the archival field . What the Scholarship Covers Selected recipients can use the funding for a wide range of expenses, including: Conference registration fees Travel and accommodation Memberships in professional organizations Training programs and workshops Archival supplies and project materials Personal time dedicated to project development This flexibility allows applicants to tailor the funding to their specific needs and career goals. Program Impact and Vision The NAP Scholarship is rooted in the belief that archivists play a critical role in shaping historical narratives and preserving marginalized voices. Through this initiative, the Nomadic Archivists Project seeks to: Empower archivists of African descent globally Foster community-driven archival practices Encourage ethical and inclusive documentation Strengthen participation in professional archival spaces The scholarship also highlights the importance of platforms like the Society of American Archivists (SAA), where archivists can: Share research and projects Build professional networks Participate in leadership opportunities Engage in workshops and training sessions Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a current undergraduate or graduate student , or an independent archivist Be of African descent Demonstrate a commitment to pursuing a career in archives Show a clear financial need for the scholarship Application Requirements To be considered for the NAP Scholarship 2026, applicants must submit: A 500–750 word essay outlining: Their interest in archival work, or A proposed archival project A resume A professional headshot Additional Requirement (if applicable): Applicants seeking funding for a project must include a detailed budget (e.g., supplies, travel, production costs) Application Process All applications must be submitted via email. 📧 Submission Email: miranda@nomadicarchivistsproject.com Applicants are encouraged to ensure that all required documents are complete and clearly presented before submission. For questions regarding eligibility or the application process, applicants can contact Miranda Mims via the same email address. title: CARIBEquity Investment Training Program 2026: Strengthening Investor Capacity and Unlocking Capital in the Caribbean
summary: The Caribbean region is rich in untapped capital and emerging innovation. However, despite this potential, one of the key challenges facing the region is not the absence of financial resources, but rather the lack of shared investment frameworks, investor confidence, and practical tools to effectively deploy capital. The CARIBEquity Investment Training Program 2026 has been developed to directly address these challenges. This initiative is a strategic effort aimed at strengthening investor capacity, improving coordination among regional stakeholders, and facilitating smarter, more impactful capital allocation into early-stage ventures. Applications are currently open and will remain available until April 10, 2026 . Understanding the Need: Why This Program Matters While capital exists across the Caribbean, investment ecosystems often remain fragmented. This fragmentation leads to: Limited collaboration between investors Inconsistent investment practices Reduced trust in early-stage ventures Missed opportunities for innovation-driven growth The CARIBEquity initiative recognizes that building a strong investment ecosystem requires more than funding. It requires education, alignment, and confidence among investors. What is the CARIBEquity Investment Training Program? The CARIBEquity Investment Training Program is a specialized capacity-building initiative designed to equip investors and ecosystem stakeholders with the knowledge and tools necessary to engage effectively in early-stage investing. Developed alongside the CARIBEquity Investor Toolkit , the program forms part of a broader, long-term strategy to strengthen the Caribbean’s entrepreneurial and investment landscape. Core Objectives of the Program The program is structured around three key goals: 1. Building Trust in Early-Stage Investing By providing practical knowledge and proven frameworks, the program helps investors better understand and manage the risks associated with early-stage ventures. 2. Reducing Market Fragmentation The initiative promotes alignment among investors, institutions, and ecosystem actors, fostering a more cohesive regional investment environment. 3. Mobilizing Capital Locally and Regionally By enhancing investor confidence and capability, the program encourages more capital to be deployed within the Caribbean, supporting innovation and economic growth. Who Should Apply? The program is specifically designed for individuals and organizations involved in or interested in investment activities within the Caribbean ecosystem. Ideal participants include: Investors seeking to expand into early-stage opportunities High-Net-Worth Individuals (HNWIs) exploring venture investments Corporate leaders interested in innovation and strategic investments Financial institutions aiming to support startup ecosystems Ecosystem partners working to strengthen regional entrepreneurship What Participants Will Gain Participants in the CARIBEquity Investment Training Program will benefit from: A deeper understanding of early-stage investment strategies Access to practical tools and frameworks for decision-making Insights into regional market dynamics Opportunities to connect with key stakeholders and investors Enhanced confidence in deploying capital effectively Program Partners and Implementation The CARIBEquity initiative is part of a collaborative effort involving international and regional actors committed to strengthening economic development in the Caribbean. The program is: Co-funded by the European Union and IDB Lab Implemented by Seedstars and EOG (Entrepreneurs Of Greatness) This collaboration ensures a high-quality, globally informed, and locally relevant training experience. Application Deadline Deadline to Apply: April 10, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to secure their place in this competitive program. Why You Should Apply The CARIBEquity Investment Training Program represents a unique opportunity to: Strengthen your investment knowledge and skills Contribute to the growth of the Caribbean innovation ecosystem Build valuable connections with regional and international stakeholders Play a role in shaping the future of investment in emerging markets For anyone serious about investing in innovation and driving economic transformation in the Caribbean, this program offers both strategic insight and practical value.
