title: Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 for Future Legal Professionals
summary: The Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 offers an exceptional hands-on opportunity for students and recent graduates interested in criminal law, public policy, and public interest work. Hosted by Georgetown University’s Criminal Justice Clinic, this internship provides real-world investigative experience supporting active criminal defense cases in Washington, DC. With a strong legacy dating back to 1985, the Investigative Internship Program plays a critical role in ensuring high-quality legal representation for indigent defendants. Interns work closely with attorneys across multiple clinics, contributing directly to case preparation, client advocacy, and justice outcomes. Applications for the Fall 2026 intake are currently open, with a final deadline of May 15, 2026. However, due to rolling admissions, applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early. About the Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program The Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program was established to support the work of the Criminal Justice Clinic, which has been providing legal representation to underserved communities since 1971. Today, the program supports three key clinics: • Criminal Defense Prisoner Advocacy Clinic • Criminal Justice Clinic • Juvenile Justice Clinic Interns are integrated into legal teams and play an active role in investigative work that directly impacts the outcome of criminal cases. The program emphasizes experiential learning, allowing participants to engage deeply with the legal system and understand the realities of criminal defense work. Hands-On Legal and Investigative Experience This internship is highly practical and immersive, giving participants the opportunity to work on real cases from start to finish. Interns conduct field investigations in pairs and contribute to every stage of case preparation. Key responsibilities include: • Locating and interviewing witnesses in communities and correctional facilities • Drafting statements and legal memoranda for court use • Conducting criminal background checks • Reviewing and analyzing police and surveillance footage • Collecting and summarizing case-related documents • Documenting and analyzing crime scenes • Creating evidence materials for trial Interns are also involved in high-level legal processes such as client consultations, plea negotiations, and strategy sessions with attorneys. Holistic Approach to Criminal Defense A unique feature of the Georgetown Investigative Internship Program is its holistic approach to criminal defense. The program recognizes that effective advocacy extends beyond legal representation and addresses broader social challenges faced by clients. Interns assist clients with: • Accessing housing support • Enrolling in treatment and rehabilitation programs • Preparing for reintegration into society This approach ensures that clients are supported as individuals, not just as legal cases, and provides interns with a deeper understanding of the social dimensions of justice. Trial Preparation and Courtroom Exposure Interns play a crucial role in trial preparation, assisting attorneys with last-minute case developments and logistical coordination. They help ensure that defense witnesses are prepared, available, and able to attend court proceedings. In some cases, interns may even be called to testify, providing them with rare and valuable courtroom experience. Additionally, interns contribute to post-conviction cases, including compassionate release applications and resentencing efforts under key legal frameworks such as IRAA and SLAA. Learning and Professional Development The internship offers a highly educational environment where participants work alongside experienced attorneys, law students, and faculty members. Interns gain: • A deep understanding of the criminal legal system • Practical investigative and legal skills • Exposure to real-world legal challenges • Insight into careers in law and public service The program also provides mentorship and career guidance, helping interns make informed decisions about pursuing legal careers. Who Should Apply The Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 is ideal for individuals considering careers in law, criminal justice, investigative journalism, or public policy. Applicants may include: • Undergraduate students (especially juniors and seniors) • Recent graduates • Graduate students No specific major is required, and applicants from diverse academic backgrounds are encouraged to apply. While prior experience in law or criminal justice is helpful, it is not mandatory. Applicants should demonstrate strong communication skills, the ability to work in diverse environments, and a willingness to engage in field-based investigative work. Important Considerations This internship is intensive and may involve long or irregular working hours, particularly during trial preparation periods. It also requires fieldwork in urban communities, which may be unfamiliar to some applicants. However, interns receive training and work in pairs to ensure safety and support throughout their experience. Application Process Applicants must complete an online application form and may submit additional materials such as writing samples, publications, or project work to strengthen their application. The selection process evaluates candidates based on: • Quality of application materials • Academic and professional background • Communication skills • References and supporting documents Shortlisted candidates may be invited for video interviews. Deadline for Applications The final deadline to apply for the Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 is May 15, 2026. Due to the rolling selection process, early applications are strongly recommended. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 and apply, click here Conclusion The Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 is a powerful opportunity for aspiring legal professionals to gain real-world experience in criminal defense and investigative work. By participating in this program, interns develop critical skills, contribute to meaningful legal cases, and gain a deeper understanding of justice and advocacy. For more internship opportunities on OFY Opportunities click here The post Powerful Georgetown Law Investigative Internship Program 2026 for Future Legal Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Green Opportunities Fund 2026: £2.2 Million Grant Supporting Youth Green Careers in the UK
summary: The Green Opportunities Fund is a major £2.2 million funding initiative designed to support young people across the United Kingdom in exploring and accessing career pathways within the green economy. This fund is a collaborative effort between the Co-op Foundation and Co-op, reflecting a shared commitment to building sustainable communities while ensuring equal access to emerging opportunities in the rapidly evolving green sector. About the Green Opportunities Fund The Green Opportunities Fund has been established to address a growing need: preparing the next generation for careers that contribute to environmental sustainability. As global economies shift toward greener practices, there is an increasing demand for skilled individuals who can contribute to sectors such as renewable energy, environmental conservation, sustainable agriculture, and green technology. Recognizing this need, the Co-op Foundation partnered with Co-op to create a funding mechanism that empowers organizations working directly with young people. The fund specifically targets initiatives that inspire, educate, and equip youth with the skills and knowledge required to thrive in green careers. This initiative is rooted in a broader mission to foster sustainable communities where opportunities are accessible to all, regardless of background. By investing in youth-focused programs, the fund aims to bridge the gap between interest in sustainability and actual career opportunities in the green economy. Research-Driven Approach The development of the Green Opportunities Fund is strongly informed by research. In 2023, the Co-op Foundation published its Gen Z(ero) report, which revealed that young people are highly interested in understanding the transition to a sustainable economy. The report highlighted a strong desire among youth to learn more about green jobs and how they can be part of this transformation. At the same time, the UK faces a significant green skills gap, with industries struggling to find qualified individuals to fill roles that support environmental sustainability. This disconnect between interest and opportunity became a key driver behind the creation of the fund. By aligning funding priorities with research insights, the Green Opportunities Fund seeks to ensure that its impact is both relevant and effective in addressing real-world challenges. Youth-Led Input and Governance A distinctive feature of the Green Opportunities Fund is its commitment to youth participation in decision-making. The fund has been developed in collaboration with a group of eight young individuals known as the Environmental Collective. This group has played a critical advisory role in shaping the fund’s priorities, ensuring that it reflects the needs, aspirations, and perspectives of young people themselves. Their involvement does not end with the design phase—they will continue to contribute to key decisions throughout the lifecycle of the funding program. This approach reinforces the fund’s credibility and relevance, as it integrates the voices of those it is designed to benefit. Funding Focus and Objectives The Green Opportunities Fund is specifically aimed at supporting initiatives that influence young people aged 14 to 20. The goal is to encourage this age group to: Develop practical and transferable green skills Gain awareness of career opportunities within the green economy Pursue pathways that lead to sustainable and environmentally focused jobs The fund prioritizes programs that not only educate but also actively engage young people, helping them transition from awareness to action. Organizations are encouraged to propose innovative and impactful projects that can inspire long-term interest and participation in green careers. Eligibility Criteria The Green Opportunities Fund is open to a range of organizations operating within the UK. Eligible applicants include: Charities Community Interest Companies (CICs) Co-operatives Community Benefit Societies By opening the fund to diverse organizational structures, the initiative aims to reach a wide spectrum of communities and deliver localized, context-specific solutions. Application Process Applications for the first round of the Green Opportunities Fund are currently open. Interested organizations are encouraged to review all relevant documentation before applying, including: Fund guidelines Application questions Theory of Development AI statement These resources are designed to help applicants understand the fund’s priorities, expectations, and evaluation criteria. To support potential applicants, a webinar was held on Wednesday, 18th March, providing detailed insights into the fund and addressing common questions. Those who were unable to attend can access the full webinar recording to gain a comprehensive understanding of the application process. CLICK HERE TO APPLY Application Deadline All applications must be submitted by midday on Friday, 17th April. Late submissions will not be considered, so organizations are advised to plan and complete their applications well in advance of the deadline. Support and Enquiries Applicants who have questions or require clarification during the application process can reach out directly via email at foundation@coop.co.uk . This support ensures that organizations have the guidance they need to submit strong and competitive proposals. Conclusion The Green Opportunities Fund represents a significant investment in the future of both young people and the environment. By addressing the green skills gap and empowering youth to pursue sustainable careers, the fund plays a crucial role in shaping a more inclusive and environmentally responsible economy. Through strategic partnerships, research-backed insights, and youth-led input, this initiative stands out as a forward-thinking model for funding programs that aim to create lasting social and environmental impact. title: Call for Applications: Feminist Storytelling Grant 2026 for Writers, Filmmakers, and Artists
summary: The Feminist Storytelling Grant 2026 is now open for applications, offering a powerful opportunity for individuals committed to using storytelling as a tool for resistance, equality, and transformative social change. This grant is specifically designed to support women and LGBTQI+ persons who are working to reclaim narratives, challenge dominant perspectives, and amplify the voices of marginalized communities through creative expression. About the Feminist Storytelling Grant In many societies around the world, the voices and lived experiences of women and gender-diverse individuals are often overlooked, misrepresented, or spoken for by others. The Feminist Storytelling Grant seeks to address this imbalance by creating a platform where these individuals can tell their own stories—authentically and unapologetically. Through this initiative, selected grantees will be empowered to produce compelling, community-centered stories that reflect real experiences, challenge inequalities, and inspire meaningful dialogue and action. The program welcomes a wide range of creatives, including filmmakers, writers, photographers, journalists, artists, and new media creators who are passionate about feminist storytelling and social justice. Who Can Apply? Applicants must demonstrate a strong alignment with feminist principles and a commitment to human rights. Ideal candidates should meet the following criteria: Show a firm belief in feminist values and advocacy for equality. Demonstrate the ability to creatively capture and present the voices and lived experiences of marginalized communities. Be committed to ethical, accountable, and community-centered storytelling practices. This opportunity is open to individuals across various creative disciplines who are dedicated to producing impactful narratives that contribute to social transformation. Accepted Storytelling Formats The grant supports a diverse range of storytelling formats, allowing applicants to choose the medium that best suits their creative vision. Eligible formats include: Visual Storytelling: Photography, film, animation Graphic Information Design: Graphic narratives, infographics Written Works: Essays, poetry, fiction, and other forms of creative writing Audio Storytelling: Podcasts, radio productions Performance Art: Theater and dance Digital Storytelling: Interactive or digital-first narratives Mixed Media: Innovative combinations of multiple formats This flexibility ensures that storytellers can fully express their ideas using the most effective and engaging medium. Why You Should Apply The Feminist Storytelling Grant is more than just financial support—it is a platform for change. It provides storytellers with the resources, mentorship, and community needed to bring powerful narratives to life. If you are passionate about challenging injustice, amplifying unheard voices, and using creativity as a form of resistance, this grant offers the ideal opportunity to turn your vision into impact. By supporting diverse storytellers and elevating authentic voices, this initiative contributes to a more inclusive and equitable global narrative landscape. Benefits and Support for Selected Grantees Recipients of the Feminist Storytelling Grant will gain access to a comprehensive support system designed to nurture both their creative and personal development. The program offers: 1. Mentorship Grantees will receive professional guidance from experienced mentors who will support them throughout the storytelling process. 2. Psychological Support Recognizing the emotional demands of storytelling—especially when dealing with sensitive or personal topics—the grant provides access to mental health and wellbeing resources. 3. Skill Development Participants will have opportunities to enhance their technical and creative abilities through training and capacity-building sessions. 4. Community Building Grantees will join a network of like-minded creatives, fostering collaboration, peer learning, and collective growth. 5. Exhibition Opportunities Completed projects will be showcased through various platforms, offering visibility and recognition for the storytellers and their work. Application Process To apply for the Feminist Storytelling Grant, applicants must secure an endorsement from a trusted organization. Eligible endorsing bodies include: Feminist network partners Media collectives Women Human Rights Defender (WHRD) groups LGBTQI+ community organizations This endorsement serves as a validation of the applicant’s work, credibility, and commitment to community-centered storytelling. To Apply please click here Deadline is 31st March 2026 Grant Timeline and Funding Details Project Duration: All projects must be completed between May and October 2026 , starting from the date of approval. Funding Structure: 50% of the grant will be disbursed upon selection. The remaining 50% will be released after successful project completion. Reporting Requirements: Grantees are required to submit: Monthly progress updates A final report upon project completion All reports should be sent to the designated grant contact email provided in the application guidelines. title: Apply for Global Engagement Lab 6 (GEL 2026–2027): Transformative Program for Grantmakers and Funders
summary: The Global Engagement Lab (GEL) is a unique, immersive 9-month training program designed for progressive funders and grantmakers who are ready to rethink their role in philanthropy and actively contribute to systemic change. More than a traditional capacity-building initiative, GEL is a deep learning journey that combines political education, personal transformation, and collective action. The upcoming Global Engagement Lab 6 (GEL 6) cohort, running from September 2026 to June 2027 , is centered on the theme “Organizing Funders Toward Systemic Change.” It invites participants to critically examine philanthropy as a system of power while equipping them with the tools and strategies needed to transform it from within. Understanding the Purpose of the Global Engagement Lab At its core, GEL is built on the recognition that today’s global challenges—climate crises, inequality, and social injustice—are deeply systemic. Addressing them requires more than traditional grantmaking approaches. The program focuses on three foundational pillars: Deepening knowledge of systemic crises and the movements responding to them Fostering personal transformation to align values with action Exploring the concept of the “funder organizer” as a catalyst for long-term systemic change Through these pillars, GEL creates a space where funders can move beyond surface-level alignment and begin engaging with the deeper political and structural dimensions of their work. Why GEL 6 Matters Now There is growing tension within philanthropy. While many institutions speak about justice, equity, and systems change, fewer are willing to confront the realities of power, capital, and institutional risk . GEL 6 is designed for those who recognize this gap. Rather than offering technical solutions to improve grantmaking processes, GEL 6 challenges participants to: Examine philanthropy as a site of power and influence Understand the limitations of traditional funding models Explore how to organize within institutions to drive meaningful change It is a space for honest reflection, political clarity, and collective experimentation. Key Goals of GEL 6 The program aims to equip participants with the mindset and tools needed to transform philanthropy from within. Specifically, GEL 6 seeks to: Support funders in becoming active agents of change , capable of influencing both their organizations and the broader sector Build knowledge, skills, and political awareness around power, capital, and narrative Strengthen the ability to apply organizing principles across personal, organizational, and systemic levels Move beyond values alignment toward sustained, strategic, and movement-driven action Who Should Apply? GEL 6 is designed for: Mid-career and senior funders or grantmakers Professionals who already recognize the limitations of traditional philanthropy Individuals with the capacity to influence decision-making within their institutions Those willing to engage deeply with complex issues of power, privilege, and systemic change This is not an introductory course. It is intended for practitioners ready to treat philanthropy as a political actor capable of transformation . What Participants Will Explore The GEL 6 curriculum is structured around four interconnected areas of inquiry: 1. Personal Thresholds and Political Clarity Participants reflect on their positionality within systems of power and capital. This includes examining personal values, risk tolerance, and the internal barriers that influence decision-making. The goal is to build more accountable and grounded leadership. 2. Funder Organizing GEL 6 redefines the role of funders—not just as supporters of movements, but as organizers within philanthropy . Participants learn principles such as base-building, collective strategy, and long-term power-building, and explore how to apply them within their institutions. 3. Systems Change, Capital, and Narrative The program introduces frameworks like Just Transition , Buen Vivir , and feminist and critical race theories. Participants analyze how capital and narratives shape global systems and explore ways to organize resources for transformative impact. 4. Time, Discipline, and Resilience Systemic change requires endurance. This component focuses on long-term strategy, leadership development, institutional memory, and navigating conflict with resilience and clarity. Learning Approach and Experience GEL 6 uses a cohort-based, experiential learning model that integrates: Reflective practice Applied systems thinking Peer learning and collaboration Engagement with movement practitioners Participants are expected to actively contribute, share experiences, and learn collectively. This collaborative environment fosters solidarity, accountability, and long-term alignment . Program Structure and Timeline The program combines in-person retreats, virtual workshops, and ongoing cohort engagement. Key Components: In-Person Retreat I (September 2026, Mexico) Virtual Workshops and Monthly Check-ins In-Person Retreat II (2027, location TBC – Morocco, Senegal, or Kenya) Final Reflection and Continued Engagement through EDGE Timeline Overview: 2026 March–April: Applications open May: Cohort selection June–August: Onboarding and preparation September: First in-person retreat October–November: Virtual sessions 2027 January–March: Continued workshops May: Second retreat and EDGE Conference June: Program closure and next steps All virtual sessions are scheduled at 2:00 PM UTC. Program Fees Fees vary depending on organizational budget and EDGE membership status. EDGE Members: $3,500 – Solidarity rate (under $1M budget) $5,000 – Full cost ($1M–$2M budget) $6,500 – Redistribution rate (above $2M budget) Non-Members: $4,000 – Solidarity rate $5,500 – Full cost $7,000 – Redistribution rate Fees cover all program-related expenses except flights. Facilitators and Learning Community GEL 6 will be led by experienced facilitators Bridget Brehen and Hana ElSafoury , alongside guest speakers from global movements and philanthropic organizations. Participants will also engage with a broader ecosystem of aligned initiatives such as: Justice Funders Thousand Currents These partnerships reflect GEL’s commitment to collaboration rather than competition within the field of funder organizing. What Makes GEL 6 Unique? GEL 6 stands out for its politically grounded and action-oriented approach . Unlike traditional philanthropy training programs, it: Treats philanthropy as a space for organizing and transformation Provides practical tools for institutional change Encourages deep personal and political reflection Connects participants to movement-led strategies and global networks By the end of the program, participants gain: Clearer understanding of their influence and limitations Strategies for applying organizing principles in real-world contexts A shared framework for long-term systemic impact How to Apply Applications for GEL 6 are open between March and April 2026 , with the cohort finalized in May. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early and prepare for a rigorous, transformative experience that goes beyond traditional professional development. Click Here to apply. Deadline: May 4, 2026 The Global Engagement Lab 6 is not just a program—it is a call to action for funders who are ready to move beyond conventional philanthropy and actively engage in shaping a more just and equitable world. For those willing to confront complexity, embrace transformation, and organize for systemic change, GEL 6 offers a rare and powerful opportunity. Discover more similar opportunities here . title: High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) 2026: Youth Applications Open to Attend UN Summit in New York (Fully Global Policy Engagement Opportunity)
summary: Youth applications are now open for the 2026 High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) , taking place from July 7–15, 2026 at United Nations Headquarters in New York City . This is a prestigious opportunity for young people in Canada to engage directly with global leaders, policymakers, and development practitioners working to advance the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) . What is the High-Level Political Forum (HLPF)? The High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) is the United Nations’ central platform for: Reviewing global progress on the 2030 Agenda Monitoring implementation of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Promoting accountability and policy coordination across countries Driving forward inclusive, sustainable, and evidence-based solutions Each year, HLPF convenes: Heads of State and Government Ministers and UN officials Civil society organizations Youth leaders and innovators Private sector representatives Why You Should Apply for HLPF 2026 HLPF 2026 represents a critical moment to accelerate global action toward the SDGs as the 2030 deadline approaches. Selected youth participants will have the opportunity to: Participate in high-level policy discussions at the United Nations Engage with global leaders and decision-makers Contribute to international dialogue on sustainable development Showcase community-driven solutions and local innovations Strengthen networks with global youth advocates and organizations Gain exposure to global governance and multilateral processes Eligibility Criteria To apply for HLPF 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a Canadian citizen, permanent resident, or individual with refugee status Reside in Canada for the duration of the programme and event Be aged 18–30 years at the time of the event Demonstrate active engagement in advancing the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) through: Community initiatives Academic research Advocacy work Entrepreneurship Innovative projects Important Notes Individuals temporarily in Canada (e.g., students or tourists on non-immigrant visas) are not eligible Applicants living outside Canada are not eligible If a U.S. visa is required, applicants must indicate this in their application While support letters may be provided, applicants are responsible for meeting all U.S. entry requirements What Makes a Strong Application? Strong candidates typically demonstrate: Clear commitment to sustainable development and SDG impact Evidence of leadership and initiative Experience in community or global engagement A focus on equity, inclusion, and collaboration Ability to translate local impact into global relevance Application Deadline Deadline: April 6, 2026 (11:45 PM) Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted approximately two weeks after the deadline How to Apply Submit your application here: https://forms.office.com/pages/responsepage.aspx?id=Rs4xYpvFukaie_YKxYkOFyr00ELyvRhBmoHhpGlQ_59UQjc2WFhFQkJRWkdLRUFaR0NXMVJBNTVKVi4u origin=lprLink route=shorturl The High-Level Political Forum (HLPF) 2026 is one of the most influential global platforms for shaping sustainable development policy. For young leaders in Canada, this is a rare opportunity to move from local action to global influence—bringing grassroots solutions to the world stage at the United Nations. If you are passionate about driving impact, influencing policy, and contributing to the SDGs, this is an opportunity you should not miss. title: British Council TESOL Scholarships 2026 (Fully Funded): Study in the UK for Teachers in Africa, Europe and Asia
summary: The British Council English and School Education Scholarships Scheme 2026 is a fully funded international scholarship programme designed to strengthen English language teaching (ELT) in government school systems across selected countries. Delivered in partnership with the University of Portsmouth and the University of Stirling , this programme offers 40 fully funded scholarships for experienced teachers and teacher educators to pursue a part-time, online Master’s degree in TESOL (Teaching English to Speakers of Other Languages) while continuing to work in their home countries. Overview of the TESOL Scholarship Programme The scholarship supports educators from: Ghana Kenya Türkiye Bangladesh India Sri Lanka Participants will enroll in one of the following programmes: MA TESOL (Distance Learning) – University of Portsmouth (Ghana, Kenya, Türkiye) MSc TESOL (Online) – University of Stirling (Bangladesh, India, Sri Lanka) The programme begins in September 2026 and runs part-time for up to 30 months , allowing scholars to balance work and study. Scholarship Benefits Each selected scholar will receive a comprehensive funding package, including: Full tuition fees for the TESOL master’s programme Return international flights to the UK Accommodation during the UK study visit Daily living stipend during the visit Visa application fee reimbursement Coverage of one English language test fee Additional Programme Benefits Access to world-class UK university faculty Professional mentoring and academic supervision International networking opportunities Membership in the British Council global alumni network UK Study Visit All scholars will participate in a fully funded two-week academic study visit to the United Kingdom , typically scheduled for Summer 2027 . This visit includes: In-person academic sessions Professional collaboration with peers Networking with global education experts Cultural exchange experiences Programme Structure The TESOL master’s programmes are delivered fully online , featuring: Weekly learning modules Asynchronous study materials Live virtual sessions (where applicable) One-to-one tutor support Dissertation supervision This flexible format allows participants to remain in their home countries while advancing their qualifications. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be citizens and permanent residents of an eligible country Work in or directly with the government education system Be active English language teachers or teacher educators Meet the academic requirements of the selected university Demonstrate commitment to improving national ELT systems Eligible Applicants Government school teachers Teacher trainers and educators Contract or temporary government teachers (if within the public system) Who Cannot Apply Private-sector teachers not affiliated with government systems Dual British citizens British Council or UK government employees (and close relatives) Individuals who studied in the UK within the past 5 years Applicants currently receiving UK government funding Individuals not residing in an eligible country Application Process Applicants must follow a two-step process : Step 1: Apply to a TESOL Master’s Programme Apply to the relevant university (Portsmouth or Stirling) Receive a conditional or unconditional offer Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship Submit the scholarship application after receiving the university offer Applications without a university offer will not be considered Applicants from Ghana, Kenya, and Türkiye: University of Portsmouth MA TESOL (Distance Learning) programme. Watch the information webinar. And apply or enquire. Applicants from Bangladesh, India, and Sri Lanka: University of Stirling MSc TESOL (Online) programme. Watch the information webinar. And apply or enquire. Required Documents For university admission, applicants typically need: Degree certificates and academic transcripts Curriculum Vitae (CV) Personal statement Academic or professional reference English language test score (if already available) English Language Requirements Not required at the initial application stage Required only after shortlisting Accepted tests include: IELTS TOEFL Pearson Cambridge English The scholarship covers one test attempt . Key Dates Application deadline: 30 April 2026 Programme start: September 2026 Scholarship decisions: End of June 2026 UK study visit: Summer 2027 Duration of Study University of Stirling: Up to 30 months University of Portsmouth: Up to 26 months Scholar Expectations After completing the programme, scholars are expected to: Continue working within the government education system Contribute to teacher development and training Strengthen English language teaching practices nationally Participate in British Council alumni activities Why Apply for the British Council TESOL Scholarships? This fully funded scholarship provides a unique opportunity to: Gain an internationally recognized TESOL master’s degree Enhance teaching practice and leadership skills Access global academic and professional networks Contribute to national education system improvement Learn more and apply on the official website here . Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Can I apply if I already have a Master’s degree? Yes, but priority is given to applicants without a TESOL or ELT master’s degree. Do I need to be in my home country? Yes. Applicants must live and work in their eligible country. Can I transfer previous ELT qualifications? No. Scholarship recipients must complete the full programme. Is the programme fully online? Yes, with one mandatory two-week UK study visit. Final Thoughts The British Council English and School Education Scholarships Scheme 2026 is a highly competitive, fully funded opportunity for educators committed to transforming English language teaching systems. By combining flexible online learning, international exposure, and leadership development, this programme equips teachers with the skills and networks needed to drive meaningful educational change. If you want, I can also create: Learn more and apply on the official website here . Looking for more Scholarship opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website! title: Heidelberg Laureate Forum 2026 Journalist Travel Grant (Fully Funded to Germany)
summary: The Heidelberg Laureate Forum Foundation is offering an exceptional opportunity for science and technology journalists worldwide to attend the 13th Heidelberg Laureate Forum (HLF) in Germany from September 13–18, 2026 . This prestigious event gathers winners of the Fields Medal, Turing Award, and Abel Prize , along with 200 of the world’s most outstanding young researchers in mathematics and computer science. It provides a unique platform for journalists to engage directly with leading experts shaping the future of science. 🌟 About the Heidelberg Laureate Forum The Heidelberg Laureate Forum is a globally recognized networking conference that fosters dialogue between top-tier researchers and laureates in mathematics and computer science. Journalists selected for the program gain exclusive access to: In-depth discussions on cutting-edge research Direct interactions with globally recognized laureates Insights into emerging scientific trends A vibrant international network of experts and communicators 🎤 Special Highlight A key highlight of the 2026 forum is the Lindau Lecture , which will be delivered by Nobel Prize-winning physicist Brian Schmidt , renowned for discovering the accelerating expansion of the universe. 💰 Travel Grant Benefits Successful applicants will receive full financial support, including: Round-trip travel to Germany Accommodation throughout the forum Visa costs fully covered Full access to all sessions and networking opportunities This ensures participants can focus entirely on storytelling, learning, and professional growth. 📝 Application Process To apply for the travel grant: Create an account on the official application portal Enter your personal details (name, email, login credentials) Activate your account via the confirmation email Log in and complete your application form Submit before the deadline Ensure your application clearly demonstrates your experience in science or technology journalism and your motivation for attending. ⏰ Application Deadline The application period runs from March 2 to April 8, 2026 . Final Deadline: April 8, 2026 Applications submitted after this date will not be considered. 🚀 Why You Should Apply This opportunity offers immense value for journalists looking to elevate their careers: Access to exclusive, high-level scientific discussions Opportunities to produce impactful global stories Networking with top scientists and fellow journalists Exposure to groundbreaking developments in math and computer science 🔗 How to Apply Applicants should visit the official Heidelberg Laureate Forum website, create an account, and complete the application before the deadline. title: Apply for WHO Climate and SRH Research Grant  — Up to $55,000 for Community-Based Projects
summary: Are you part of a research team working at the intersection of climate change and public health? The World Health Organization (WHO), through its Human Reproduction Programme (HRP), is inviting applications for a global call for research proposals focused on climate change and sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRH) . This opportunity supports research teams in low- and middle-income countries to generate community-driven evidence on how climate change impacts SRH outcomes. With funding of up to USD 55,000 per project , this call is designed to strengthen research capacity while addressing urgent global health challenges through participatory and interdisciplinary approaches. About the Programme This call for proposals focuses on understanding how climate-related events — such as droughts, floods, and extreme temperatures — affect key areas of sexual and reproductive health and rights. These include maternal health, gender-based violence, access to contraception, and abortion care. Selected research teams will participate in a collaborative, cross-country research initiative. Rather than submitting a finalized protocol, applicants are asked to propose a research vision that demonstrates their approach, expertise, and connection to the communities they aim to study. A central component of the programme is a Protocol Development Workshop , scheduled to take place in Geneva in July 2026. During this workshop, selected teams will co-design a unified research framework and methodology. Travel and accommodation costs for up to two team members will be covered by WHO. Why This Opportunity Matters Climate change is increasingly recognized as a major global health challenge, yet its impact on sexual and reproductive health remains under-researched. This call aims to address critical evidence gaps while empowering local researchers and communities. By supporting participatory, community-led research, the programme ensures that those most affected by climate change play an active role in shaping solutions. This approach not only improves research quality but also enhances policy relevance and real-world impact. For research teams, this opportunity offers a pathway to contribute to global knowledge, influence policy, and strengthen long-term research capacity. What Participants Will Gain This opportunity provides both financial and institutional support to research teams working on critical global health issues. Selected teams will receive up to USD 55,000 in research funding , which can be used to support personnel, community engagement activities, data collection, and dissemination efforts. Beyond funding, participants will gain access to capacity-building resources , including training, technical guidance, and research tools provided by WHO. This support helps strengthen research design, implementation, and analysis capabilities. Teams will also benefit from international collaboration , working alongside other selected research groups across different countries. This cross-country exchange allows for shared learning, comparative analysis, and broader research impact. Another key benefit is access to WHO expertise and networks . Selected teams will receive guidance on ethics approval processes, data collection strategies, and dissemination, as well as connections to WHO country offices. Additionally, the programme supports participatory research approaches , meaning community members are actively involved as co-researchers. This enhances the relevance and impact of the research while strengthening community engagement. Who Can Apply This call is open to research teams based in low- and middle-income countries , particularly those in priority countries identified by WHO. Eligible applicants include: Academic and research institutions Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Community-based organizations and civil society groups Multi-institutional research consortia The Principal Investigator and core research team must be based in an eligible country. While international collaboration is encouraged, high-income country partners cannot lead the project and may receive no more than 15% of the total grant. Teams should demonstrate: Experience in SRH or climate-related research Expertise in participatory and mixed-methods approaches Strong community partnerships Capacity to conduct interdisciplinary research Research Focus Areas Applicants must select one of four priority research questions: Maternal health outcomes in the context of climate change Gender-based violence during climate-related events Access to contraception during climate shocks Access to abortion care in climate-affected settings All proposals must apply a human rights-based and intersectional approach , considering how factors such as gender, socioeconomic status, and migration influence vulnerability. Application Process and Deadline Applicants must submit their proposals through the official online platform, including all required documents such as budget, CVs, and conflict of interest declarations. Application Deadline: 12 April 2026 (23:59 GMT+1) Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted. Selected teams will be notified by 11 May 2026 . Learn more and apply through the official WHO/HRP application platform . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Apply for WHO Climate and SRH Research Grant — Up to $55,000 for Community-Based Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities)
summary: The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is an international financial institution and a specialized United Nations agency dedicated to eradicating rural poverty and hunger. It does so by investing in rural people. IFAD finances programmes and projects that increase agricultural productivity and raise rural incomes, and advocates at the local, national and international level for policies that contribute to rural transformation. Job Role The regular Internship Programme (IP) and Enhanced Internship Programme (EIP) offer a unique learning experience of six months to recent university graduates or students who are currently specializing in areas of work relevant to IFAD s mission. Candidates for the Internship Programme must be 30 years old or less and must be enrolled in an accredited university or graduate school, have attended courses in the last 12 months, and have completed at least two years of undergraduate studies when joining the programme. Candidates must meet the following criteria: be enrolled in a university or graduate school, have attended courses in the last 12 months, and have completed at least two years of undergraduate studies when joining IFAD through the programme or; have recently completed university studies at the undergraduate or postgraduate level; be fluent in English; fluency in other IFAD official languages is an asset. In the case of interns recruited in IFAD country office, multi country office or regional office locations, fluency in the main country language may be required; be 30 years of age or younger. The Enhanced Internship Programme, in particular, is designed to enhance geographic distribution of staff at IFAD and is open to candidates who meet the above eligibility criteria and who are not based in Rome. Benefits include: Monthly stipend Housing and travel allowances (Enhanced Internship Programme) Mentorship, learning, and growth opportunities in a dynamic, global environment Financial support: In addition to a monthly allowance, Enhanced Internship Programme interns receive a housing and travel allowance. To apply, please login or register as an intern in IFAD s applicant system . Then click on ‘View all jobs’ and select the option under Job Opening Type, Internship Programme. For more info visit: https://www.ifad.org/en/internship-programme FAQS You may also be interested in this : Click here for Geospatial Analysis Internship at IFAD in Rome The post United Nations IFAD Internship Programme(Paid Internship open to various nationalities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: PAYA Youth Council 2026: Paid Leadership Opportunity for Youth Apprentices in the U.S.

title: MSc in Public Policy and Human Development 2026: Study Policy, Development, and Global Change in the Netherlands(Scholarship Available)
summary: Applications are now open for the Master of Science in Public Policy and Human Development (MPP) , a prestigious one-year postgraduate programme offered through a collaboration between United Nations University (UNU-MERIT) and Maastricht University . Delivered in Maastricht , Netherlands , this internationally recognized programme is designed for individuals who want to tackle complex global challenges and contribute to shaping effective public policies aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals. About the Programme The MSc in Public Policy and Human Development is an intensive, full-time programme that runs for one year and awards two diplomas —one from Maastricht University and one from the United Nations University. The programme combines theoretical knowledge with practical skills, preparing students to design, implement, monitor, and evaluate public policies across sectors. With a strong focus on real-world impact, students gain both quantitative and qualitative skills needed to influence policy decisions at local, national, and global levels. Programme Structure The programme is divided into two main phases: Autumn Semester Focuses on quantitative skills such as statistics, regression analysis, and the use of tools like STATA. This phase equips students with analytical skills essential for policy research. Spring Semester Centers on specialization and thesis writing, allowing students to tailor their learning based on their interests. Students can choose from four specialization tracks: Social Protection Policy Migration Studies Governance of Innovation Global Governance for Development The programme uses Problem-Based Learning, encouraging critical thinking, collaboration, and active participation. What You Will Learn Participants will develop the ability to: Analyze complex societal challenges Design and evaluate effective public policies Apply data-driven approaches to decision-making Develop solutions aligned with sustainable development goals Communicate policy recommendations effectively The programme also includes workshops, simulations, debates, and networking events with global experts. Programme Benefits Students benefit from a highly international and personalized learning experience. Key advantages include: Dual degree from two globally recognized institutions Small class sizes and personalized academic support Access to global networks and policy experts A dedicated employability track with mentoring and career support Strong career prospects in international organizations, governments, NGOs, and private sector institutions Graduates have gone on to work with organizations such as international development banks, UN agencies, consulting firms, and government ministries. Scholarships and Tuition Tuition fees for the 2026/2027 academic year are: Statutory fee: €2,695 Institutional fee: €21,500 The programme offers financial support options, including the MPP Scholarship , which covers the full institutional tuition fee for one outstanding non-EU applicant. Applicants are encouraged to explore additional funding options such as the Commonwealth Scholarship. Programme Details Programme start date: September 1, 2026 Duration: 1 year (full-time) Location: Maastricht, Netherlands Application Deadlines Non-EU/EEA applicants (requiring a visa): May 1, 2026 EU applicants: June 1, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early to secure admission and explore scholarship opportunities. Who Should Apply The programme welcomes applicants from diverse academic and professional backgrounds, including recent graduates and mid-career professionals. It is ideal for individuals interested in: Public policy International development Governance and global affairs Economic and social policy How to Apply Interested candidates can learn more about the programme and submit their application through the official Maastricht University portal. Prospective students are advised to carefully review admission requirements and prepare all necessary documents before applying. For more Scholarship opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Women’s Impact Award 2026: Global Call for Women Researchers Driving Scientific Excellence and Societal Impact
summary: The Women’s Impact Award 2026, presented by the Falling Walls Foundation in collaboration with the Elsevier Foundation, is a prestigious global recognition designed to celebrate outstanding women researchers whose work combines scientific excellence with measurable societal impact. As part of the broader Global Call for Science Breakthroughs 2026, this award highlights transformative research that addresses pressing global challenges while promoting equity, inclusion, and sustainable development. It is an opportunity for leading women scientists and researchers worldwide to gain international recognition, expand their influence, and contribute to shaping a more equitable future. About the Women’s Impact Award The Women’s Impact Award is a flagship initiative that honors women who are not only advancing scientific knowledge but also applying their research to create tangible, real-world change. The award specifically recognizes: Scientific excellence at the highest level Research with clear societal relevance and impact Contributions that advance equity, inclusion, and justice Leadership in addressing global and community-level challenges This initiative is part of the Falling Walls Foundation’s mission to identify and celebrate breakthroughs in science that have the potential to change the world. About the Global Call for Science Breakthroughs 2026 The Global Call for Science Breakthroughs is an international platform that invites nominations and applications from leading researchers across disciplines. It provides a stage for groundbreaking work that pushes the boundaries of knowledge and contributes to solving critical global issues. The Women’s Impact Award is a dedicated category within this global call, focusing specifically on women and gender-diverse researchers. What the Award Recognizes The Women’s Impact Award celebrates research projects that combine academic rigor with societal relevance. Applicants must demonstrate that their work contributes meaningfully to one or more of the following areas: 1. Advancing Gender Equality through Research Eligible projects may include: Research addressing gender bias in fields such as artificial intelligence, healthcare, education, finance, or employment Studies that incorporate sex and gender differences in data collection, analysis, and interpretation Projects using gender-sensitive methodologies, including: Gender-disaggregated data Co-design approaches involving women and gender-diverse populations Interdisciplinary research that promotes inclusive innovation 2. Supporting Underserved and Marginalized Communities Applicants may also qualify if their research focuses on empowering underserved populations through: Participatory research involving communities such as: Refugees Rural populations People with disabilities Low-income groups Development of technologies, policies, or interventions that improve: Access to healthcare Educational opportunities Justice and legal systems Safety and protection Economic inclusion Research outcomes that actively reduce inequality or dismantle systemic barriers Projects are not required to focus exclusively on women or girls, but they must clearly demonstrate a commitment to equity, inclusion, and positive societal transformation. Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the Women’s Impact Award 2026, applicants must meet the following requirements: 1. Career Level Applicants must be at: Postdoctoral level or above, or An equivalent level of professional independence and responsibility Eligible roles include: Postdoctoral researchers Research fellows Senior researchers Principal investigators Group leaders Professors 2. Inclusive Eligibility The award embraces diversity and inclusivity. Applications are welcome from: Cisgender women Transgender women Genderqueer individuals Non-binary individuals This inclusive approach ensures representation across diverse gender identities within the scientific community. Research Project Requirements Applicants must be actively engaged in a research project at the time of submission. Eligible Project Stages: Data collection or analysis Pilot or testing phases Implementation in real-world settings Ineligible Projects: Projects at the idea stage Concepts under development Proposals seeking funding without active implementation Language Requirement: All applications must be submitted in English Who Can Nominate Nominations for the Women’s Impact Award 2026 can be submitted by a wide range of institutions and individuals, including: Universities and higher education institutions Research organizations and institutes Companies with research laboratories Scientific foundations Partner organizations of the Falling Walls Foundation Leading scientists and experts in relevant fields Applicants may also apply directly, depending on the nomination pathway. Benefits of the Women’s Impact Award While specific prize details may vary, recipients of the Women’s Impact Award typically benefit from: Global recognition at a prestigious international platform Visibility among leading scientists, policymakers, and innovators Opportunities to expand professional networks Increased credibility and influence in their field Access to future collaborations and funding opportunities Notable Past Winners The Women’s Impact Award has previously recognized exceptional researchers whose work has made significant contributions to science and society. Some recent awardees include: Ângela Gonçalves (German Cancer Research Center) Colette Wabnitz (Stanford Center for Ocean Solutions; University of British Columbia) Omneya Attallah (Arab Academy for Science, Technology, and Maritime Transport) These winners exemplify the caliber of research and impact the award seeks to celebrate. Application Process Interested candidates are encouraged to apply or be nominated through the Official Global Call for Science Breakthroughs platform. Steps to Apply: Prepare detailed information about your research project Demonstrate scientific excellence and societal impact Submit your application in English Ensure your project meets all eligibility criteria Application Deadline: 15-Apr-2026 Why You Should Apply The Women’s Impact Award 2026 offers a unique opportunity to: Showcase your research on a global stage Gain recognition for impactful scientific work Contribute to advancing gender equality and inclusion Join a network of leading researchers and innovators Strengthen your academic and professional profile Learn more about the Women’s Impact Award 2026 HERE. Click HERE to discover more global Award opportunities on OFY. title: Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town
summary: If you are a young leader passionate about creating real change in your community, the Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 offers one of the most prestigious fully funded opportunities to gain global exposure, leadership training, and international recognition. This scholarship, offered by One Young World in partnership with the Capital City of Podgorica, provides a unique pathway to attend the globally acclaimed One Young World Summit 2026 . About the Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 The Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship is a highly competitive, fully funded opportunity designed to support one exceptional young leader from Podgorica to participate in a global leadership experience. Podgorica, one of Europe’s youngest capitals and a future European Youth Capital 2028, is actively investing in youth empowerment, innovation, and civic participation. Through this initiative, selected scholars will join thousands of global changemakers at the One Young World Summit, where leaders from over 190 countries gather to address pressing global challenges such as: Climate change and environmental protection Social inequality and education Youth participation and governance Entrepreneurship and innovation What Makes the One Young World Summit So Valuable? The One Young World Summit 2026 is one of the most influential global gatherings of young leaders. It brings together: Entrepreneurs and innovators Activists and policy advocates Students and professionals Social impact leaders Participants gain access to world-class mentorship, networking opportunities, and leadership development programs that can transform their careers and amplify their impact globally. Scholarship Benefits (Fully Funded Package) The Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 provides comprehensive financial and professional support, including: 1. Full Access to the Global Summit Participation in all sessions of the One Young World Summit 2026 2. Travel Coverage Round-trip international flights (economy class) 3. Accommodation Hotel stay from 2 November to 7 November 2026 4. Meals and Catering Breakfast at the hotel Lunch and dinner during the Summit 5. Local Transportation Transport between accommodation and Summit venue 6. Visa Support Visa application costs and related travel expenses covered 7. Leadership Development Opportunities Access to the One Young World Global Leadership Programme Participation in the Action Accelerator Lifetime membership in the One Young World Ambassador Community Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates) This opportunity is highly specific and targeted. Applicants must meet all the following requirements: Geographic Eligibility Must be a national of Montenegro Must be living and actively contributing in Podgorica Age Requirement Must be between 18 and 30 years old by November 2026 Language Requirement Must have a strong working knowledge of English Availability Must be available full-time from 2–7 November 2026 Target Audience (Who This Opportunity Is For) This scholarship is ideal for: Students Young professionals Social entrepreneurs Activists and advocates Innovators and community leaders Applicants must demonstrate a strong commitment to creating impact in areas such as: Environmental sustainability Youth engagement and participation Cultural development Digital innovation Education and equality Entrepreneurship Selection Criteria (How Winners Are Chosen) The selection process is rigorous and based on multiple impact-driven factors: 1. Proven Impact Applicants must show measurable contributions to positive change in Podgorica or Montenegro. 2. Community Engagement Evidence of active participation in: Volunteering Civic initiatives Youth programs 3. Academic or Professional Excellence Strong achievements in education, career, or extracurricular activities. 4. Vision for Future Impact A clear plan for how the Summit experience will be used to benefit the local community. 5. Leadership Potential Ability to represent Podgorica on a global stage and act as a youth ambassador. 6. Alignment with Global Values Commitment to building a fair, sustainable, and inclusive future. Important Dates Applications Opened: 29 January 2026 Summit Dates: 3–6 November 2026 Missing the deadline automatically disqualifies your application, so early submission is strongly recommended. Why You Should Apply This is more than just a scholarship—it is a career-defining opportunity. Scholars gain: Global recognition and credibility Access to an elite international network Exposure to world leaders and changemakers Opportunities for future funding and collaborations Long-term leadership development According to program insights, participants return with stronger networks, enhanced skills, and greater capacity to drive impact locally and globally. Apply here Deadline 23rd March 2026 The Capital City of Podgorica Scholarship 2026 stands out as a highly prestigious, fully funded opportunity for young leaders committed to meaningful change. However, it is geographically restricted to Montenegro, making it extremely competitive within that pool. title: Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026: €60,000 Award for Global Leaders Advancing Gender Equality
summary: The Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026 is a prestigious international award presented by the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe (PACE), in partnership with the Government of Iceland. This highly respected prize recognizes and rewards outstanding individuals, organizations, and initiatives that have demonstrated exceptional commitment to advancing women’s empowerment and gender equality across the world. Named after Vigdís Finnbogadóttir, the first woman in history to be democratically elected as a Head of State, this award celebrates transformative leadership, inclusive policies, and impactful actions that promote the full and equal participation of women and girls in all spheres of life. With a monetary award of 60,000 euros and global recognition, the Vigdís Prize stands as one of the most significant honors dedicated to gender equality and women’s rights. About the Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment Launched in 2023, the Vigdís Prize aims to spotlight initiatives and leaders who are making meaningful contributions toward empowering women and girls in diverse contexts. The award specifically recognizes efforts that: Advance gender equality at local, national, or global levels Promote equal participation in political, social, and economic decision-making Foster inclusive policies and practices Address systemic barriers affecting women and girls Support diversity and inclusion across all communities At a time when gender equality continues to face challenges globally, this prize serves as a powerful platform to highlight impactful work and inspire further progress. Prize Value and Recognition The Vigdís Prize offers both financial and symbolic recognition to its winner: A monetary award of 60,000 euros A prestigious trophy International recognition and visibility Opportunity to present work on a global stage The award ceremony takes place in Strasbourg, France, during the Summer Session of the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe. Key Dates for 2026 Applicants and nominators should take note of the following timeline: 21 November 2025: Call for candidates opened 27 March 2026: Deadline for submission of nominations May 2026: First meeting of the Selection Panel in Reykjavik 21 May 2026: Shortlisting of three candidates 21 June 2026: Final selection of the winner in Strasbourg 22 June 2026: Official award ceremony Who Can Be Nominated The Vigdís Prize is open to a wide range of nominees who are actively contributing to women’s empowerment. Eligible nominees include: Individuals Civil society organizations Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Associations Private companies Both national and international candidates are eligible, reflecting the global scope of the award. Areas of Impact Recognized Nominees should demonstrate significant achievements in one or more of the following areas: 1. Advancing Gender Equality Promoting equal rights and opportunities for women and girls Addressing discrimination and gender-based inequalities Supporting inclusive governance and leadership 2. Empowering Women and Girls Initiatives that enhance access to education, healthcare, and economic opportunities Programs that support women’s leadership and participation Actions that protect women’s rights and dignity 3. Promoting Inclusive Participation Encouraging women’s involvement in decision-making processes Creating platforms for marginalized voices Strengthening representation across sectors Nomination Requirements To successfully submit a nomination for the Vigdís Prize 2026, the following requirements must be met: 1. Nomination Form Complete the Official Nomination Form Provide detailed information about the nominee and their work 2. Language Applications must be submitted in either English or French 3. Sponsorship Requirement Each nomination must be supported by at least five sponsors Sponsors must provide their signatures using the official sponsors’ form 4. Submission Format All documents must be compiled into a single PDF file File naming format: For organizations: NAME – Country For individuals: FAMILY NAME – First Name – Country 5. Submission Process Upload the complete application package through the official submission platform Selection Process The selection of the Vigdís Prize winner is carried out by an independent Selection Panel composed of experts in women’s empowerment. Composition of the Panel: Six independent members Three nominated by the Parliamentary Assembly Three nominated by the Government of Iceland Evaluation Criteria: Demonstrated impact on women’s empowerment Innovation and sustainability of the initiative Contribution to gender equality and inclusion Measurable outcomes and long-term potential Award Ceremony The Vigdís Prize 2026 will be awarded during an official ceremony held in Strasbourg, France, on 22 June 2026, as part of the Summer Session of the Parliamentary Assembly. The winner will be announced by the President of the Parliamentary Assembly, providing global recognition and visibility. Previous Winners The Vigdís Prize has already recognized impactful initiatives across the world: 2025 Winner: Women of the Sun (Palestine) – recognized for their work in women’s rights and peacebuilding 2024 Winner: Irida Women’s Center (Greece) – supporting women facing poverty, social exclusion, and gender-based violence These examples highlight the diversity and impact of initiatives honored by the prize. Why You Should Apply or Nominate The Vigdís Prize offers a unique opportunity to: Gain international recognition for impactful work Secure financial support to expand initiatives Join a global network of changemakers Amplify efforts in advancing gender equality Inspire others through proven impact How to Apply To apply or nominate a candidate: Carefully review the Official regulations Complete the Nomination form Gather at least five Sponsor signatures Compile all documents into one PDF Submit via the Official Platform before the deadline Deadline All nominations must be submitted by 27 March 2026. Late submissions will not be considered. Learn more about the Vigdís Prize for Women’s Empowerment 2026 HERE. Click HERE for more global Award opportunities on Opportunities for Youth. title: Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 (Fully Funded): A Life-Changing Opportunity for Young Leaders in Africa, Asia and the Middle East to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town
summary: The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 is one of the most prestigious fully funded global leadership opportunities designed for young changemakers who are actively transforming their communities. Offered by One Young World , this scholarship provides selected candidates with a fully funded chance to attend the globally renowned One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town . About the Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship was created to empower emerging leaders who are addressing some of the most pressing global challenges affecting women, girls, and vulnerable populations. The initiative is named after Hauwa Ojeifo , a globally recognized advocate for mental health and gender equality. The scholarship aims to build a new generation of leaders equipped with the tools, networks, and global exposure needed to drive meaningful change in their communities. It focuses particularly on social impact areas such as mental health, gender-based violence, and reproductive health rights. What is the One Young World Summit? The One Young World Summit is a prestigious global event that gathers outstanding young leaders from across the world. Delegates participate in high-level discussions, networking sessions, and leadership training alongside global influencers, policymakers, and industry leaders. Participants also gain lifetime membership in the One Young World Ambassador community, enabling long-term collaboration and impact. Fully Funded Benefits of the Scholarship The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 provides a comprehensive funding package that removes financial barriers and ensures full participation: Full access to the One Young World Summit 2026 Round-trip airfare to Cape Town Hotel accommodation for the entire duration of the summit Daily meals (breakfast, lunch, and dinner) Ground transportation between accommodation and event venue Lifetime membership in the One Young World Ambassador network Access to leadership programs and global initiatives Opportunity to apply for funding of up to $25,000 for social impact projects Fields and Focus Areas Applicants must be actively working in at least one of the following impact areas: Mental health and psychosocial support Gender-based violence and domestic abuse prevention Sexual and reproductive health rights Maternal and child health Disability rights and inclusion Advocacy for the girl child (e.g., child marriage, early pregnancy) Non-communicable diseases These focus areas align with global development priorities and aim to support initiatives that create sustainable social impact. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Categories of Applicants) This scholarship is designed for: Young leaders and changemakers Social entrepreneurs and nonprofit founders Community organizers and activists Professionals working in public health, gender equality, or human rights Individuals leading grassroots or national-level initiatives Eligibility Criteria (Detailed Requirements) To qualify for the Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026, applicants must meet the following criteria: 1. Age Requirement Must be between 18 and 30 years old Candidates above 30 may be considered if they demonstrate exceptional impact 2. Leadership and Impact Must hold a leadership role or lead a project/initiative Demonstrated measurable impact in their community 3. Thematic Alignment Work must align with at least one of the scholarship’s focus areas 4. Geographic Eligibility Applicants must live and create impact in Africa, Asia, or the Middle East 5. Language Requirement Proficiency in English (spoken and written) 6. Commitment Must be available to attend the summit and actively participate in all sessions Why You Should Apply for This Scholarship This opportunity goes beyond financial support. It offers: Global exposure and recognition Access to influential leaders and mentors Opportunities for funding and scaling your project A powerful international network of changemakers Personal and professional development through leadership training The scholarship is particularly impactful for individuals who are already making a difference but lack the global platform to amplify their work. Apply here Deadline 27th March 2026 The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship 2026 stands out as a transformative opportunity for young leaders dedicated to advancing gender equality, mental health awareness, and social justice. By removing financial barriers and connecting changemakers to a global platform, One Young World continues to empower the next generation of leaders who are shaping the future. title: Apply for Youth Leaders for Peace Fellowship (YL4P) 2026 in India — Build Skills for Community Impact
summary: Are you an international-minded young leader working to create change in your community? The Youth Leaders for Peace Fellowship (YL4P) – India Chapter offers a unique opportunity to strengthen your leadership, expand your impact, and connect with a national network of changemakers committed to social justice and peacebuilding. Organized by the White Dot Solidarity Network, this fellowship is designed for young people across India who are already engaged in social action and are looking to deepen their skills, knowledge, and long-term impact. Through a structured online programme, participants will gain practical tools to sustain and elevate their work in areas such as human rights, climate change, gender justice, and community development. About the Fellowship The YL4P is a one-month intensive training programme that combines interactive learning sessions with hands-on community engagement. Running from 1 May to 1 July 2026 , the fellowship is delivered entirely online via Zoom, making it accessible to participants across India. The programme includes five structured training sessions held weekly, each lasting two hours. These sessions are complemented by a month-long community engagement assignment, where fellows apply their learning to real-world challenges within their communities. A distinctive feature of the fellowship is its intergenerational learning approach , where each session is co-facilitated by both youth experts and senior practitioners. This allows participants to gain diverse perspectives, practical insights, and mentorship from experienced leaders. The fellowship is supported by the Youth Empowerment Fund of Global Youth Mobilisation, funded by the European Union and a consortium of global youth organizations. Why This Opportunity Matters Young leaders working in social impact often face challenges such as limited resources, lack of structured training, and minimal access to networks. The YL4P Fellowship directly addresses these gaps by providing both skills and support systems. By combining training, mentorship, funding support, and networking, the programme equips participants to sustain and scale their work more effectively. It also creates a platform for collaboration among youth leaders across different regions and sectors. For individuals committed to creating meaningful change, this fellowship offers a practical pathway to strengthen both leadership capacity and community impact. What Participants Will Gain One of the strongest aspects of the YL4P Fellowship is its focus on practical, real-world impact rather than purely theoretical learning. Participants will gain structured training in key areas such as leadership, advocacy, research, fundraising, and digital security. These skills are essential for individuals working in social impact spaces and help strengthen both personal and organizational effectiveness. A major benefit of the programme is the community engagement assignment , where fellows design and implement a project related to their area of work. This hands-on component allows participants to directly apply their learning while making a tangible difference in their communities. Fellows will also receive an honorarium to support the costs associated with their community projects, making it easier to implement meaningful initiatives. In addition, participants gain access to ongoing mentorship , which helps them refine their ideas, overcome challenges, and strengthen their long-term impact strategies. Upon successful completion, fellows receive a certificate and become part of the White Dot Solidarity Network’s pan-India community of youth leaders. This network provides opportunities for collaboration, visibility, and future engagement. Who Can Apply The YL4P fellowship is open to young leaders who meet the following criteria: Aged between 18 and 35 years Currently residing in India Have 1–3 years of experience in community work (including volunteering, internships, or civil society engagement) Actively involved in a social intervention, initiative, or community-based project Demonstrate leadership and commitment to community development Have a working knowledge of English Programme Structure and Modules The YL4P fellowship includes weekly sessions covering key areas such as: Youth leadership and ethical community engagement Research methods and documentation Human rights frameworks and advocacy Resource mobilization and fundraising Digital security and protection practices The programme concludes with a project presentation session, followed by the final submission of community project reports. Application Process and Deadline Interested applicants must complete the online application form and s ubmit all required information before the deadline. Application Deadline: 10 April 2026 Incomplete applications will not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to review their submissions carefully before applying. Apply through the official application form . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one?
