title: Apply Now: Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 2026 for Educators Using AI
summary: The Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious global program designed to empower academic professionals to lead the transformation of higher education through artificial intelligence. As institutions worldwide navigate the rapid integration of AI into teaching, research, and administration, this fellowship positions faculty members not just as adopters of technology, but as strategic leaders shaping the future of education . This comprehensive guide explores every aspect of the fellowship—from its purpose and benefits to the step-by-step application process—helping you understand how to become part of this influential global community. Overview: Shaping the Future of AI in Higher Education The Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship is built for forward-thinking educators who are already experimenting with or implementing AI in their academic work. Rather than focusing solely on instructional techniques, the program emphasizes institutional transformation, policy development, and innovation at scale . Participants join a global network of academic pioneers committed to: Advancing AI-driven research and discovery Enhancing student learning experiences Developing responsible AI governance frameworks Leading systemic change across higher education institutions The fellowship is especially suited for faculty members who want to transition from individual innovation to institution-wide and global impact . Fellowship Objectives and Vision The core goal of the program is to cultivate a new generation of academic leaders who can: Strategically integrate AI into higher education systems Address complex challenges such as academic integrity, assessment redesign, and ethical AI use Influence institutional and global conversations around AI in education Fellows are expected to act as change agents , bridging the gap between emerging technologies and real-world academic applications. Key Benefits of the Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 1. Strategic Leadership Opportunities Fellows gain a platform to lead conversations and initiatives around AI adoption within their institutions. This includes tackling high-level challenges such as: Redesigning assessment models in the age of AI Establishing governance and ethical guidelines Integrating AI into institutional strategy 2. Global Recognition and Ambassador Role Participants are positioned as AI Ambassadors , representing their institutions while guiding peers through the evolving AI landscape. This recognition enhances both academic credibility and professional visibility. 3. Access to a Global Network The fellowship connects participants with a curated community of educators, researchers, and innovators from around the world. This network enables: Cross-institutional collaboration Knowledge sharing on AI applications Joint research and innovation opportunities 4. Professional Growth and Skill Development Fellows develop advanced competencies in: AI tools and applications for education Research innovation using AI Strategic decision-making and leadership These skills help participants remain competitive and influential in a rapidly evolving academic environment. 5. Institutional Impact Beyond individual growth, fellows bring value back to their institutions by: Leading AI-driven innovation projects Enhancing teaching and learning systems Ensuring their campuses remain at the forefront of technological advancement How Fellows Make a Difference Leading Institutional Transformation Fellows are empowered to address critical challenges within their universities or colleges. This could involve: Developing AI policies and frameworks Transforming curriculum design Improving student outcomes through AI integration Amplifying Professional Impact Through collaboration and visibility, fellows expand their influence beyond their institutions, contributing to global discussions on AI in higher education. Driving Innovation Across Higher Education The fellowship encourages participants to think beyond their immediate environment and contribute to system-wide advancements in education. 2026 Fellowship Program Details The 2026 cohort marks the inaugural intake of the Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship, making it a unique opportunity to be among the first group of global pioneers in this space. Fellowship Timeline Applications are currently open for the 2026 cohort The program is structured across multiple phases throughout the year (Q1 to Q3) Selected fellows will participate in a series of collaborative, research, and leadership activities during this period Applicants are encouraged to prepare early to ensure they meet all requirements and deadlines. Step-by-Step Application Process Admission into the fellowship is highly competitive and curated, targeting faculty who have already demonstrated meaningful engagement with AI. Step 1: Initiate Your Candidacy There are two pathways to apply: Nomination Route : Receive a formal invitation through nomination by a Google leader Direct Application : Submit your application independently through the official platform This dual-entry system ensures both recognition of established leaders and openness to emerging innovators. Step 2: Demonstrate Your Expertise Applicants must submit a Portfolio of Innovation , which may include: AI-related academic projects Research publications Redesigned courses incorporating AI Case studies or practical implementations This portfolio serves as evidence of your ability to apply AI meaningfully in academic settings. Step 3: Propose an Impact Challenge A critical component of the application is identifying a real-world challenge you intend to address during the fellowship. This challenge should be relevant to: Assessment and evaluation methods Institutional AI policy or governance Student learning outcomes The goal is to ensure that every fellow contributes to solving a tangible and impactful problem. Step 4: Submit a Letter of Recommendation Applicants must include a formal endorsement from a: Dean Program Chair The letter should: Support your participation in the fellowship Highlight your leadership potential Confirm institutional backing for your proposed work Who Should Apply? The fellowship is ideal for: University and college faculty members Academic researchers integrating AI into their work Educators leading curriculum innovation Institutional leaders interested in AI policy and strategy Applicants should already have hands-on experience with AI and a clear vision for how it can transform higher education. Why This Fellowship Matters Artificial intelligence is reshaping every aspect of higher education—from how students learn to how institutions operate. However, the success of this transformation depends on informed, ethical, and strategic leadership . The Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship addresses this need by: Empowering educators to lead AI adoption responsibly Building a global community of experts Driving innovation that benefits both institutions and learners By participating, fellows play a direct role in shaping the future of education on a global scale. In addition to this fellowship, there are numerous programs and communities available for educators at different stages of their careers. Engaging with such initiatives can further expand your impact, network, and expertise in AI and education. The Higher Ed Faculty AI Fellowship 2026 is more than a professional development program—it is a leadership platform for transforming higher education through AI. With its focus on innovation, collaboration, and real-world impact, the fellowship offers a unique opportunity to influence the direction of academic institutions worldwide. If you are an educator already exploring AI and looking to scale your impact, this fellowship provides the structure, network, and recognition needed to take your work to the next level. title: UNCCD Youth Caucus Call for Youth Consultants 2026 (Paid Opportunity for Young Researchers)
summary: The UNCCD Youth Caucus is inviting applications for youth consultants to support a global assessment on youth engagement in the implementation of the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD). This is a paid, short-term consultancy opportunity running from March to June 2026. It is designed for young professionals with experience in research, data collection, and youth engagement who are interested in contributing to global environmental policy processes. About the Opportunity The UNCCD Youth Caucus aims to: Assess youth engagement in the implementation of the UNCCD Evaluate the effectiveness of the Youth Engagement Strategy Identify best practices and lessons learned Provide recommendations to strengthen meaningful youth participation Contribute to a report submitted to the Conference of the Parties (COP) under Decision 5/COP 16 Selected consultants will contribute to shaping evidence-based recommendations that inform global decision-making on youth participation in climate and environmental governance. Key Responsibilities Selected youth consultants will: Support the development and circulation of surveys Organise and facilitate consultations with young people Conduct interviews and focus group discussions Provide rapporteur support during meetings and consultations Assist with data analysis, synthesis, and visualization Contribute to drafting the final report for submission to COP Benefits Paid consultancy (details shared with shortlisted candidates) Experience working within a UN-related global process Opportunity to contribute to high-level policy and reporting Strengthening of research, facilitation, and analytical skills Engagement with an international network of youth leaders and experts Eligibility Criteria Applicants should have: Experience in data collection, analysis, and visualization Strong research and writing skills (with work samples) Knowledge or interest in youth engagement within UN processes (UNCCD experience is an advantage) A proactive mindset with the ability to contribute innovative ideas Ability to work collaboratively in a diverse, global team Age requirement: Applicants must be below 35 years old Timeline Consultancy period: March to June 2026 Application: Open now Why You Should Apply This opportunity provides a platform to: Influence global environmental and climate policy discussions Support evidence-based youth engagement strategies Build experience within international development and UN processes Contribute to advancing meaningful youth participation globally How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official application form provided by the UNCCD Youth Caucus. Application link: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSex6WbmJ_xgSO0Z_XK7gpchoYn2F0fJTFcOFL3qWzfqWi2M5Q/viewform Explore More Opportunities For more scholarships, internships, fellowships, and grants: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org The post UNCCD Youth Caucus Call for Youth Consultants 2026 (Paid Opportunity for Young Researchers) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Fully Funded MOOD for Climate Action Program 2026 (Global Youth Opportunity)
summary: Meaningful Open Opportunities for Discovery (MOOD) for Climate Action is a transformative, fully funded program designed to empower young people to take meaningful action against climate change. This experiential learning journey equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and confidence needed to move from climate anxiety to climate action—while building impactful projects and joining a global network of changemakers. From Climate Anxiety to Climate Action Climate change is no longer a distant concern—it is a present and urgent global challenge. Yet, many educators and young people feel unprepared to address it effectively. Studies show that while most teachers recognize the importance of climate education, fewer than half feel equipped to teach it. At the same time, a significant majority of young people express fear about the future and concern over global inaction. The MOOD for Climate Action program responds to this gap by going beyond theory. It focuses on empowering learners not only to understand climate challenges but also to take meaningful, localized action while managing the emotional impact of the crisis. Rather than viewing climate change solely as a crisis, this program reframes it as an opportunity—for self-discovery, collaboration, innovation, and leadership. What the MOOD Journey Offers This program is a highly scalable and immersive learning experience designed to build agency and real-world impact. Participants will: Discover their personal purpose and how they can contribute to the world Gain a systemic understanding of climate challenges and solutions Take action on a local climate-related issue Build essential lifelong skills, including: Systems thinking Leadership and collaboration Social entrepreneurship Critical thinking Cross-cultural teamwork Emotional intelligence and regulation Project design and management Join a global community of like-minded young changemakers Enhance their CV with a project showcase and certification Each completed module earns a certificate issued by a United Nations University (UNU) Hub , adding strong international recognition to participants’ profiles. Who Can Apply? The MOOD for Climate Action program is open to: Young adults aged 15 to 35 years Individuals available for at least 5 hours per week Those passionate about climate action and personal development Applicants willing to actively participate in collaborative projects Individuals with a stable internet connection for virtual sessions Participants with proficiency in English for global collaboration Program Structure: 3 Certified Modules Participants can choose to complete one, two, or all three modules depending on their availability and goals. Each module includes certification upon completion. Key Highlights: Each module = 1 certification from a UNU Hub Flexible participation based on your schedule Top-performing projects receive incubation support from the INCO incubator to ensure long-term sustainability A Holistic Learning Experience The MOOD program is built around three core dimensions: 1. Thematic (Knowledge) Participants explore: Causes and impacts of climate change Climate justice and inequalities Sustainable solutions and carbon reduction strategies Adaptation approaches across different global regions Systems thinking and long-term climate foresight 2. Socio-Emotional (Self) This dimension focuses on personal development through: Self-awareness exercises Emotional regulation techniques Group collaboration and communication skills 3. Action (Impact) Participants gain hands-on experience by: Identifying local climate challenges Conducting fieldwork and interviews Designing and refining solutions Developing entrepreneurial and project implementation skills Pedagogical Infrastructure Learning Experience The program combines flexibility with high-quality support and interactive learning tools. Workload 12 hours of live workshops and mentoring per module Minimum 24 hours total commitment per module Flexible schedule compatible with studies, jobs, or internships Expert Support Participants learn from a diverse team of experts, including: Psychologists (for emotional and mental resilience) Social entrepreneurs (for project development) Climatologists (for scientific insights) Dedicated mentors for project-based modules Digital Learning Platform Interactive, AI-enhanced learning environment Real-time collaboration with global peers Continuous community engagement and support Certification Career Benefits Upon completing each module, participants receive a certification from a United Nations University Hub in France , enhancing their academic and professional profiles. Additionally, participants: Build a portfolio-ready climate project Gain international exposure Access opportunities for project incubation and scaling Why You Should Apply If you are passionate about climate action and want to move beyond awareness into real-world impact, this program provides the tools, network, and support you need. “In the MOOD for Climate Action” is more than a course—it’s a launchpad for the next generation of climate leaders. How to Apply Interested candidates can: Apply to the standard program Apply for a fully funded cohort Register Here, to begin their journey For more information, applicants are encouraged to visit the official program page or contact the organizers directly. Climate change demands urgent and collective action. Programs like MOOD for Climate Action are bridging the gap between knowledge and action by empowering young people with the skills, mindset, and opportunities needed to drive real change. If you are ready to become a climate changemaker, this could be your moment. Visit here for more details about the Learning institute. Discover more similar opportunities by clicking Here . title: Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026: Paid Eight-Week Fellowship in the Office of the Governor for Future Public Service Leaders
summary: The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for students passionate about public service, policy development, and government leadership to gain first-hand experience working within the Office of the Governor of Virginia . Designed to cultivate the next generation of public leaders, this prestigious program provides selected fellows with an immersive eight-week professional experience inside the Governor’s Administration . Participants contribute to real policy initiatives, support daily government operations, and collaborate with experienced policymakers while building valuable professional skills. The program provides an inside look at how state government functions and gives participants an opportunity to engage with the processes that shape policies impacting millions of citizens across the Commonwealth of Virginia. Participants also receive a stipend for their two-month service , making this fellowship both a professional development opportunity and a paid experience in public service. Overview of the Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program The Governor’s Fellows Program places participants directly within the Governor’s Office and related administrative units , where they assist with key initiatives that support governance and public administration. Fellows work closely with policy advisors, senior administrators, and other government professionals while contributing to projects that advance the priorities of the Commonwealth. This program provides fellows with: Hands-on experience in state government operations Exposure to public policy development and implementation Professional mentorship from government leaders Opportunities to collaborate with fellow participants on impactful policy initiatives The program is designed to equip participants with the knowledge, skills, and professional connections necessary for careers in public service, policy, law, governance, and civic leadership . Fellowship Structure and Learning Components The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program combines practical work experience with structured professional development activities . Fellows not only contribute to daily operations but also complete several program components that deepen their understanding of government and policy. 1. Office Placement and Administrative Work Each fellow is placed within an office in the Governor’s Administration based on their academic background, interests, and professional goals. In their assigned offices, fellows may assist with: Policy research and analysis Administrative and operational support Legislative and policy briefings Public service initiatives Special projects relevant to the administration’s priorities This placement ensures that participants gain meaningful exposure to how state government functions on a day-to-day basis. 2. Professional Portfolio Development During the fellowship, participants develop a professional portfolio that highlights their work and growth throughout the program. The portfolio may include: Research outputs Policy analysis contributions Reports or briefings completed during the program Reflections on professional development and learning experiences This portfolio serves as a valuable professional asset that fellows can use in future career and academic opportunities. 3. Policy Project Another central component of the fellowship is the policy project . Fellows work in small teams to research and develop solutions for policy issues affecting the Commonwealth of Virginia. The policy project includes: Identifying a relevant policy challenge Conducting research and data analysis Developing policy recommendations Presenting findings to administration officials At the conclusion of the fellowship, fellows present their projects in a formal policy briefing , demonstrating their analytical skills and ability to develop actionable solutions. Key Benefits of the Program The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program offers numerous benefits that support participants’ academic, professional, and leadership development. Professional Experience in Government Participants gain valuable insights into the inner workings of state government , policy development, and administrative operations. Paid Fellowship Experience Each fellow receives a stipend for their two-month service to the Commonwealth of Virginia. Policy Development Experience Through the policy project and office assignments, fellows develop practical skills in: Policy analysis Government research Strategic problem solving Public sector communication Networking and Mentorship Fellows have opportunities to connect with: Senior government officials Policy advisors Public service leaders Fellow students interested in governance and public administration These connections can help launch careers in government, policy, law, and public leadership . Who Should Apply The Governor’s Fellows Program welcomes applicants who demonstrate strong commitment to public service and leadership. Eligible applicants include: Rising college seniors Graduating seniors Graduate students Applicants must also meet one of the following conditions: Be enrolled at a Virginia college or university , or Be a Virginia resident studying at an out-of-state institution Applicants should demonstrate: Academic excellence Leadership potential Civic engagement and community service Participation in extracurricular activities and initiatives The program seeks candidates who are motivated, thoughtful, and eager to contribute to the work of the Commonwealth. Program Timeline Applicants should carefully review the timeline for the 2026 fellowship cycle. Application Deadline April 10, 2026 All application materials must be submitted by this date. Late or incomplete submissions will not be considered. Interview Phase April 21, 2026 Selected applicants will participate in virtual interviews with program officials. Final Decision Notifications April 30, 2026 All applicants will receive notification regarding their application status. Fellowship Program Dates June 8 – July 31, 2026 The fellowship is conducted full-time and in person during this period. Participants must be available for the entire duration of the program. Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package through the official online portal. Required application materials include: Completed online application form Résumé outlining academic and professional experience Official or unofficial academic transcript Two letters of recommendation from academic or professional references Essay explaining commitment to public service and goals for participation Selection of up to three areas of interest for placement consideration These materials allow the selection committee to evaluate candidates based on their qualifications, leadership potential, and commitment to public service. Selection Process Fellows are selected through a competitive merit-based review process . The program follows equal opportunity principles and does not discriminate based on: Race Sex Color National origin Religion Sexual orientation Gender identity Age Political affiliation Disability Veteran status Applicants are evaluated based on academic performance, leadership potential, civic engagement, and commitment to public service. Career Impact of the Governor’s Fellows Program Many past fellows credit the program as a launchpad for successful careers in government and policy leadership . Participants gain: Professional experience in high-level policymaking environments Mentorship from government leaders Exposure to real public policy challenges Networking opportunities that support long-term career development Several alumni have gone on to serve in influential roles within state government, federal agencies, and policy organizations . How to Apply Interested applicants can learn more and submit their application through the Official Governor of Virginia website. Applicants are encouraged to prepare their materials early and ensure all required documents are submitted before the deadline. title: Apply for the Key Habitats Program 2026 — Funding & Support for Conservation and Community Initiatives in the Caribbean
summary: Conservationists, community leaders, NGOs, and environmental project teams in the Caribbean now have a valuable opportunity to apply for the Key Habitats Program 2026 , a funding initiative designed to support projects that conserve critical ecosystems and promote sustainable, community‑driven solutions. Led by the Dutch Caribbean Nature Alliance (DCNA) , the program provides financial support, technical guidance, and collaborative networks to help protect and restore biodiversity, strengthen local conservation capacity, and integrate community needs with environmental stewardship. ( dcnanature.org ) The Key Habitats Program targets initiatives that address priority conservation challenges, empowering local stakeholders to lead environmental action while contributing to long‑term ecosystem resilience across the Dutch Caribbean. About DCNA and the Key Habitats Program The Dutch Caribbean Nature Alliance is a regional partnership of nature conservation organizations across the Dutch Caribbean islands, including Aruba, Bonaire, Curaçao, Saba, Sint Eustatius, and Sint Maarten. DCNA works to protect biodiversity, strengthen institutional capacity, and ensure sustainable use of natural resources. The Key Habitats Program serves as a strategic funding mechanism that facilitates impactful, community‑linked conservation projects. The initiative prioritizes efforts that are guided by scientific evidence and develop lasting environmental and social benefits. By providing targeted funding and tailored support, the program helps emerging and established conservation efforts realize measurable outcomes. Why This Opportunity Matters The Caribbean region is home to some of the world’s most unique and vulnerable ecosystems, including coral reefs, mangroves, and tropical forests. These habitats are crucial not only for biodiversity but also for the livelihoods, food security, and cultural heritage of communities. However, pressures such as climate change, habitat degradation, invasive species, and unsustainable use of resources threaten these ecosystems and the services they provide. The Key Habitats Program supports locally led efforts that address these challenges by strengthening institutional capacity, elevating community voices in conservation, and providing tools and resources for impactful action. For conservation practitioners, community leaders, and environmental advocates, this program offers a platform to implement meaningful change that resonates across ecological and social systems. What the Program Offers The Key Habitats Program provides a combination of financial resources, technical support, and collaborative engagement designed to elevate conservation projects from concept to action. Financial Support Selected applicants receive project funding that can be used to implement conservation activities, build capacity, or support community‑driven solutions. Funding helps cover essential project costs such as: Fieldwork and data collection Community education or outreach materials Equipment and supplies Training and facilitation activities This financial support enables organizations and initiatives to execute conservation plans that might otherwise be limited by resource constraints. Technical and Organizational Support In addition to funding, the program offers technical guidance from conservation specialists and professionals. This may include support with: Project planning and design Monitoring and evaluation Data analysis and reporting Communications and stakeholder engagement By connecting teams with technical expertise, DCNA ensures that projects are both scientifically sound and culturally relevant to local communities. Networking and Collaboration The Key Habitats Program encourages collaboration across the region. Participants gain access to: Regional conservation networks Peer learning opportunities Knowledge exchange with practitioners and experts Support from partner organizations in the Dutch Caribbean These connections help strengthen long‑term capacity and foster partnerships that extend beyond the duration of individual projects. Who Should Apply The Key Habitats Program is open to a range of applicants working on conservation and sustainability initiatives within the Dutch Caribbean region. Eligible applicants include: Nonprofit organizations engaged in conservation or community development Community groups or cooperatives with environmental projects Research teams working on habitat protection, restoration, or biodiversity studies Youth‑led or community‑led initiatives with a focus on sustainability Projects may vary in scope, but successful applications typically demonstrate a clear conservation objective, measurable outcomes, and strong community engagement. Areas of Focus and Project Priorities The Key Habitats Program prioritizes projects that contribute to the conservation of critical ecosystems and support local community resilience. Priority areas include: Habitat protection and restoration Biodiversity monitoring and research Community engagement and environmental education Sustainable livelihood integration with conservation goals Projects that link environmental outcomes to community benefits — such as sustainable fishing practices, ecotourism, or food security solutions — are particularly encouraged. By emphasizing both ecological and social dimensions, the program seeks to create holistic approaches that benefit both nature and people. Application Process and Timeline Interested applicants should review the Key Habitats Program guidelines and submit their proposals through the official DCNA application process. Applications typically require: A clear project description Objectives and measurable outcomes Budget and resource plan Letters of support or partner endorsements While DCNA publishes specific timelines and deadlines for each funding round, applicants are encouraged to prepare materials well in advance to ensure completeness and competitive quality. Application Deadline: 13 April 2026 Applicants should consult the official DCNA Key Habitats Program page for more submission details . title: Applications Open for Claude Community Ambassadors Program 2026
summary: Deadline: Rolling Applications Anthropic has opened applications for the Claude Community Ambassadors Program , a global initiative designed to support individuals who want to build and lead local communities around Claude. The program invites community builders, technical users, and AI enthusiasts to host events, share knowledge, and contribute to shaping the future of AI tools. The initiative focuses on empowering individuals to create vibrant local ecosystems where builders, developers, and curious learners can come together to explore and experiment with Claude. By supporting grassroots community efforts, Anthropic aims to expand access to AI knowledge while fostering collaboration across regions. About the Claude Community Ambassadors Program The Claude Community Ambassadors Program is built around the idea that strong communities drive innovation. Ambassadors are expected to organise meetups, workshops, and hackathons in their cities, creating spaces where people can learn, build, and exchange ideas. Participants are not required to be professional developers. While applicants should have meaningful experience using Claude tools such as Claude Code or Claude Cowork, the program places greater emphasis on community involvement and the ability to bring people together. This makes the opportunity accessible to a wide range of individuals, including educators, organisers, and tech enthusiasts. What Ambassadors Receive Selected ambassadors gain access to a range of resources to support their community-building efforts. These include: Funding support for local events such as meetups and hackathons Ready-to-use content and promotional assistance Swag and materials to enhance community engagement Monthly API credits to run demos and prototype ideas In addition, ambassadors receive early access to pre-release features and can participate in Builders Council sessions, where they share feedback directly with Anthropic’s product teams. Global and Flexible Participation The program is open to applicants from anywhere in the world, reflecting Anthropic’s focus on geographic diversity. There is no requirement to be based in a specific location, and multiple ambassadors can operate within the same city. This flexibility allows communities to grow organically across regions and ensures that participation is not limited by geography. Roles and Responsibilities Ambassadors are expected to take an active role in growing their local communities. This includes hosting events, running live demonstrations, and encouraging collaboration among participants. They are also responsible for sharing insights from their communities with Anthropic, helping shape future product development. Participants will join a private Slack network where they can connect with other ambassadors globally, exchange ideas, and collaborate on initiatives. Compensation and Benefits While the program does not provide direct financial compensation, it offers substantial support in other forms. Ambassadors receive event sponsorship, API credits, networking opportunities, promotional support, and recognition within the global AI community. These benefits are designed to help participants build impactful communities and expand their professional networks. Application Process Applications are open on a rolling basis, allowing interested individuals to apply at any time. Selected candidates will go through an onboarding process that includes signing an ambassador agreement, joining the private Slack workspace, and planning their first event. There is no cost to join the program, and all resources are provided by Anthropic. Why This Matters As AI continues to shape industries and societies, community-led initiatives play an important role in ensuring that knowledge and access are widely distributed. The Claude Community Ambassadors Program supports individuals who want to take the lead in making AI tools more accessible, collaborative, and locally relevant. For those passionate about community building and emerging technologies, the program offers a unique opportunity to contribute to a global network while creating impact at the local level. Deadline: Rolling Applications Application can be accessed from here . To know more about such opportunities, click here . title: Apply for STIAS Fellowships 2027 — Fully Funded Research Fellowships in South Africa
summary: Are you a research scholar, scientist, or artist looking for dedicated time and resources to advance your work? The Stellenbosch Institute for Advanced Study (STIAS) is now accepting applications for it s 2027 Fellowship Programme. This fully funded fellowship provides scholars and artists with the financial support, interdisciplinary community, and protected time needed to make meaningful progress on ambitious research or creative projects. Located in Stellenbosch, South Africa, STIAS brings together international and local fellows to foster collaboration, innovation, and intellectual exchange across a wide range of disciplines. Whether your work is in the sciences, humanities, arts, or social sciences, STIAS provides the environment to deepen your research, produce new work, and connect with peers and mentors who can help amplify your impact. What the STIAS Fellowship Offers Full Financial Support Selected fellows receive comprehensive funding, including: A fully equipped offic e Weekday lunch living stipend Accommodation during the fellowship period Travel to and from Stellenbosch, South Africa This funding ensures fellows can dedicate themselves fully to their work without balancing additional employment or academic duties. Protected Research or Creative Time One of the greatest benefits of a STIAS fellowship is uninterrupted focus . Fellows are relieved of teaching, administrative, or other professional obligations, allowing them to: Conduct in-depth research or experiments Write academic publications, books, or reports Develop creative or artistic projects Experiment with interdisciplinary collaborations This protected time enables scholars and artists to tackle ambitious projects that may be difficult to achieve in a typical academic or professional setting. Interdisciplinary Engagement and Networking STIAS emphasizes cross-disciplinary collaboration . Fellows interact with a diverse community of scholars, scientists, and artists, exchanging ideas and methodologies. This fosters innovation and can open doors to new perspectives and opportunities. Attend seminars and workshops led by leading experts Engage in informal peer discussions and idea exchanges Build long-term professional networks locally, regionally, and internationally Career and Academic Development Beyond research and creative outputs, STIAS fellowships offer invaluable professional development: Opportunities to collaborate on international research projects Exposure to cutting-edge ideas across disciplines Mentorship from established scholars and practitioners Enhanced CV and professional profile for future grants, positions, or exhibitions Why This Opportunity Matters STIAS fellowships offer a rare chance to dedicate uninterrupted time to high-impact research or creative practice. For scholars, this can lead to publications, grant proposals, or collaborative projects that advance knowledge in their field. For artists, it can provide the time, resources, and community support to complete ambitious creative projects or develop new artistic directions. The programme also contributes to South Africa’s academic and cultural landscape by bringing global expertise to Stellenbosch, fostering collaborations with local institutions, and enhancing cross-disciplinary dialogue. Who Should Apply The STIAS Fellowship Programme is open to both emerging and established scholars, scientists, and artists . Ideal applicants include: Researchers with a strong record of publications or projects Scientists conducting advanced studies in their field Practicing artists or creative professionals with significant work in progress Individuals who benefit from interdisciplinary engagement and mentorship Applicants from any discipline are encouraged to apply, as long as the project will benefit from dedicated time, financial support, and STIAS’s collaborative environment. Application Process and Deadline Applicants should submit: A detailed research or creative proposal CV or portfolio References or letters of support Applications are accepted until the deadline listed on the official STIAS website. Fellows are selected based on the quality and feasibility of their projects, the potential impact of their work, and the alignment with STIAS’s interdisciplinary mission . Application Deadline: 30 April 2026 Learn more and apply on the official STIAS website. title: Applications Open for Rooted and Rising: Youth Leaders Grant 2026
summary: Deadline: April 17, 2026 The Conrad Prebys Foundation has launched the Rooted and Rising: Youth Leaders Grant initiative, aimed at strengthening civic participation by investing in youth leadership programs across San Diego County. The initiative recognises that a healthy democracy depends on active engagement—yet many young people are still positioned as passive recipients rather than active contributors to change. This funding opportunity seeks to shift that narrative by supporting programs that equip young people with the skills, confidence, and platforms needed to engage meaningfully in civic life. About the Youth Leaders Grant 2026 The Rooted and Rising initiative is designed to support programs that combine leadership development with real-world civic engagement. By focusing on middle and high school-aged youth, the program aims to nurture a generation capable of shaping more inclusive and equitable communities. The foundation highlights that young people already bring creativity, resilience, and a strong desire to contribute. However, access to structured civic engagement opportunities remains uneven. Through this initiative, the foundation aims to bridge that gap and position youth as active partners in community problem-solving. In recent years, there has been growing recognition that early exposure to civic processes—such as dialogue, community organising, and collaborative decision-making—can significantly shape long-term democratic participation. Programs that engage youth at this stage not only build individual confidence but also strengthen collective capacity within communities, ensuring that future leaders are better equipped to address complex social challenges. Key Focus Areas Funded programs must integrate skill-building with practical experience. This includes: Leadership, collaboration, and problem-solving training Experiential learning tied to real community challenges such as climate resilience, arts and culture, health, and democratic participation Opportunities for youth to engage in storytelling, content creation, and visible leadership roles Strong mentorship networks connecting youth with civic leaders and career pathways A central requirement is that all programs culminate in an action project , where participants actively contribute to solving real community issues. Funding Details Grant Amount: Up to $150,000 per award Total Funding Pool: $2.5 million Number of Grants: Approximately 15–20 Grant Duration: July 2026 to December 2027 (18 months) Funding Type: Programmatic Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, fiscally sponsored organisation, or Tribal entity Operate in and/or serve San Diego County Focus on middle and/or high school-aged youth Deliver programs centred on leadership development, civic engagement, and community problem-solving Be an existing program that is expanding or strengthening its impact Programs must also demonstrate ongoing engagement rather than one-time activities and show clear outcomes such as participant numbers, skill development, and types of civic engagement activities. Program Expectations Proposals should include measurable outcomes and clearly defined program goals. Applicants are encouraged to highlight: Youth participation and leadership growth Civic engagement activities such as volunteering, advisory councils, or peer-led initiatives Use of youth-generated content like videos or social media storytelling Inclusion of diverse perspectives and community-informed approaches Preference will be given to programs that involve youth as co-designers or decision-makers and demonstrate strong community impact through structured frameworks or learning models. Application Process and Timeline Application Opens: March 9, 2026 Office Hours: March 17 – April 16, 2026 Application Deadline: April 17, 2026 (5:00 PM) Notification of Results: Late May to early June 2026 Grant Period: July 2026 – December 2027 Applicants must register on the foundation’s grant portal before applying. Registration approval may take 2–3 days, so early preparation is recommended. Why This Matters By investing in youth leadership, the initiative aims to strengthen not just individual participants but the broader civic ecosystem of San Diego. The program envisions a future where young people see themselves as stakeholders in their communities—capable of driving meaningful, long-term change. Organisations working at the intersection of youth development and civic engagement are encouraged to apply and contribute to building a more participatory and resilient society. Deadline: April 17, 2026 Application can be accessed from here. To know more about such opportunities, click here . title: Paid Student Fellowship Opportunity with the Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US)
summary: Students and young professionals seeking practical experience in international development, education policy, advocacy, and nonprofit communications now have an exciting opportunity through the Paid Student Fellowship offered by the Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US). This fellowship provides students with hands-on experience working with a leading advocacy coalition that promotes universal access to quality and inclusive education worldwide. The fellowship is designed to support students who are passionate about social change, global education rights, and policy advocacy. It allows participants to contribute meaningfully to international education campaigns while developing professional skills in communications, policy engagement, research, and nonprofit management. The fellowship also offers financial compensation and flexible working hours suitable for students balancing academic commitments. Participants can gain valuable exposure to international education policy networks and build connections with organizations advocating for education as a fundamental human right. About the Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US) The Global Campaign for Education-US (GCE-US) is a coalition of organizations working together to ensure that all children and youth worldwide have access to quality, inclusive, and equitable education. The organization focuses on advocacy efforts aimed at mobilizing political will in the United States and globally to support education initiatives. The coalition collaborates with civil society organizations, policy leaders, educators, and international partners to promote education policies that support sustainable development and human rights. Education is recognized as a key driver of social, economic, and political progress, and GCE-US works to ensure it remains at the center of global development agendas. Through this fellowship program, students gain a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of advocacy, policy development, communication, and coalition building. Overview of the Paid Student Fellowship The GCE-US Paid Student Fellowship provides students with the opportunity to participate in real-world advocacy efforts supporting global education initiatives. Fellowship Key Details Category: Fellowship / Internship Compensation: Approximately $16.10 per hour Work Schedule: 10–20 hours per week Location: 100% remote (telework) Preferred Locations: Washington, DC or New York City metro areas for occasional meetings Duration: Flexible; preference for fellows who can continue for at least one academic year Start Date: As soon as possible Academic Credit: May be available depending on the student’s university Application Method: Email submission of cover letter and resume The fellowship is structured to allow students to work remotely while contributing to meaningful global education advocacy campaigns. Responsibilities of the GCE-US Student Fellow Fellows play an active role in supporting the coalition’s advocacy work, communication strategies, and member engagement. The fellowship provides a dynamic learning environment where participants gain practical experience in nonprofit operations and international policy advocacy. Communications and Social Media Management One of the key responsibilities of fellows is supporting GCE-US communication strategies. Fellows will: Create and manage social media content across platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, LinkedIn, and YouTube. Design graphics and visual materials highlighting global education statistics and advocacy campaigns. Monitor engagement and analyze social media campaign performance. This experience helps fellows develop valuable digital communications skills applicable to nonprofit organizations, advocacy groups, and international development agencies. Research and Policy Advocacy Another important component of the fellowship involves conducting research on global education issues. Fellows will: Research international education policies and global education trends. Prepare presentations and reports for coalition members. Support advocacy initiatives aimed at promoting education rights globally. This research contributes to the organization’s efforts to influence policy decisions and strengthen global education systems. Coalition Engagement and Partnership Development GCE-US works with a wide network of partner organizations. Fellows help maintain these relationships through: Communicating with member organizations Coordinating coalition meetings Engaging partners in collaborative advocacy campaigns Supporting outreach to new organizations This responsibility allows fellows to gain experience in stakeholder engagement and international collaboration. Event Coordination and Meeting Support Fellows assist in planning and coordinating meetings, events, and advocacy campaigns. Typical responsibilities may include: Preparing agendas for coalition meetings Contacting speakers and participants Scheduling and organizing meetings Taking meeting minutes and tracking follow-up actions Supporting fundraising and advocacy events These tasks help fellows develop organizational and event management skills. Content Creation and Publication Fellows also contribute to producing educational and advocacy materials. Tasks may include: Writing blog posts and articles Coordinating the organization’s monthly newsletter Updating website content Publishing press releases and partner highlights These responsibilities strengthen fellows’ writing, editing, and public communication abilities. Qualifications and Eligibility Requirements To ensure fellows can effectively contribute to the organization’s work, GCE-US has outlined several qualifications and expectations. Academic Requirements Applicants must be: Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate degree program, or Recently graduated from a recognized university. Fields of study that align well with the fellowship include: International development Education policy Public policy Communications Political science Social sciences Nonprofit management Professional Skills and Competencies Successful applicants should demonstrate the following skills: Strong writing, editing, and communication skills Ability to organize and manage multiple tasks Experience or interest in advocacy and social impact initiatives Knowledge of social media management and digital communications Ability to work independently and collaboratively in remote environments Applicants should also possess a strong attention to detail and excellent organizational skills. Technical Skills Preferred technical skills include familiarity with: Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Google Workspace tools Zoom and remote collaboration platforms Graphic design tools such as Canva Website management platforms like WordPress Data analytics tools such as Google Analytics Although not mandatory, experience with these tools strengthens an applicant’s profile. Language Requirements The primary working language for the fellowship is English. However, additional language skills are considered an advantage. Languages that may strengthen an application include: Arabic French Portuguese Spanish International Sign Language American Sign Language Multilingual candidates are particularly valuable in international advocacy organizations working across different regions. Who Can Apply for the GCE-US Fellowship? Eligible Categories of Applicants The opportunity is open to: Undergraduate students Graduate students Recent university graduates Students interested in international development and advocacy Candidates from marginalized and underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply. Geographic Eligibility Applicants must be legally authorized to work in the United States because the organization cannot sponsor work permits. Therefore, eligible applicants typically include: Students studying in the United States U.S. citizens or permanent residents International students with legal work authorization in the U.S. Benefits of the GCE-US Paid Student Fellowship Participating in this fellowship offers several professional and academic benefits. Professional Development Fellows gain real-world experience working with an international advocacy organization. This exposure helps participants understand how policy advocacy campaigns operate at national and global levels. Networking Opportunities The fellowship allows students to connect with: Education policy experts International development professionals Nonprofit leaders Advocacy organizations and coalition members These connections can open doors to future employment opportunities. Advocacy Experience Fellows gain firsthand experience contributing to campaigns aimed at promoting education as a human right. This experience can be valuable for careers in: International development Public policy Education reform Human rights advocacy Academic Integration Because the fellowship offers flexible hours and potential academic credit, it is particularly suitable for students who want to combine practical experience with academic studies. How to Apply for the GCE-US Paid Student Fellowship Applicants must submit their application via email. Required Application Documents Applicants should send the following materials in a single document: Cover letter Resume The subject line should include: “Fellowship Application.” Apply here Information to Include in the Cover Letter Applicants must also address several questions in their cover letter: Why they want to work in the role How they learned about the opportunity Their salary expectations Availability and preferred work schedule Academic program details Confirmation of legal authorization to work in the United States Applications should be sent to the email address listed on the official opportunity page. Why This Fellowship Is Valuable for Future Global Leaders Education advocacy remains one of the most important global development priorities. Access to quality education improves economic opportunity, promotes gender equality, and supports sustainable development worldwide. The GCE-US fellowship allows students to participate directly in advocacy initiatives that influence international education policy and funding decisions. Through hands-on work with coalition members and policy advocates, fellows gain the experience necessary to become future leaders in education reform and global development. This fellowship also equips students with practical skills in communication, research, nonprofit management, and coalition building, all of which are highly valued in the international development sector. Application Deadline 22nd March 2026 Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and only shortlisted candidates will be contacted). The GCE-US Paid Student Fellowship is an excellent opportunity for students passionate about global education, advocacy, and nonprofit leadership. With paid compensation, flexible working hours, and the chance to contribute to international education campaigns, the fellowship provides a meaningful platform for students to build their careers while making a positive global impact. title: GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme 2026 in Germany: Fully Funded Global Public Health Fellowship for Professionals Working in Epidemic Intelligence and Outbreak Response
summary: JThe GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme 2026 is a prestigious international fellowship designed to strengthen global capacity in epidemic intelligence, outbreak response, and public health emergency preparedness. The program is jointly coordinated by the Robert Koch Institute (RKI), the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence, and the Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network (GOARN). This six-month fellowship provides public health professionals with the opportunity to design and implement impactful projects that improve surveillance systems, strengthen outbreak response capabilities, and contribute to global health security. The program equips fellows with advanced skills in public health surveillance, emergency response, and epidemic intelligence while fostering collaboration with leading global health experts and institutions. The fellowship is designed for professionals working within GOARN partner institutions and technical networks worldwide. Participants gain hands-on experience through mentorship, research, and collaborative projects while contributing to the development of innovative strategies that enhance global preparedness for future health emergencies. Overview of the GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme The GOARN Berlin Fellowship is a six-month project-based training and research fellowship aimed at developing the next generation of global health leaders. Participants undertake a project related to epidemic intelligence, public health surveillance, outbreak control, or emergency preparedness. The fellowship focuses on strengthening the capacity of institutions around the world to detect and respond to emerging public health threats. By working closely with experts from leading global health organizations, fellows gain practical insights and advanced knowledge that can be applied in their home institutions and countries. Key objectives of the fellowship include: Enhancing expertise in epidemic intelligence and disease surveillance Strengthening global collaboration in outbreak response and public health preparedness Supporting innovative research and operational projects that improve early warning systems Developing leadership capacity among public health professionals Building stronger international networks of public health experts Through the fellowship, participants become part of a global network of professionals working together to improve health security worldwide. Fellowship Structure and Tracks Beginning with the 2026 cohort, the GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme offers two flexible participation tracks, allowing fellows to tailor their experience to their professional circumstances and goals. 1. Berlin Track Under the Berlin Track, fellows relocate to Berlin, Germany, where they are based at the WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence and the Robert Koch Institute. During their stay, fellows collaborate with global experts and participate in intensive training sessions, workshops, and project development activities. This track offers a fully immersive experience within one of the world’s leading public health ecosystems. 2. Country Track The Country Track allows fellows to implement their projects while remaining in their home country or institution. Although fellows work primarily within their local context, they still participate in joint training programs and collaborative activities in Berlin and Geneva. This flexible approach enables professionals who cannot relocate for the full six months to benefit from the fellowship while contributing directly to capacity development in their home institutions. Both tracks provide fellows with equal opportunities to engage in mentorship, collaborative research, and global health networking. Fellowship Duration The GOARN Berlin Fellowship runs for six months, during which fellows focus on implementing their project while receiving expert mentorship and training. The program is structured around: Project development and implementation Mentorship and technical guidance Training workshops and seminars Collaborative activities with global health experts Cohort learning experiences Throughout the fellowship, participants engage with professionals from international public health agencies, research institutions, and government health authorities. Institutions Coordinating the Fellowship The GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme is a collaborative initiative coordinated by several leading global health organizations: Robert Koch Institute (RKI) The Robert Koch Institute is Germany’s national public health institute and plays a central role in disease surveillance, epidemiological research, and health protection. WHO Hub for Pandemic and Epidemic Intelligence This World Health Organization hub focuses on improving global surveillance systems by using data and analytics to detect and respond to emerging health threats. Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network (GOARN) GOARN is a global network of institutions and experts coordinated by the World Health Organization that responds to public health emergencies and disease outbreaks worldwide. Together, these organizations provide fellows with access to cutting-edge research environments, advanced data tools, and global expertise in epidemic intelligence. Fields and Areas of Work Projects developed during the fellowship typically focus on areas such as: Epidemic intelligence Public health surveillance systems Outbreak detection and response Pandemic preparedness and response strategies Risk assessment and emergency management Early warning systems for infectious diseases Data-driven public health decision making These projects contribute directly to strengthening national and global capacities for disease surveillance and outbreak response. Benefits of the GOARN Berlin Fellowship Participants in the GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme receive numerous professional and academic benefits, including: Advanced Training Fellows participate in training programs designed to strengthen skills in epidemic intelligence, outbreak response, and data analysis. Mentorship from Global Experts Participants receive mentorship from leading experts at the Robert Koch Institute, WHO Hub, and GOARN. International Collaboration The fellowship connects participants with global networks of public health professionals and institutions. Practical Project Implementation Fellows work on real-world projects that strengthen surveillance systems and outbreak preparedness in their home institutions. Professional Networking Participants build long-term collaborations with international researchers, policymakers, and public health experts. Global Health Leadership Development The program prepares fellows to become leaders in epidemic intelligence and public health emergency response. Eligibility Criteria To apply for the GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme, candidates must meet several eligibility requirements. Institutional Eligibility Applicants must be working at a GOARN partner institution or technical network. Only professionals affiliated with these institutions are eligible to apply. Professional Background Candidates should be public health professionals with experience in areas such as: Epidemiology Disease surveillance Emergency preparedness and response Public health research Risk communication and outbreak management Project Proposal Applicants are typically required to submit a project proposal related to epidemic intelligence, surveillance, or outbreak response. Professional Commitment Candidates should demonstrate a strong commitment to improving public health systems and contributing to global health security. Eligible Countries and Regions The fellowship is open to applicants from institutions that are members of the Global Outbreak Alert and Response Network. GOARN partner institutions are located across multiple regions, including: Africa Asia Europe The Americas The Middle East Oceania Because GOARN is a global network, professionals from many countries worldwide may be eligible to apply if their organization is part of the network. Who Should Apply The fellowship is ideal for professionals who: Work in national public health institutes Serve in ministries of health Conduct public health research Participate in outbreak response teams Work in international health organizations Contribute to epidemic surveillance systems The program is particularly beneficial for professionals seeking to strengthen their expertise in epidemic intelligence and contribute to the improvement of disease surveillance systems in their home countries. Impact of the Fellowship The GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme has already supported public health professionals from multiple countries in developing innovative projects that strengthen surveillance systems and outbreak preparedness. Fellows from previous cohorts have worked on projects such as: Improving early warning systems for infectious disease outbreaks Strengthening national surveillance platforms Enhancing risk assessment frameworks for public health emergencies Developing new approaches to epidemic intelligence These initiatives contribute to building stronger, data-driven public health systems worldwide. How to Apply Applicants interested in the fellowship should follow these steps: Confirm that their institution is a GOARN partner organization. Contact their institution’s GOARN focal point for application guidance. Prepare a project proposal related to epidemic intelligence or outbreak response. Complete the official application process through the program’s designated application channels. Because the program accepts a limited number of fellows, applicants are encouraged to submit strong proposals that demonstrate innovation, feasibility, and potential impact on global health. Apply here Application Deadline 16th March 2026 at 17:00 CET Applicants should ensure all required documents and project proposals are submitted before the deadline.The GOARN Berlin Fellowship Programme is one of the most impactful global health training opportunities available to public health professionals today. By combining advanced training, real-world project implementation, and collaboration with leading global health institutions, the fellowship equips participants with the knowledge and skills necessary to strengthen outbreak preparedness and response worldwide. Professionals working in public health institutions that are part of the GOARN network are strongly encouraged to apply for this prestigious fellowship and contribute to building stronger global health systems capable of responding to future epidemics and pandemics. title: McKinsey Forward Program 2026: Fully Free Global Career Accelerator for Young Professionals

The McKinsey Forward Program is a free, structured online learning experience that helps young professionals develop practical, real-world skills that are immediately applicable in the workplace. The program has already impacted over 500,000 learners globally, helping them build confidence, resilience, and career-ready competencies. It focuses on bridging the gap between academic knowledge and workplace performance—making it ideal for early-career individuals trying to stand out in competitive job markets. title: UN Women Leadership in Climate Action Free Online Course 2026 (With Certificate) – Fully Funded Global Opportunity to Build Climate Leadership Skills
summary: The “Developing Skills for Women Leadership in Climate Action” course is a globally recognized online training program offered through UN CC:Learn . This course is designed to empower women and girls across the world with the knowledge, tools, and leadership skills necessary to actively contribute to solving the global climate crisis. In a time when climate change is one of the most urgent global challenges, this opportunity provides a structured and accessible pathway for individuals—especially women—to gain the competencies required to lead impactful climate initiatives in their communities and beyond. This course is especially relevant for individuals from developing countries, Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and Oceania who are looking to build careers or projects in sustainability, climate action, and environmental leadership. About the UN CC:Learn Platform UN CC:Learn is a collaborative initiative involving more than 30 multilateral organizations that aims to strengthen global capacity to tackle climate change through education and training. It was launched to support countries and individuals in developing practical knowledge and skills to mitigate and adapt to climate change effectively. The platform offers a wide range of free online courses covering climate science, green economy, sustainable development, and environmental policy, making it one of the most trusted global learning hubs in this field. Overview of the Women Leadership in Climate Action Course The Developing Skills for Women Leadership in Climate Action course is a self-paced e-learning program that focuses on empowering women and girls to take leadership roles in climate action initiatives. Key Course Details: Course Type: Self-paced online training Duration: Approximately 9 hours (+ 3 hours of practical exercises) Languages Available: English, French, Spanish Certification: Available (USD 30 optional fee) Access: Free learning content Mode: 100% online The course combines theoretical knowledge with practical tools, ensuring that participants not only understand climate change but can also implement real-world solutions. Why You Should Apply for This Course This course is more than just a learning experience—it is a transformational opportunity to become part of the global climate solution. Key Benefits: Gain In-Demand Climate Knowledge Understand climate science, sustainability, and environmental challenges. Learn how climate change affects different populations, especially women. Develop Leadership Skills Build confidence to lead climate initiatives. Learn how to influence stakeholders and mobilize communities. Practical Project Development Gain tools to design and implement climate action projects. Strengthen existing environmental initiatives. Global Recognition Earn a certificate from a United Nations-affiliated platform. Flexible Learning Study at your own pace from anywhere in the world. Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria) This opportunity is open to a wide range of participants globally. This course is ideal for individuals who: Want to start a career in climate change or sustainability Are already working in environmental or social sectors Have ideas for climate-related projects but lack direction Want to influence climate policy or community initiatives Are passionate about gender equality and environmental justice Skills You Will Gain By the end of the course, participants will be able to: Explain key climate change concepts Analyze the impact of climate change on women Identify opportunities for climate action Design and implement inclusive climate projects Communicate and advocate for environmental change effectively These skills are highly valuable in careers related to sustainability, development, policy, and environmental science. Certification Details While the course content is completely free, participants can opt to receive a digital certificate upon completion by paying a small fee (approximately USD 30). This certificate can be used to: Enhance your CV or resume Strengthen job and scholarship applications Demonstrate commitment to climate action Why This Opportunity Matters Globally Climate change is a global crisis requiring inclusive and diverse leadership. This course bridges the gap by: Promoting gender-inclusive climate leadership Providing accessible education to underserved populations Empowering grassroots climate action According to global climate learning initiatives, building awareness and skills is critical for enabling individuals and communities to effectively respond to climate challenges.
