title: Fully Funded A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship at Oxford University for African Conservation Professionals
summary: The A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship Programme is a prestigious conservation fellowship designed specifically for African professionals working to protect biodiversity. Hosted at the University of Oxford , the programme provides conservation practitioners, policymakers, and researchers with an opportunity to collaborate with leading experts while accessing world-class academic resources. Launched in September 2021 and supported by the A. G. Leventis Foundation , the fellowship is run by E. J. Milner-Gulland , the Tasso Leventis Professor of Biodiversity at the Department of Biology. The initiative is coordinated through the Interdisciplinary Centre for Conservation Science (ICCS) , which focuses on bridging academic research with practical conservation work. The fellowship aims to strengthen biodiversity conservation across Africa by enabling professionals to gain advanced training, build global networks, and collaborate with researchers at Oxford. About the Fellowship Programme The fellowship offers African conservation professionals the chance to spend up to three months in Oxford, United Kingdom , working closely with researchers and academics. Fellows can join any department within the University as long as their project aligns with biodiversity conservation . During their stay, fellows benefit from: Specialized training courses and workshops Opportunities to develop research papers and conservation strategies Access to Oxford’s research facilities and academic resources Networking with global conservation experts Collaboration with leading researchers in biodiversity science In addition to the in-person residency at Oxford, the programme also provides remote mentoring, training, and networking opportunities both before and after the fellowship visit. Each year, three fellows are selected for the programme, making it a highly competitive opportunity. Core Objectives of the Fellowship The programme is built around three key goals designed to enhance conservation impact in Africa: 1. Strengthening Conservation Leadership The fellowship supports professionals from NGOs, government institutions, and private sector organizations who are involved in designing and implementing biodiversity conservation initiatives. Participants gain new skills, strengthen their professional networks, and improve their capacity to deliver impactful conservation outcomes in their home countries. 2. Bridging Research and Real-World Conservation Another major objective is to ensure that academic research at Oxford reflects real conservation challenges. By collaborating with practitioners from Africa, the university ensures its research is practical, relevant, and applicable on the ground. 3. Building Long-Term Partnerships The programme fosters long-term collaboration between Oxford researchers and conservation practitioners. These partnerships strengthen knowledge exchange and support sustainable conservation strategies across Africa. Fellowship Activities and Opportunities The fellowship provides a supportive environment where participants can focus on developing their conservation work. Fellows are encouraged to use their time at Oxford to: Analyse and maximise the value of their conservation datasets Develop new biodiversity conservation strategies Design and test innovative approaches to conservation challenges Attend professional training courses Build partnerships with international collaborators Share knowledge and experiences with Oxford researchers and students These activities contribute to more effective biodiversity conservation efforts across Africa. Cultural and Professional Experience in the UK Beyond academic engagement, the fellowship offers a rich cultural experience in the United Kingdom. Participants have opportunities to: Visit Oxford’s historic colleges and world-renowned museums Attend traditional college banquets Experience cultural events such as Evensong Participate in activities like punting on the River Cherwell Explore birdwatching and other nature activities These experiences allow fellows to immerse themselves in British culture while forming meaningful friendships and professional relationships. Mentorship and Support System To ensure fellows receive strong support during their stay, each participant is paired with: An Oxford academic mentor who works in their area of expertise An early-career researcher “buddy” who provides academic guidance and social support This mentoring structure helps fellows navigate both academic work and daily life in Oxford while fostering collaborative learning. Why This Fellowship Matters The A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship Programme plays a vital role in strengthening conservation leadership across Africa. By connecting practitioners with academic experts and global networks, the programme helps transform research insights into practical conservation solutions. Ultimately, the fellowship aims to support more effective and sustainable biodiversity conservation across Africa , ensuring long-term protection of ecosystems and wildlife. Who Should Apply? The fellowship is open to a wide range of professionals involved in biodiversity conservation. Senior Professionals Senior staff from NGOs, government agencies, and businesses may benefit from stepping away from their daily responsibilities to: Develop new conservation strategies Strengthen partnerships Write policy or research outputs Build international collaborations Early-Career Professionals Early-career conservation professionals and researchers are also encouraged to apply, especially those who wish to: Learn new research and analytical skills Analyse and publish their conservation datasets Expand their international professional networks Eligibility Criteria The fellowship has a clear eligibility requirement: Applicants must be African nationals. Candidates should be actively working on biodiversity conservation in sectors such as NGOs, government institutions, academia, or businesses involved in environmental sustainability. Deadline is 31st March 2026. Click Here , to apply. Discover more similar opportunities by clicking here. The post Fully Funded A. G. Leventis African Biodiversity Fellowship at Oxford University for African Conservation Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Young Leaders to Attend the One Young World Summit in Cape Town
summary: The Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for young African leaders who are driving impactful initiatives that support vulnerable and marginalized youth in transitioning from education to sustainable employment or entrepreneurship. This prestigious scholarship will support 25 young changemakers from across Africa to attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa , scheduled to take place from 3–6 November 2026 . The scholarship is offered through a partnership between the Z Zurich Foundation and One Young World , an international organization that convenes one of the most influential global communities of young leaders working to address the world’s most pressing social, economic, and environmental challenges. The Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship specifically focuses on empowering African youth leaders whose initiatives promote social equity through education, employability, entrepreneurship, and inclusive economic opportunities . Selected scholars will gain access to leadership training, international networking opportunities, and a global platform to amplify their work. Applications for the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026 open on 16 March 2026 and close on 4 May 2026 . About the Z Zurich Foundation The Z Zurich Foundation is committed to building brighter futures for vulnerable populations worldwide. The foundation prioritizes preventive action in addressing some of society’s most urgent and complex challenges. Its work focuses on four major pillars: Adapting to climate change Improving mental well-being Enabling social equity Responding to crises These areas address systemic barriers that affect individuals and communities across the globe. By supporting emerging leaders, the foundation aims to empower individuals who can drive sustainable solutions within their communities. The Z Zurich Foundation believes that creating lasting social impact requires connection, collaboration, and grassroots leadership . This philosophy underpins its partnership with the One Young World Summit. About the One Young World Summit The One Young World Summit is one of the world’s most prominent global gatherings for young leaders. Each year, the summit brings together thousands of young changemakers, entrepreneurs, activists, and innovators from across the globe. Participants attend leadership sessions, networking events, and panel discussions led by influential global leaders from sectors such as: Government Business Non-profit organizations Academia Social entrepreneurship The summit provides a platform for young leaders to exchange ideas, develop innovative solutions, and collaborate on initiatives addressing global challenges. Through scholarships such as the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship, One Young World ensures that emerging leaders from underserved regions have access to this transformative experience. Why the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship Focuses on Africa Africa is the youngest continent in the world, with over 60 percent of its population under the age of 25 . By 2030, African youth are expected to represent one-fifth of the global youth population . Despite this demographic advantage, many African countries face significant challenges, including: Limited employment opportunities for young people Fragmented education and skills development systems Unequal access to finance and digital technologies Climate change impacts Economic instability and conflict Structural inequalities affecting vulnerable populations These challenges create barriers that prevent young people from transitioning successfully from learning to earning . Supporting youth-led grassroots initiatives is therefore essential. Young leaders who understand their communities are uniquely positioned to develop innovative solutions that address local challenges and empower others. The Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship aims to identify and empower these leaders , enabling them to amplify their impact and influence systems that shape economic and social opportunities across Africa. Overview of the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026 The scholarship will select 25 outstanding young leaders from Africa who are working on initiatives that support youth in overcoming barriers to education, employment, or entrepreneurship. Successful applicants will attend the One Young World Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa , and will also participate in a series of leadership development programs designed to enhance their capacity as changemakers. These activities include: A pre-summit leadership preparation program Networking sessions with peers and experts A pre-summit connection day in Cape Town Post-summit engagement and reflection activities Potential eligibility for an additional leadership fellowship The goal of the program is to help participants expand their leadership skills, strengthen their networks, and scale their social impact initiatives. Scholarship Benefits Selected Z Zurich Foundation Scholars will receive a fully funded package that covers participation in the One Young World Summit as well as additional leadership development opportunities. 1. Full Participation in the One Young World Summit Scholars will participate in the One Young World Summit 2026 , held in Cape Town from 3–6 November 2026 . This global event will allow scholars to engage with other young leaders, industry experts, and international decision-makers. 2. Accommodation Scholars will receive hotel accommodation in Cape Town from 1–6 November 2026 . 3. Round-Trip Airfare The scholarship includes return international flights to and from Cape Town, South Africa . 4. Meals and Catering The scholarship provides full catering throughout the summit , including breakfast, lunch, and dinner. 5. Local Transportation Transportation between summit venues in Cape Town will be covered. Additional Leadership Development Opportunities Beyond summit participation, scholars will engage in curated leadership development activities designed to deepen their impact. 1. Pre-Summit Engagement and Preparation Program Between September and October 2026 , scholars will participate in a virtual leadership development program that focuses on: Personal leadership development Preparing for effective participation in the summit Learning from expert contributors Developing strategies for scaling social impact 2. Pre-Summit Connection Day On 2 November 2026 , scholars will participate in an in-person event in Cape Town that allows participants to: Connect with fellow scholars Align on shared goals Engage with the Z Zurich Foundation 3. Post-Summit Debrief Session A post-summit online session will take place in late November 2026 , allowing scholars to reflect on their experiences and plan future impact strategies. 4. Opportunity for Post-Summit Fellowship Scholars will also have the opportunity to apply for a one-year Post-Summit Fellowship through the Z Zurich Foundation Global Changemakers Program . This fellowship provides additional support and capacity-building resources to help leaders scale their initiatives. Only a limited number of scholars will be selected for this additional opportunity. Focus Areas for the Scholarship The Z Zurich Foundation is seeking leaders who are implementing initiatives that help vulnerable youth transition successfully from education to employment or entrepreneurship. Priority focus areas include the following. 1. Youth Employability Initiatives that support young people in accessing meaningful employment opportunities. Examples include: Aligning education and vocational training with labor market demands Improving pathways from education to employment Addressing barriers that prevent young people from completing education 2. Youth Entrepreneurship Programs that enable youth to create sustainable livelihoods through entrepreneurship. These initiatives may focus on: Financial literacy Access to funding Business development support Income generation strategies 3. Digital Inclusion and Future of Work Projects that help bridge the digital divide by: Expanding access to digital technologies Building digital and future-ready skills Connecting youth with emerging employment opportunities Learn more about our work: Social equity | Z Zurich Foundation . Cross-Cutting Priorities Across all focus areas, the Z Zurich Foundation is particularly interested in initiatives that: Promote safe, decent, and productive employment Support displaced or refugee youth Empower marginalized communities Improve youth mental well-being Strengthen climate-resilient and green livelihoods Create opportunities in rural or agricultural sectors Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following criteria to qualify for the scholarship. Age between 18 and 35 years by the date of the summit in November 2026. Must be nationals and residents of African countries . Demonstrate measurable impact aligned with the Enabling Social Equity pillar of the Z Zurich Foundation. Be leading an early-stage initiative that does not yet have major corporate or institutional funding. Lead a formally registered organization with a not-for-profit mission . The organization must have a bank account registered in its name . Serve in a primary leadership role within the initiative. Commit to participating in the Pre-Summit Engagement and Preparation Program . Participate in the Post-Summit Debrief Session . Be willing to actively collaborate and engage with fellow scholars before, during, and after the summit. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026 is 4 May 2026 . Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early. How to Apply Applicants must submit their application through the Official One Young World Application Platform. Applications will be reviewed based on: Leadership potential Social impact of the applicant’s initiative Alignment with the foundation’s social equity goals Commitment to community development Click HERE to learn more about the Z Zurich Foundation Scholarship 2026. Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE. title: NYU Stern Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Journalists in New York
summary: Applications are now open for the Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship 2026 at NYU Stern School of Business , a unique opportunity for journalists worldwide to deepen their understanding of the economic forces shaping climate change. The fellowship brings selected participants to New York University’s Stern School of Business in Greenwich Village, New York City , for a two-day immersive program focused on the intersection of climate change, financial markets, and the global economy. Climate change is rapidly transforming industries, policies, and financial systems. For journalists reporting on environmental issues, understanding climate economics is increasingly essential. This prestigious fellowship is designed to help journalists interpret complex economic developments and report more effectively on climate-related topics. Program Overview The Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship will take place September 17–18, 2026 , at NYU Stern’s campus in New York City. The program brings together a small cohort of journalists who will learn from globally recognized experts in climate economics, finance, and policy. Participants will attend lectures, discussions, and networking events designed to strengthen their reporting skills and deepen their knowledge of the economic impacts of climate change. The fellowship is organized by the Climate Finance Initiative at NYU Stern and includes contributions from faculty across New York University. Financial Support NYU Stern fully funds the fellowship program. Selected fellows will receive: $2,000 stipend for journalists residing outside the New York Metro Area to cover travel and accommodation $1,000 stipend for journalists residing within the New York Metro Area All program costs covered , including meals and event participation This support ensures that journalists from different regions can participate without financial barriers. What Fellows Will Learn The program is designed for journalists who want to better understand how climate change influences economic systems and financial markets. Sessions will explore key topics such as: Climate risks to financial stability The impact of climate change on housing and insurance markets Corporate decision-making in response to climate risks Climate regulation and carbon markets Biodiversity loss as an economic risk Renewable energy and electric vehicle market economics Emerging climate finance regulations and legislation Participants will also explore pressing questions such as the economics behind carbon pricing, carbon border adjustments, cap-and-trade systems, and the social cost of carbon. In addition to academic sessions, fellows will engage in networking and social events that provide opportunities to connect with other journalists and NYU faculty. Who Should Apply The fellowship welcomes applications from: Reporters Editors Freelance journalists Applicants should have an interest in covering the intersection of climate change and the global economy . Prior training in economics is not required , making the program accessible to journalists from diverse reporting backgrounds. Although some discussions may reference U.S. policies and institutions, journalists based outside the United States are strongly encouraged to apply . Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following materials: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Cover letter explaining motivation for applying Samples of published work Fellowship recipients will be selected based on the quality of these materials, particularly the applicant’s demonstrated interest in climate and economic reporting. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Important Dates Application Deadline: April 20, 2026 Notification of Selected Fellows: Late May 2026 Program Dates: September 17–18, 2026 Location: NYU Stern School of Business, Greenwich Village, New York City The fellowship takes place shortly before Climate Week NYC , offering participants additional opportunities to cover major climate-related events and discussions. Why This Fellowship Matters As climate change reshapes financial systems, markets, and public policy, journalists play a critical role in translating complex economic developments for global audiences. The NYU Stern Climate Economics Journalism Fellowship equips journalists with the knowledge, tools, and expert connections needed to produce insightful and impactful climate reporting. By participating in this program, journalists gain a deeper understanding of climate economics while building a global network of professionals committed to covering one of the most important stories of our time. Interested journalists are encouraged to apply before the deadline and take advantage of this rare opportunity to learn from leading experts in climate economics. title: Transformative Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 for Future Global Health Leaders
summary: The Prestigious Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity for aspiring public health leaders to pursue a Master of Public Health (MPH) degree with full tuition support at Cornell University . This fellowship program is designed to train the next generation of professionals who will tackle some of the world’s most pressing public health challenges. In today’s rapidly changing world, societies face complex health threats that require innovative solutions and skilled leadership. Climate change, growing health inequities, emerging infectious diseases, and the erosion of trust in science are just a few of the challenges affecting global health systems. The Cornell Public Health Fellowship was established to prepare talented students to address these interconnected issues and rebuild resilient public health systems. Through this fellowship, selected participants will receive full tuition support to pursue Cornell’s two-year Master of Public Health program or the 12-month accelerated MPH program for candidates who meet the eligibility requirements. The application deadline for the Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 is March 23, 2026 . Cornell Fellowship Program Overview The Cornell Public Health Fellowship is designed to equip future public health professionals with the knowledge, leadership skills, and practical experience needed to solve complex global health challenges. Fellows will study within Cornell’s interdisciplinary academic environment, where students collaborate with faculty and experts from multiple disciplines. The program combines academic training with applied learning experiences that allow students to translate theoretical knowledge into practical solutions. Students enrolled in the program will gain exposure to community-based learning opportunities, policy discussions, and applied research projects that address real-world health challenges. This experiential approach prepares fellows to work effectively across public health systems, government agencies, international organizations, and non-profit sectors. Academic Training in the Cornell Fellowship The Cornell MPH program emphasizes interdisciplinary learning and community engagement. Fellows will work closely with faculty members who specialize in diverse fields of public health and global health policy. The curriculum focuses on building strong competencies in public health research, policy analysis, epidemiology, environmental health, and health systems leadership. Students will also gain practical experience through fieldwork, collaborative projects, and applied research opportunities. This comprehensive academic approach prepares fellows to address emerging health challenges while contributing to evidence-based public health policies and programs. Cornell Fellowship Concentration Areas Students selected for the Cornell Public Health Fellowship will choose one of four specialized concentration areas within the MPH program. Food Systems and Health This concentration examines the relationship between food systems, nutrition, and public health. Students explore how food production, distribution, and access influence health outcomes and societal well-being. Environment, Climate and Health This specialization focuses on the intersection between environmental changes and public health. Students study the health impacts of climate change, environmental degradation, and pollution. Infectious Disease Epidemiology Students in this concentration learn how to monitor, analyze, and respond to infectious disease outbreaks. Training focuses on epidemiological methods used to track and control diseases at local, national, and global levels. Emergency Preparedness and Management This concentration prepares students to respond effectively to public health emergencies such as pandemics, natural disasters, and humanitarian crises. Benefits of the Cornell Public Health Fellowship The Cornell Public Health Fellowship provides several important benefits to selected students. These include: • Full tuition support for the Master of Public Health degree • Access to world-class faculty and interdisciplinary research • Opportunities for community-engaged learning • Hands-on experience translating theory into practice • Specialized training in key public health concentration areas The fellowship allows students to focus on their academic and professional development without the burden of tuition costs. Career Opportunities After the Fellowship Graduates of the Cornell MPH program pursue careers across multiple sectors of public health. Potential career paths include: • Government health agencies • International organizations • Healthcare systems • Non-governmental organizations • Public policy institutions • Research organizations The fellowship prepares graduates to take leadership roles in addressing global health challenges and improving health systems worldwide. Application Process To be considered for the Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 , applicants must first apply to Cornell’s Master of Public Health program through the SOPHAS application platform. Applicants must also submit the Cornell Graduate School Quick Application . Candidates will be evaluated based on their academic achievements, commitment to public health, and potential to contribute to meaningful change in global health systems. Application Timeline Applicants must follow the official application timeline to be considered for the fellowship. Key dates include: • March 23, 2026: Deadline to apply to Cornell’s MPH program via SOPHAS • March 23, 2026: Deadline to submit the Cornell Graduate School Quick Application • March 31, 2026: Applicants notified of fellowship decisions • April 15, 2026: Deadline to accept the fellowship offer Meeting these deadlines is essential to ensure eligibility for the fellowship. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Prestigious Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 , click here . APPLY HERE Conclusion The Cornell Public Health Fellowship 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for students who aspire to become leaders in public health. By providing full tuition support and access to world-class academic training, the fellowship empowers students to address the complex health challenges facing communities around the world. Through interdisciplinary learning, practical experience, and specialized training, fellows will develop the skills needed to transform public health systems and improve health outcomes for future generations.
