title: Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All – Global Leadership Accelerator
summary: The Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator (GLA) presents a remarkable opportunity for experienced professionals who are passionate about reshaping education systems globally. This remote leadership position allows individuals to work with one of the world’s most influential education networks while supporting thousands of leaders dedicated to creating equitable learning opportunities for children. Teach For All is a global network of more than 60 independent organizations, supporting over 15,000 teachers currently working in classrooms and nearly 125,000 alumni leaders across multiple countries. These leaders collectively impact millions of children worldwide, working to address systemic inequities in education. The Leadership Director for the Global Leadership Accelerator will play a crucial role in designing leadership development experiences, coaching senior leaders, and shaping global initiatives that accelerate system-wide change. Through this position, the selected candidate will work with social entrepreneurs, educators, policymakers, and changemakers across the globe. This job opportunity is remote and available to candidates located in several Asia-Pacific countries, including Bangladesh, Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Pakistan, the Philippines, Singapore, and Thailand. Leadership Role in the Global Leadership Accelerator The Leadership Director will work within the Global Leadership Accelerator, a strategic initiative that focuses on cultivating strong leadership across the Teach For All network. The Accelerator aims to build deeply conscious leaders who are motivated by purpose and committed to transforming inequitable systems in education. By developing leadership capacity among educators, social entrepreneurs, and policymakers, the program seeks to drive meaningful change in global education systems. This role also connects closely with the Global Institute for Shaping a Better Future, an initiative that facilitates learning and collaboration among education leaders, researchers, and policymakers around the world. The institute contributes to the development of leadership skills while also generating insights, research, and tools that support the transformation of education systems globally. Key Responsibilities of the Leadership Director The Leadership Director will help launch new initiatives and expand existing leadership development programs across the Teach For All network. Some of the most important responsibilities include designing leadership experiences and supporting senior leaders who are working to reshape education systems. Key responsibilities include: Designing Leadership Programs The selected candidate will help co-design leadership development initiatives that bring together emerging and established leaders across the global education sector. Examples include: Designing fellowships for policymakers and political leaders Creating leadership development programs for educators and system leaders Facilitating global learning experiences and workshops Coaching Senior Leaders The Leadership Director will coach and support senior leaders within the Teach For All network. This involves: Leading peer learning groups Supporting leadership teams in strengthening their impact Providing guidance on leadership development and strategic thinking Facilitating Global Learning Experiences Another important responsibility involves facilitating workshops and leadership programs for educators and changemakers worldwide. These learning experiences may include both in-person and virtual formats. Managing Strategic Leadership Initiatives The Leadership Director will manage projects from conception to execution. This includes: Program design Communications and marketing Participant enrollment Program delivery Monitoring impact and outcomes Building Partnerships The role also involves developing partnerships with external organizations and collaborators to co-create flagship leadership programs. These partnerships help expand the reach and effectiveness of leadership development initiatives. Qualifications Required for the Leadership Director Role Teach For All is seeking experienced professionals with strong expertise in leadership development and organizational transformation. The ideal candidate will possess: Extensive experience in leadership development or organizational development Strong coaching and facilitation skills Experience working with senior leaders or executive teams Expertise in designing learning experiences for adults Exceptional strategic thinking and problem-solving abilities Candidates should also demonstrate the ability to work effectively in global environments and collaborate with diverse teams across cultures and regions. Additional Experience and Skills While not mandatory, the following experience is considered highly valuable: Background in education leadership or school leadership Experience in community organizing or systems change Expertise in diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives Experience designing programs at scale Familiarity with the Teach For All network or one of its partner organizations Teach For All is particularly interested in candidates who demonstrate an entrepreneurial mindset and the ability to envision and launch innovative leadership programs. Work Environment and Travel Expectations This role is fully remote, allowing professionals to work from one of several eligible countries across the Asia-Pacific region. Although the position is primarily virtual, the role may require up to 20% travel to support leadership programs, workshops, or global learning initiatives. Because the organization operates globally, team members may occasionally need to work across different time zones. Benefits of the Leadership Director Role Teach For All offers a supportive work environment and a strong commitment to staff well-being and development. Benefits include: Opportunities for professional growth and leadership development Flexible work arrangements and remote work Generous leave policies Comprehensive employee benefits Opportunities to contribute to global education transformation Employees also become part of a collaborative global network of education leaders dedicated to ensuring every child has access to quality education. Application Process Interested candidates should submit the following documents: Resume or Curriculum Vitae (CV) A one-page motivation letter written in English The motivation letter should explain the applicant’s interest in the role and how their experience aligns with the responsibilities of the position. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early applications are strongly encouraged. Work Authorization Requirement Teach For All does not provide visa sponsorship or immigration assistance for this role. Applicants must already have legal authorization to work in the country where they are applying. Learn More About This Opportunity To learn more about the Transformative Leadership Director Job 2026 at Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator, visit the official application page here . APPLY HERE Conclusion The Leadership Director position within Teach For All’s Global Leadership Accelerator represents an exceptional opportunity for experienced professionals who are passionate about education transformation and leadership development. Through this role, the selected candidate will contribute to shaping leadership across one of the largest global networks working to ensure that all children have access to quality education. title: Fully Funded 2026 DTH-Lab Research Fellowship for Students and Early-Career Professionals
summary: The Digital Transformations for Health Lab (DTH-Lab) has officially announced the 2026 Research Fellowship Programme, a highly competitive global opportunity designed to empower emerging researchers and professionals interested in digital health, public policy, and technological transformation in healthcare systems. This prestigious fellowship provides students and early-career professionals from low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) the chance to contribute to cutting-edge research projects shaping the future of digital health governance and policy worldwide. The fellowship is part of the organization’s mission to develop the next generation of global health leaders capable of navigating the complex intersection of technology, public health, and global governance. Through this program, selected fellows will collaborate with leading experts, contribute to meaningful research initiatives, and help shape innovative policies for digital health transformation. About the Digital Transformations for Health Lab (DTH-Lab) The Digital Transformations for Health Lab is a global consortium that works at the intersection of digital technology, public health systems, and governance. The organization focuses on implementing recommendations from the Lancet and Financial Times Commission on Governing Health Futures 2030, emphasizing value-driven and youth-centered digital health transformations. DTH-Lab collaborates with policymakers, academic institutions, global health organizations, and youth leaders to advance innovative approaches that improve health outcomes through technology. Its work focuses on: Digital health policy and governance Health data systems and public health technology Youth participation in global health transformation AI and emerging technologies in healthcare Digital well-being and technology-driven public health solutions Through fellowships like this one, the organization builds the capacity of future health leaders who can guide governments and institutions through the rapidly evolving digital healthcare landscape. Purpose of the DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme The DTH-Lab Research Fellowship was designed to nurture young professionals who want to influence how digital technologies shape public health policies and healthcare systems. The fellowship program has three major goals: 1. Build the Capacity of Future Health Leaders Participants will gain hands-on experience conducting research related to digital health governance and innovation. The program equips fellows with critical analytical, research, and policy skills needed in global health leadership. 2. Advance Research on Digital Health Transformation Fellows will contribute to research projects that explore how digital technologies influence healthcare systems, health equity, and youth well-being. 3. Promote Youth Engagement in Health Policy The fellowship encourages young professionals to play a meaningful role in shaping digital health strategies at national and international levels. Ultimately, the program aims to develop a new generation of researchers and policymakers capable of addressing the ethical, technological, and societal implications of digital health systems. Research Topics for the 2026 Fellowship For the 2026 cohort, selected fellows will work on research projects addressing some of the most urgent issues in digital health transformation. Key research themes include: 1. National Policies for Young People’s Digital Well-Being This research explores how governments design policies that protect young people in digital environments while promoting positive digital experiences and mental well-being. 2. Digital Determinants of Health in Africa This topic examines how digital access, connectivity, data governance, and technology infrastructure influence public health outcomes across the African continent. 3. Digital Citizenship for Health This research focuses on building frameworks that enable individuals to responsibly use digital technologies to improve personal and community health. 4. Artificial Intelligence and the Health Workforce Participants will examine how AI technologies are transforming healthcare delivery and redefining roles within the healthcare workforce. 5. Emerging Roles in an AI-Enabled Health Workforce This research investigates how healthcare professionals must adapt as artificial intelligence becomes integrated into clinical practice and health systems. Each fellow will contribute to a specific research area, producing insights that may inform global digital health policies. Who Can Apply The DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme 2026 is open to a specific group of individuals with strong interest in digital health, public health research, and policy development. Eligible Applicants Applicants must fall into one of the following categories: University students Early-career researchers Young professionals in health, policy, or technology sectors Professionals interested in digital health transformation Researchers exploring digital governance or AI in healthcare The program targets individuals who are passionate about shaping the future of healthcare through digital innovation and research. Apply here Countries and Regions Eligible to Apply The fellowship is open to applicants from low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) around the world. This means candidates may apply from regions including: Africa Asia Latin America The Caribbean Middle East Other LMIC regions globally Applicants must be based in one of these eligible countries at the time of application. Key Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme, applicants should meet several important criteria. 1. Academic or Professional Background Applicants should have a background in fields such as: Public health Health policy Digital health Technology and data science Global health Public policy Artificial intelligence in healthcare Health governance Development studies 2. Career Stage The fellowship specifically targets: Students Early-career researchers Young professionals at the beginning stages of their careers 3. Interest in Digital Health Governance Applicants must demonstrate a clear interest in the intersection of: Technology Health systems Policy development Digital governance 4. Research and Analytical Skills Candidates should have strong research and analytical capabilities, as the fellowship focuses heavily on policy analysis and academic research. Benefits of the Fellowship Being selected as a DTH-Lab Research Fellow provides numerous academic and professional advantages. 1. Global Research Experience Fellows will work on impactful projects exploring the future of digital health systems. 2. Collaboration With Global Experts Participants will collaborate with researchers, policymakers, and international health experts. 3. Career Development The program helps fellows develop key competencies such as: Research methodology Policy analysis Health governance expertise International collaboration skills 4. Exposure to Global Health Policy Fellows gain insight into how digital technologies influence healthcare governance and policy decisions worldwide. 5. Contribution to Global Digital Health Discussions Research produced through the fellowship may influence international debates on digital health transformation. Why This Fellowship Matters Digital technology is rapidly transforming healthcare systems around the world. From artificial intelligence to telemedicine and digital data systems, technology is reshaping how healthcare services are delivered and governed. However, these transformations also raise complex questions related to: Ethics Equity Data governance Health access Digital rights The DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme provides a unique platform for emerging leaders to contribute to solving these challenges. By engaging students and young professionals in meaningful research and policy discussions, the program ensures that future digital health systems are designed to benefit communities worldwide. Important Dates Applications Open: 30th March 2026 Application Deadline: 12th April 2026 The 2026 DTH-Lab Research Fellowship Programme is an exceptional opportunity for students and early-career professionals from low- and middle-income countries to become actively involved in shaping the future of global health systems. Participants will gain valuable research experience, collaborate with global experts, and contribute to discussions that influence how digital technologies transform healthcare worldwide. title: Young Scholars Invent STEM Program at Rice University 2026: A Powerful Opportunity for High School Students to Build Real-World Engineering Solutions
summary: Students passionate about science, engineering, and innovation now have an exceptional opportunity to participate in the Young Scholars Invent program organized by the Office of STEM Engagement at Rice University . This program is designed to immerse high school students in real-world engineering and technology challenges, allowing them to collaborate, design, test, and present innovative solutions. Hosted in Houston , the initiative equips participants with essential research, design, and communication skills that are critical in modern STEM careers. The program is run by the Rice University Office of STEM Engagement (often known as R-STEM), a unit dedicated to expanding access to high-quality science and technology education for students. Through immersive experiences like Young Scholars Invent, students gain exposure to the engineering design process and learn how to translate creative ideas into practical innovations. What Is the Young Scholars Invent Program? The Young Scholars Invent Program is a hands-on STEM workshop that introduces students to collaborative engineering problem-solving and design innovation. The workshop typically runs for about 1.5 days, during which students work in multidisciplinary teams to solve real-world challenges provided by industry partners and organizations. Rather than focusing on theoretical learning, the program emphasizes experiential learning through design thinking, experimentation, and teamwork. Participants go through the entire innovation process: Understanding a real-world problem Researching possible solutions Forming diverse teams Designing and prototyping solutions Testing ideas Presenting their final concept to judges This immersive experience simulates the collaborative environment used in professional engineering and technology industries. Why the Young Scholars Invent Program Is Unique Unlike many STEM competitions that focus on school-versus-school rivalry, the Young Scholars Invent initiative encourages collaboration across schools and backgrounds. Students are allowed to choose the problem they want to solve and form teams with participants from other schools based on shared interests. This model reflects how innovation happens in real-world workplaces where professionals from different disciplines collaborate to solve complex problems. Key distinctive features include: 1. Industry-Simulated Design Experience Students participate in an industry-style engineering design challenge, gaining insight into how engineers approach real problems. 2. Collaborative Multidisciplinary Teams Participants form teams composed of students from various schools and backgrounds, encouraging diverse perspectives. 3. Real-World Innovation Challenges Problems addressed in the program are drawn from industry and agency partners, ensuring relevance and practical value. 4. Emphasis on Communication and Presentation Students must present their solutions to a panel of judges, strengthening public speaking and technical communication skills. Program Objectives and Learning Outcomes The Young Scholars Invent program is designed to cultivate key skills required in modern science and engineering careers. Students who participate can significantly enhance their abilities in the following areas: 1. Engineering Research Skills Participants learn how to apply scientific and engineering principles to develop solutions to real-world problems. 2. Problem Identification and Analysis -Students learn to: Identify engineering challenges Analyze data Develop innovative solutions 3. Experimental Design and Testing Participants design and test prototypes while learning how to evaluate and refine their ideas. 4. Team Collaboration The program emphasizes teamwork across disciplines, teaching participants how to function effectively within collaborative environments. 5. Scientific Communication Students gain experience in presenting ideas clearly and persuasively to judges and peers. These experiences help students develop skills that are essential for careers in engineering, technology, and scientific research. Who Can Apply for the Young Scholars Invent Program? Eligible Applicants The program primarily targets high school students, specifically those in: Grade 9 Grade 10 Grade 11 These students are at a critical stage where early exposure to research and engineering can significantly influence their academic and career pathways. Eligible Countries and Regions The program is primarily designed for students residing in the Houston area of Texas, United States, due to the in-person nature of the workshop. However, students should always check official updates for potential expansions or special programs that may allow broader participation. Required Qualifications Applicants do not need extensive prior experience in programming or engineering to participate. Basic Requirements Eligible students should: Be currently enrolled in high school (grades 9–11) Demonstrate interest in STEM fields Be willing to collaborate in multidisciplinary teams Be prepared to participate in an intensive design workshop Have parental or guardian consent (for minors) According to the organizers, prior programming experience is not required, making the program accessible to students from diverse educational backgrounds. Participants are encouraged to bring a laptop computer, which is used for research, design work, and creating presentations. Skills and Knowledge Participants Will Gain Students who complete the program gain exposure to several essential competencies that are highly valued in higher education and STEM careers. 1. STEM Knowledge Application Participants learn how to apply knowledge from mathematics, science, and engineering to solve real-world problems. 2. Data Analysis and Experimentation Students develop skills in: Designing experiments Interpreting data Testing prototype designs 3. Systems Design Participants learn how to design systems and processes that meet real-world constraints such as: Environmental impact Economic feasibility Social considerations Sustainability 4. Leadership and Collaboration Working in teams helps students develop both leadership and teamwork capabilities. 5. Professional Communication Students present their ideas to judges and explain the reasoning behind their designs. Benefits of Participating in the Program Participating in Young Scholars Invent offers numerous educational and career advantages. 1. Early Exposure to Engineering Careers Students gain insight into what engineers and researchers actually do. 2. Networking Opportunities Participants connect with: Fellow STEM students Mentors Educators Industry professionals 3. Hands-On Innovation Experience Students learn the engineering design process through practice rather than theory. 4. Academic and Career Inspiration The program helps students discover potential career paths in fields such as: Engineering Computer science Robotics Biotechnology Environmental science 5. Strong Portfolio Development Participation can strengthen applications for: Universities Scholarships STEM competitions Research programs Structure of the Workshop The Young Scholars Invent workshop follows a carefully structured format designed to maximize learning and engagement. • Step 1: Introduction to the Challenge Participants are introduced to real-world problems provided by industry partners. • Step 2: Team Formation- Students select problems that interest them and form teams with peers from different schools. • Step 3: Research and Concept Development Teams research the problem and brainstorm innovative solutions. • Step 4: Prototype Design Participants design and test prototypes or conceptual models. • Step 5: Final Presentation Teams present their ideas to judges and explain how their solution addresses the challenge. Why STEM Programs Like Young Scholars Invent Matter Programs like Young Scholars Invent are increasingly important in a world driven by technological innovation. Studies show that early exposure to STEM education increases students likelihood of pursuing careers in science and technology fields. These careers are also among the fastest-growing globally. STEM programs help students develop critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving abilities—skills essential for addressing global challenges such as climate change, healthcare innovation, and sustainable development. How to Apply Students interested in participating should: Visit the official application page provided in the reference link. Complete the application form. Submit required documentation, including parental consent forms if required. Applicants should also regularly check the official page for announcements regarding the next application cycle and workshop dates. Apply here Deadline 3rd April 2026 The Young Scholars Invent program at Rice University represents an outstanding opportunity for ambitious high school students to explore engineering innovation and scientific discovery in a collaborative environment. By combining hands-on design challenges with mentorship and teamwork, the program empowers students to become creative problem-solvers capable of tackling real-world issues. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Young Scholars Invent STEM Program at Rice University 2026: A Powerful Opportunity for High School Students to Build Real-World Engineering Solutions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Women and Nonbinary Journalists Covering Human Rights and Social Justice
summary: The Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027 is one of the most prestigious global opportunities available to journalists dedicated to reporting on human rights and social justice issues. Organized by the International Women’s Media Foundation (IWMF), this competitive program provides professional and academic training in the United States for outstanding journalists from around the world. The fellowship was established to honor the legacy of Elizabeth Neuffer , a courageous correspondent for The Boston Globe who was killed while reporting in Iraq in 2003. Through this initiative, the IWMF aims to empower journalists who are committed to exposing injustice and promoting human rights through powerful storytelling. For journalists seeking to strengthen their investigative reporting skills, gain international exposure, and work alongside leading media institutions, this fellowship offers a life-changing opportunity. About the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship The Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship was created by the International Women’s Media Foundation to support journalists whose work focuses on human rights, democracy, and social justice. The program aims to: Strengthen the investigative and reporting skills of journalists Provide academic exposure to global political and social issues Encourage impactful storytelling that exposes injustice Support journalists working in challenging environments The fellowship reflects Elizabeth Neuffer’s lifelong dedication to uncovering truth and giving voice to marginalized communities affected by conflict and human rights violations. Benefits of the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027 The fellowship is widely recognized as a fully funded professional development opportunity for journalists. The selected fellow receives multiple forms of support to enable full participation in the program. Financial Support The fellowship provides: A monthly stipend to cover living expenses Round-trip airfare to and from the United States Housing arrangements in fellowship cities Health insurance coverage Transportation between fellowship locations Visa assistance for international fellows These benefits allow the fellow to focus entirely on academic research, reporting, and professional growth during the program. Academic and Professional Training Components The fellowship combines academic study with practical newsroom experience. Academic Research and Study Fellows spend part of the program at MIT’s Center for International Studies, where they can: Audit courses in international relations, politics, and journalism Conduct research on global human rights issues Engage with leading scholars and policy experts Develop analytical and investigative skills This academic component strengthens journalists’ ability to contextualize and report complex global issues. Newsroom Internship Another major aspect of the fellowship is professional newsroom training. Fellows work closely with journalists at major media institutions, gaining real-world reporting experience and editorial mentorship. This hands-on training provides opportunities to: Work on investigative reporting projects Publish stories under their bylines Learn advanced reporting techniques Build international journalism networks Such exposure significantly enhances the fellow’s career prospects in global journalism. Who Can Apply for the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027 The fellowship is designed specifically for journalists who are actively reporting on issues related to human rights and social justice. Eligible Category of Applicants The following individuals are eligible: Women journalists Nonbinary journalists Gender-nonconforming journalists Applicants must be professional journalists working in: Print media Broadcast media Digital media Freelance journalism The fellowship prioritizes journalists whose work focuses on issues such as: Human rights violations Social justice Conflict and war reporting Democracy and governance Marginalized communities Countries Eligible to Apply One of the major strengths of this program is its global inclusivity. Eligible Regions: Applicants from all countries worldwide can apply for the fellowship. There are no regional restrictions. Journalists from Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and Oceania are encouraged to submit applications. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027, applicants must meet several requirements. Professional Experience Applicants must have: At least three years of full-time professional journalism experience Important note: Internships completed during university do not count toward the required professional experience. Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate: Fluent English reading, writing, and speaking skills This requirement is necessary because the fellowship activities, courses, and newsroom work are conducted in English. Employment Status Eligible applicants include: Staff journalists working in media organizations Freelance journalists producing independent journalism Both categories are welcome to apply. Required Application Materials A complete fellowship application typically includes several documents demonstrating the applicant’s experience and potential. Applicants may need to submit: A professional biography A current CV or resume Samples of published journalism work A personal statement explaining their career goals Letters of recommendation Applicants must submit their materials through the official online application portal. Apply here Why the Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship Matters The Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship is more than a professional training program. It plays a crucial role in strengthening independent journalism worldwide. Through this fellowship: Journalists gain skills to investigate complex human rights issues Stories exposing injustice reach global audiences Emerging journalists develop leadership within the media industry Press freedom and ethical journalism are strengthened The fellowship also creates a global network of journalists committed to accountability, transparency, and truth. Career Impact of the Fellowship Many past fellows have gone on to become leading journalists, investigative reporters, and international correspondents. The program helps fellows: Publish influential investigative stories Build relationships with global media institutions Develop expertise in human rights reporting Expand their professional networks across countries Participation in this fellowship often significantly elevates a journalist’s career. Host Country and Program Location Host Country: United States Primary Locations: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), Cambridge, Massachusetts The Boston Globe newsroom in Boston Additional placement during the fellowship period in the United States The fellowship begins with academic research and coursework at MIT’s Center for International Studies and includes professional newsroom experience with major media organizations. Fellowship Duration and Timeline The fellowship lasts approximately six months, offering a combination of academic and professional development. Fellowship Schedule June 2026: Fellow selected February – May 2027: Academic research and coursework at MIT and internship in Boston June – July 2027: Additional professional placement in the United States July 2027: Fellowship concludes During this time, the selected fellow engages in research, journalism training, and hands-on reporting experience. Deadline 19th April 2026 The Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027 represents a rare opportunity for journalists passionate about human rights reporting to gain world-class training and international recognition. With its combination of academic research at MIT, newsroom experience with leading media organizations, and full financial support, the fellowship equips journalists with the tools necessary to produce impactful journalism that holds power accountable. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Elizabeth Neuffer Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Women and Nonbinary Journalists Covering Human Rights and Social Justice appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship 2026: A Global Lifeline for Threatened Scholars and Academics Worldwide
summary: The Institute of International Education Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship (IIE-SRF) is one of the most important international opportunities designed to support scholars whose academic work and personal safety are under threat in their home countries. Managed by the Institute of International Education, this prestigious program provides temporary academic placements and financial support to ensure that scholars facing persecution, conflict, or repression can continue their teaching and research in a safe academic environment. Since its establishment, the program has become a vital global initiative dedicated to protecting intellectual freedom and preserving academic knowledge by assisting scholars who would otherwise be unable to continue their work. More than 1,000 scholars from over 60 countries have already benefited from the fellowship, with placements in hundreds of universities across the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship The Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship is the only global program dedicated exclusively to arranging and funding fellowships for threatened and displaced scholars. The fellowship offers scholars an opportunity to temporarily relocate to host universities around the world where they can continue their academic work safely. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Many academics around the world face severe threats due to their research, political climate, armed conflicts, or persecution for their ideas. These threats often make it impossible for them to continue teaching, conducting research, or publishing in their home countries. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The fellowship program addresses this challenge by providing: Safe academic placement at international institutions Financial support to sustain research and teaching Opportunities to maintain academic careers Support for intellectual freedom and academic independence In many cases, fellows continue contributing to global scholarship while maintaining ties to their home regions and academic communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Financial Benefits and Funding Coverage The Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship offers significant financial and professional support to successful applicants. Fellowship Funding Successful fellows receive approximately USD $30,000 for one year , which supports a temporary academic appointment at a host institution. Possible Extension Many fellows may apply for a second and final year of fellowship support , depending on their circumstances and progress. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Additional Program Support Apart from the stipend, the fellowship includes: Placement at an accredited higher education institution Academic mentorship and institutional support Networking with international scholars Assistance from program administrators before and during the fellowship This financial and institutional support enables scholars to maintain their academic careers despite displacement. Who Can Apply for the Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship One of the unique aspects of the program is its global accessibility. Scholars from any country and academic discipline can apply, provided they meet the required conditions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Eligible Applicants The fellowship accepts applications from: Professors Researchers Public intellectuals University lecturers Academic scholars working in recognized fields Applicants must be individuals who cannot continue their academic work in their home countries due to threats or persecution. Countries and Regions Eligible to Apply The fellowship is open to scholars from all countries worldwide. There are no restrictions based on nationality, continent, or academic field. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Eligible applicants may come from: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Middle East Oceania However, applicants must demonstrate that they are facing serious threats that prevent them from safely continuing their work in their home country. Academic Fields Covered The program supports scholars from all academic disciplines, including but not limited to: Social sciences Humanities Engineering Medicine Natural sciences Political science Environmental science Law Education Technology and innovation This broad eligibility ensures that scholars from diverse fields can continue contributing to global knowledge and research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship, applicants must meet several criteria relating to academic qualifications, professional experience, and personal circumstances. Academic Qualifications Applicants are generally expected to: Hold a PhD or the highest academic degree in their field Have significant teaching or research experience at a university or higher education institution Demonstrate a strong record of academic achievement Have published research or scholarly work These qualifications demonstrate that applicants are active contributors to academic knowledge. Threat or Risk Conditions The fellowship is designed specifically for scholars facing serious risks. Applicants must demonstrate that they: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Face immediate threats to their life, safety, or academic career Have recently fled their country due to persecution Are unable to continue academic work safely in their home country Selection committees carefully evaluate the urgency and severity of these threats. Additional Selection Considerations Preference is given to applicants who: Have an established record of published research Show strong academic promise or achievement Can contribute to host institutions academically Will benefit both their host and home academic communities ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); These criteria help ensure that fellows can continue meaningful scholarly work during the fellowship. Who Is Not Eligible The program does not typically consider applications from individuals who: Are applying primarily to continue academic studies such as a PhD Have lived safely outside their home country for more than two years Already hold permanent residency in a safe third country Have already completed two years of similar fellowships for threatened scholars These restrictions help the program focus resources on scholars facing urgent risks. Application Requirements Applicants must submit several documents to demonstrate eligibility and academic qualifications. Required Documents Typical application materials include: Completed application form Updated academic CV or resume Academic statement describing research work Personal statement explaining circumstances and threats Letters of recommendation Copy of PhD or highest academic degree Samples of academic publications Optional host institution letter (if available) The CV should clearly include: Academic degrees and institutions Current and previous academic positions Teaching responsibilities Complete publication list These materials help reviewers assess the applicant s academic background and the urgency of their situation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Application Process The application process is designed to accommodate scholars who may face difficult circumstances. Step-by-Step Application Process Prepare all required documents and supporting materials. Complete the fellowship application form. Compile your academic CV and research publications. Write a personal statement describing the threats you face. Submit the application materials by email. Applicants who cannot submit their applications personally may ask a third party to submit the application on their behalf. Apply here Selection Process The selection committee evaluates applicants based on several factors. Evaluation Criteria Applicants are assessed according to: Academic qualifications and professional experience Quality and impact of their research work Urgency and severity of threats faced Potential to contribute to host institutions Due to high demand, not all eligible applicants receive fellowships. Impact of the Scholar Rescue Fund The Scholar Rescue Fund has had a significant impact on global academic communities. Key achievements include: Supporting more than 1,185 scholars Representing scholars from over 62 countries Collaborating with over 500 host universities worldwide These numbers highlight the importance of the program in protecting scholars and preserving academic knowledge globally. Why the Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship Matters Academic freedom is essential for global progress, innovation, and knowledge creation. When scholars are silenced or forced into exile, the loss extends beyond individuals to entire societies. The Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship ensures that: Intellectual freedom is preserved Scholars can continue producing knowledge Universities maintain diverse global perspectives Academic communities remain resilient in times of crisis By protecting scholars, the fellowship protects the future of education and research worldwide. Application Deadline 26th April 2026 The Scholar Rescue Fund Fellowship offered by the Institute of International Education stands as one of the most critical global initiatives supporting academics in danger. With substantial financial support, international placements, and institutional backing, the program provides scholars with a rare opportunity to continue their research and teaching despite difficult circumstances. title: Simply Sport Foundation Multi-Year Grant Partnership Program 2026: A Transformational 3-Year Funding Opportunity for Grassroots Sports Organisations Supporting Girls in Sport
summary: Grassroots sports organisations play a critical role in shaping opportunities for young athletes, particularly girls who often face barriers to participation, access, and long-term development. Recognising this challenge, the Simply Sport Foundation (SSF) has launched the Multi-Year Grant Partnership Program 2026 , a comprehensive three-year initiative designed to strengthen grassroots sports organisations and create safe, sustainable pathways for girls to participate and thrive in sports. Unlike traditional funding schemes that provide short-term financial support, the SSF Multi-Year Grant Partnership goes far beyond monetary assistance. The program combines structured financial grants, technical support, organisational development, and long-term collaboration , enabling sports organisations to grow sustainably while improving opportunities for girl athletes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Applications for the 2026 cycle opened on March 11, 2026 , and will remain open until April 10, 2026 , giving eligible grassroots sports organisations the opportunity to join a transformative three-year journey focused on impact, growth, and empowerment through sport. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Understanding the SSF Multi-Year Grant Partnership Program The SSF Multi-Year Grant Partnership Program is a structured development model created to support grassroots sports organisations working to promote sports participation among girls. Its team works alongside participating organisations, providing not only financial resources but also expert guidance, technical assistance, and institutional knowledge. This approach ensures that partner organisations are equipped with the tools and systems required to deliver sustainable sports programs that can continue benefiting communities long after the funding period ends. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The program s core objective is simple yet powerful: To create safe, inclusive, and sustainable sporting opportunities for girls while strengthening grassroots sports organisations that nurture future athletes. What Makes the SSF Grant Partnership Different from Traditional Funding? Many grant programs offer financial support for a limited time and expect organisations to independently manage program delivery afterward. The SSF model challenges this traditional approach by establishing a long-term partnership built on collaboration, accountability, and shared goals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Key distinguishing features include: Long-Term Collaboration SSF remains actively involved throughout the full three-year period, ensuring that partner organisations receive continuous support and mentorship. Integrated Development Support Beyond funding, the program provides technical expertise, institutional guidance, and strategic development support that strengthens both the organisation and its sports programs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Focus on Girls in Sport The initiative specifically prioritises increasing opportunities for girls , addressing barriers to participation, retention, and development in sports. Structured Growth Model The program s phased framework allows organisations to build strong foundations before scaling their activities and impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); This unique model transforms the grant into a holistic development partnership rather than a one-time funding opportunity. The funding is allocated as follows: Year 1: 4 Lakhs Year 2: 5 Lakhs Year 3: 6 Lakhs This progressive funding structure reflects the program s philosophy of supporting sustainable growth , allowing organisations to build capacity before expanding their reach and impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); However, SSF emphasises that the grant amount represents only one part of the partnership s value. Comprehensive Support Beyond Financial Grants While the financial grant provides the foundation for program activities, the SSF Multi-Year Grant Partnership extends far beyond funding alone. Partner organisations gain access to a wide range of development resources designed to strengthen both their programs and internal structures. This holistic support includes: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Institutional Capacity Building Organisations receive guidance on improving governance structures, operational systems, and program management practices. Technical Expertise SSF provides expert insights and mentorship to help organisations refine training programs, improve athlete development pathways, and implement effective sports management practices. Child Safeguarding Implementation One of the program s key priorities is ensuring safe sporting environments. SSF supports partners in adopting and implementing child safeguarding policies that protect young athletes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Monitoring and Evaluation Support Organisations are encouraged to integrate monitoring and evaluation frameworks to track progress, measure outcomes, and strengthen program effectiveness. Health and Well-Being Integration SSF promotes the integration of health awareness initiatives within sports programs, particularly those addressing issues affecting girl athletes. Through these combined resources, the program ensures that organisations develop strong institutional foundations capable of sustaining long-term impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); SSF s Track Record and Demonstrated Impact The Simply Sport Foundation has already demonstrated the potential of its partnership model through previous yearly grant initiatives. Over the years, SSF has collaborated with numerous grassroots sports organisations across India, helping them improve program quality and expand opportunities for young athletes. Key impact highlights from past partnerships include: Implementation of child safeguarding policies across partner organisations to strengthen athlete protection. Organisation of child safeguarding workshops and awareness sessions for coaches, staff, and stakeholders. Strengthened institutional practices within grassroots sports organisations. Increased participation among girl athletes through targeted health and awareness programs. These achievements demonstrate the effectiveness of SSF s approach in creating safer, more inclusive sports environments while empowering grassroots organisations to grow sustainably. Voices from Past Partner Organisations Feedback from previous partner organisations illustrates the real-world impact of SSF s support. One partner organisation highlighted how the grant enabled access to better sports infrastructure and athlete development resources: Grants of this nature, designed to support initiatives across the spectrum from grassroots to elite youth, are virtually non-existent in India. This collaboration has enabled our residential hockey program to access quality equipment and competitive exposure opportunities. It has also opened doors to essential sports science resources that will meaningfully enhance the performance of these young athletes at elite levels. Another organisation reported a significant increase in girls participation after health education initiatives supported by SSF: The workshop on female athletes menstrual health has motivated many girls to take up sports. The awareness about menstrual cycles and menstrual health resulted in a 30% increase in girls participation in sports . These testimonials highlight the transformative potential of a partnership model that combines funding, education, and institutional support. The Three-Year Partnership Framework The SSF program is structured as a phased development model , ensuring that organisations grow gradually and sustainably over the three-year period. Each year builds upon the progress of the previous one: Year 1: Foundation Building Organisations focus on establishing strong program structures, improving training systems, and adopting safeguarding policies. Year 2: Program Strengthening Partners refine their sports programs, improve athlete development pathways, and expand participation. Year 3: Sustainability and Scaling By the final year, organisations are expected to operate with stronger institutional systems and the capacity to sustain programs independently. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); This gradual progression ensures that the partnership leads to long-term organisational resilience rather than short-term program expansion. Expected Outcomes of the Program The SSF Multi-Year Grant Partnership Program aims to achieve several measurable outcomes that directly impact the lives of girl athletes. Key targets include: 20 30% reduction in dropout rates among girl athletes enrolled in partner programs. Institutionalisation of child safeguarding practices as a routine part of sports operations. Engagement of senior girl athletes as mentors, coaches, and role models for younger participants. Development of replicable program models that can inspire similar initiatives across the country. These outcomes reflect SSF s broader mission of ensuring that sport becomes a lasting and empowering space for girls. Eligibility Criteria for Applicant Organisations To ensure that selected organisations can fully benefit from the program, SSF has established a set of eligibility criteria that applicants must meet to progress to the second round of the selection process. Eligible organisations must: Be legally registered as a Trust, Society, or Section 8 Company. Have been operational for at least three years with ongoing sports activities. Employ at least one designated coach responsible for girls training programs . Be located in rural or semi-urban areas . Demonstrate active involvement of founders or senior leadership in daily operations. Have existing girl athlete enrolment or a clear plan for expansion . Show willingness to implement child safeguarding practices and maintain program records . Commit to monitoring, evaluation, health integration, and youth development principles . It elevates the visibility of organizations that are making meaningful contributions to humanity. Receiving the Prize can: Amplify global awareness of the organization s mission Strengthen credibility and partnerships Increase funding opportunities Expand humanitarian impact worldwide ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Through this prestigious recognition, the Conrad N. Hilton Foundation continues to highlight organizations that are leading the fight against human suffering and driving sustainable change around the world. Current Nomination Cycle Nominations are accepted year-round, but the nomination date determines which Prize cycle the organization will be considered for. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Current nomination timeline: 2027 Hilton Humanitarian Prize Submission period: May 1, 2025 April 30, 2026 Deadline: April 30, 2026 Organizations nominated within this timeframe will be considered for the 2027 Hilton Humanitarian Prize . Who Can Be Nominated The Hilton Humanitarian Prize is awarded to organizations rather than individuals. Eligible nominees must meet the following criteria: Must be a nonprofit organization. Must be an established nongovernmental and publicly supported charitable organization. Must possess tax-exempt status such as: U.S. 501(c)(3), or International equivalent documentation. Must have been legally established for at least five years. Must have audited financial statements for at least five consecutive years. Must have operational expenditures greater than USD 750,000 during the most recent audited fiscal year. Must not be: A previous Hilton Humanitarian Prize laureate A local office or branch of a previous laureate A program or project within a larger organization A university or academic institution that grants degrees However, organizations affiliated with academic institutions may qualify if they: Maintain independent nonprofit status Have their own audited financial statements Who Can Submit a Nomination The nomination must be submitted by an individual who has direct knowledge of the organization s work. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); The nominator must meet the following conditions: Must not be an employee or officer of the nominated organization Must not receive compensation from the nominated organization Must not be the founder of the organization Must not be a family member of the organization s employees or officers Must not be a member of the Hilton Foundation staff, jury, or board Must not be a Hilton family member Board members and volunteers may nominate an organization only if they receive no compensation for their services. Additionally, each nominator may submit only one nomination per Prize cycle . Nomination Letter Requirements The nomination letter is one of the most important components of the submission. Key requirements include: Must be written in English Must be submitted through the online nomination portal Must include a handwritten or electronic signature Must not exceed four typed pages Must use 12-point font ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The letter should be addressed to: Hilton Humanitarian Prize Jury The nomination letter should focus on the organization s achievements rather than future plans . Key areas that should be addressed include: The organization s extraordinary contributions to alleviating human suffering The significance of the humanitarian issues the organization addresses The organization s proven track record of impactful work The strength and innovation of its programs The sustainability and scalability of its approach Partnerships and collaborations that enhance impact Evaluation and learning mechanisms within programs The potential impact the Prize would have on the organization s mission Evaluation Criteria All nominations undergo a thorough review process based on several evaluation considerations. These include: Extraordinary contributions toward alleviating human suffering. Importance and urgency of the humanitarian issues addressed. Demonstrated record of achievements and measurable impact. Strength of the organization s programmatic strategy. Organizational capacity and operational effectiveness. Evidence of partnerships that enhance program reach and impact. Commitment to program evaluation and continuous improvement. Potential impact of the Prize on the organization s work and mission. Nomination and Selection Process The Hilton Humanitarian Prize selection process is rigorous and multi-staged. Step 1: Nomination Submission The nominator submits the nomination through the official nominations portal and uploads the signed nomination letter. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Step 2: Nominee Notification The Conrad N. Hilton Foundation contacts the nominated organization and invites them to submit additional information. Step 3: Phase 1 Application The nominated organization completes the Phase 1 application within approximately five business days. Phase 1 includes: Organizational profile Mission and program descriptions Communities served Geographic scope of operations Financial information Audited financial statements Annual reports or newsletters ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Step 4: Internal Review The Foundation conducts an internal review of all Phase 1 submissions. Step 5: Phase 2 Application Selected organizations are invited to complete a more detailed Phase 2 application. They are given approximately ten business days to submit additional materials. Step 6: Finalist Selection Following the Phase 2 review, a shortlist of finalist organizations is selected. Step 7: Jury Review Finalists are reviewed by an independent international jury. Step 8: Prize Recipient Selection The jury selects the final recipient of the Hilton Humanitarian Prize. Timeline of the Selection Process The general timeline is as follows: Nomination submission before the deadline Nominee notification via email Phase 1 application submission Phase 1 review Phase 2 application invitation Phase 2 review Shortlist of finalists Jury review Final Prize recipient selected All nominators are notified of the final decision via email. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) What do I need to do to submit a nomination? To submit a nomination, visit the Hilton Humanitarian Prize nominations portal and upload the required nomination form and signed nomination letter before the deadline. Email submissions are not accepted. Can I nominate an individual for the Prize? No. The Prize is awarded only to nonprofit organizations. Individual nominations are not accepted. Can I nominate myself or my own organization? No. Self-nominations are not allowed. The nomination must be submitted by an external nominator. Can I nominate an organization where I serve as a board member? Yes. Board members and volunteers may nominate an organization if they receive no compensation for their service. How many organizations can I nominate in one cycle? A nominator may submit only one nomination per Prize cycle. However, it is possible to nominate two different organizations within the same year if they fall under different Prize cycles. What happens if multiple people nominate the same organization? Only the first nomination received will serve as the official nomination. Additional nominations will be registered as letters of support. A maximum of four letters of support will be recorded. How will organizations know they have been nominated? The Hilton Foundation will contact the nominated organization directly via email using the contact information provided by the nominator. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Do organizations need to work internationally to qualify? No. Organizations may operate in a single country or across multiple countries. Are current or past Hilton Foundation grantees eligible? Yes. Both current and past grantees are eligible to be nominated. Read more and apply through the Official Website. Discover other global awards on OFY HERE. The post Conrad N. title: ETH Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027 at ETH Zurich: Fully Funded Opportunity for Outstanding Early-Career Researchers
summary: The ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme (ETH Fellows) at ETH Zurich is one of Europe s most prestigious early-career research opportunities designed to support outstanding postdoctoral researchers from around the world. The fellowship enables talented scholars to conduct independent research while working with leading experts at ETH Zurich , one of the world s top-ranked universities. This highly competitive programme is specifically designed to foster academic independence and support researchers who have demonstrated exceptional scientific achievements early in their careers. Many past ETH Fellows have gone on to secure professorships and influential academic positions globally, with approximately 40 50% of fellows becoming professors within a few years of completing the fellowship . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The ETH Fellows programme provides full salary funding, research support, and access to world-class research infrastructure in Switzerland. Through mentorship from ETH Zurich faculty members, fellows gain the resources and collaborative environment necessary to develop cutting-edge research projects and accelerate their academic careers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About ETH Zurich ETH Zurich (Swiss Federal Institute of Technology Zurich) is internationally recognized for excellence in science, engineering, mathematics, and technology. The institution consistently ranks among the top universities globally and has produced numerous Nobel laureates, pioneering researchers, and influential innovators. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship programme contributes to ETH Zurich s mission of advancing scientific discovery by attracting exceptional researchers from diverse backgrounds worldwide. Key Objectives of the ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme The programme aims to: Support outstanding early-career researchers with strong scientific track records. Encourage innovative and independent research projects. Foster collaboration between international researchers and ETH Zurich faculty. Promote academic leadership and prepare fellows for future professorships. Strengthen global research networks across disciplines. Fellowship Duration The ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship has a fixed duration of two years . Important notes: The fellowship cannot be extended beyond two years. Fellows must start their fellowship within eight months after the submission deadline . The fellowship start date must be the first day of a month . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); A later start is only permitted in exceptional cases and requires approval from the ETH Zurich Grants Office. Funding and Financial Support ETH Fellows receive a comprehensive funding package that includes salary and research support. Salary The fellowship covers 100% of the fellow s salary based on ETH Zurich standards . Salary structure for 2026: Year 1 Gross salary: CHF 92,500 Total employment cost: CHF 106,400 Year 2 Gross salary: CHF 97,200 Total employment cost: CHF 111,800 Total funding over two years: Gross salary: CHF 189,700 Total employment cost: CHF 218,200 The total employment cost includes 15% social security contributions paid by ETH Zurich . Research Funding In addition to the salary, fellows receive: CHF 12,000 per year for research expenses This funding can be used for: Research materials Travel and mobility expenses Conference participation Scientific collaboration activities Note: Research funds cannot be used for salary costs , and salary funds cannot be redirected toward research expenses. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet several strict eligibility criteria at the time of the submission deadline. Doctoral Degree Requirement Applicants must: Hold a PhD degree , OR Complete their PhD within six months after the submission deadline . For applicants who have not yet completed their PhD: A certification confirming the planned defense date must be submitted. The document must be signed by the doctoral advisor or department chair . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); PhD Time Limit Applicants who already hold a PhD must have received the degree no more than two years before the submission deadline . The decisive date is the official examination or defense date (viva voce) . Publication Requirement Applicants must have: At least one scientific publication in a peer-reviewed journal , OR Received a prize for their doctoral thesis . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Mobility Requirements The fellowship follows strict international mobility rules. Applicants are not eligible if they : Have resided or worked in Switzerland for more than 12 months in the three years before the deadline . Have worked or studied for more than six months in the past three years at any of the following institutions: ETH Zurich EPF Lausanne ETH Domain Research Institutes (PSI, WSL, Empa, Eawag) University of Zurich ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Additionally: Applicants who received their PhD from ETH Zurich or the University of Zurich are not eligible . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Mentor Requirement Each applicant must have a host professor at ETH Zurich who agrees to act as a mentor. Important conditions: A professor may support only one candidate per evaluation round . The mentor must provide an official invitation letter . The host professor must upload required documents in the application system. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Applicants are strongly advised to contact potential mentors well before the application deadline . Project Proposal Expectations Applicants are expected to propose their own original research project . The programme prioritizes: Independent research ideas Innovative and impactful proposals Projects demonstrating clear synergy with the host research group Applicants and mentors should not have closely collaborated before the fellowship . Reference Letters Applicants must submit two external reference letters . Requirements include: Letters must be sent directly to the ETH Zurich Grants Office . Submission must occur at least 10 days before the deadline . Letters must include: Institutional letterhead Signature of the referee. Application Deadlines The ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship programme has two submission deadlines each year . 2026 deadlines: 2 March 2026, 17:00 Swiss local time 1 September 2026, 17:00 Swiss local time ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); If a deadline falls on a weekend, it will be moved to the following Monday at the same time. Interview Process The selection process includes two stages: Written evaluation of the submitted proposal Interview for shortlisted candidates The interviews are conducted online via video call . For the 2026 calls: March 2026 deadline interviews: 19 May 2026 20 May 2026 21 May 2026 September 2026 deadline interviews: 10 November 2026 11 November 2026 12 November 2026 Applicants are informed within two months after the deadline whether they have been invited for an interview. Final decisions are announced approximately four months after the submission deadline . How to Apply Applications must be submitted electronically through the ETH Zurich eResearch grant application system . Steps to apply: Download the Official project description templates. Prepare the required documents and convert them into PDF format. Register an account on the eResearch platform. Start a new application under My Applications . Select the ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship call. Complete all mandatory fields in the application form. Upload required documents. Add the host professor under the Participants section. Add two reference providers under the References section. Validate the application to ensure all required sections are complete. Submit the application before the deadline. Once submitted, changes are no longer allowed . Applicants are advised to download a PDF copy of the application for personal records . Evaluation and Selection Applications are evaluated by the ETH Zurich Research Commission . Evaluation criteria include: Scientific excellence Research originality Feasibility of the research proposal Candidate s academic track record Alignment with the host research group Due to the programme s prestige and limited fellowships, the competition is intense. Recent success rates have been approximately 25% . Why Apply for the ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship This fellowship provides a unique opportunity for early-career researchers to: Conduct independent research at a world-leading university Collaborate with internationally recognized scholars Gain access to cutting-edge research facilities Build strong academic networks Enhance career prospects in academia Secure funding for innovative research projects Many fellows successfully transition into tenure-track and professorship positions globally , making the programme a powerful launchpad for academic careers. Click HERE to learn more about the ETH Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme. Discover more global fellowship opportunities HERE. title: Apply for EU Funding: Up to €2.25 Million for Digital Innovation and AI Health Research in Africa

This funding call aims to address those challenges by supporting collaborative research projects that combine digital innovation, international expertise, and capacity building . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); By investing in digital health research partnerships between Africa and Europe, the initiative seeks to accelerate the development of solutions that improve health outcomes while strengthening scientific collaboration across regions. Priority Areas for Proposals The funding call invites projects that focus on one or more of the following areas: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); 1. Digital Innovation and Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare Projects may explore how AI-driven technologies can support diagnostics, treatment decision-making, health data analysis, or healthcare management systems. 2. Digital Tools for Clinical Research and Health Services Proposals can focus on integrating digital tools and data platforms into clinical research systems or health service delivery. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); 3. Research Capacity Strengthening Another priority is building stronger research infrastructure and expertise in digital health and data science across Sub-Saharan Africa. 4. Cross-Border Collaboration and Knowledge Exchange Projects are encouraged to promote partnerships between institutions across different countries to strengthen research networks. 5. Scaling Digital Health Solutions The program also supports initiatives that can expand or scale proven digital health innovations across multiple regions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); By focusing on these areas, the initiative seeks to ensure that digital health innovations can move beyond pilot programs and deliver real benefits to healthcare systems and communities. Who Can Apply Applications must be submitted by international consortia made up of multiple partner organizations . Geographic Focus Projects funded under this call must focus on Sub-Saharan Africa , with partnerships involving organizations from: African countries European Union Member States Countries associated with the Horizon Europe program By supporting partnerships between institutions across these regions, the program aims to create long-term collaborations that strengthen global health research and digital innovation capacity. title: Dell NextGen Presales Academy 2026 in Amsterdam: Launch Your Tech Career with Dell Technologies
